Select the location and click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a81e191c-6843-4ba6-91a6-68a1994fc182"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Be sure the link points to your MagicDraw (or other according to a modeling tool you are using) installation directory (see the following figure).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_link_location.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Make sure that precisely Java 17 version is used by the <strong>MagicDraw</strong> project in your Eclipse IDE:<br />3.1. <span>Right-click the <strong>MagicDraw</strong> project and select </span><strong>Properties</strong><span>.</span><br />3.2. <span>On the left side of the open dialog, select <strong>Java Build Path</strong> and then select <strong>Libraries</strong> tab on the right.</span><br /><span>3.3. <span>Double-click <strong>JRE System Library [JavaSE-17]</strong>.</span></span><br /><span>3.4. Click <strong>Installed JREs</strong> and check if Java 17 is available here. <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1ab49c4-0459-4a9c-a6c3-d0eedcc0d85d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If there is no Java 17, add it. <em>Java 17 version must be added, because later versions may not be supported by our tool.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><span>3.5. Click <strong>Apply and Close</strong>.</span><br /><span>3.6. Select <strong>Execution environment</strong> check box and in the combo box, select <strong>JavaSE-17</strong>.<br /></span><span>3.7. C</span><span><span><span>lick <strong>Environments</strong> button.</span><br /><span><span><span>3.8. In the <strong>Execution Environments</strong> list, s</span>elect <strong>JavaSE-17</strong> and in the <strong>Compatible JREs</strong> list, select Java 17.</span></span><br /><span><span><span>3.9. </span>Click <strong>Apply and Close</strong> and then <strong>Finish</strong> buttons.</span></span><br /><span><span>3.10. </span>Select <strong>Order and Export</strong> tab, click <strong>Select All</strong> button to select all check boxes.<br />3.11. Click</span><span><span><span> <strong>Apply and Close</strong>.</span><br /></span></span></span></span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Eclipse Workspace is ready for the source code development and running/debugging.</p><p><br /></p><p>To use one of the prepared launch configurations</p><hr /><ul><li>On the main menu, click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Run</strong> &gt; <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Run Configurations...</strong> (or <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Debug Configurations...</strong>). The <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Run Configurations</strong> dialog opens.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="run_configurations.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e85c7fe6-7a02-4c0e-be8f-abb4e2c1051a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The launch configuration is designed to load plugins from the MagicDraw <span>(</span><span>or other according to a modeling tool you are using</span> <span>)</span> installation directory (<ac:link ac:anchor="step2"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[see step #2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) and two plugins from the Eclipse Workspace. Thus, if the <em>md.plugins.dir</em> java system property is not defined (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Plugins directories" /></ac:link>), developing plugins are not loaded. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><em>MagicDraw with all plugins</em> launches MagicDraw (or other modeling tool) with plugins available in the IDE workspace namely <em>My Plugin 1</em> and <em>My Plugin 2</em>.</li><li><em>My Commandline</em> launches batch mode tool <em>My Commandline</em>.</li><li><em>MyPlugin1JUnit&lt;n&gt;Test</em> launches sample <em>JUnit&lt;n&gt;</em> test.</li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e64b65a-660c-4727-831d-c768b2c8581b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The libraries (jar files) of the plugin must be added to the development class path throughout the plugin dependency hierarchy if the developing code depends on that plugin.</span><br /><span>For example, if the code depends on plugin A; plugin A depends on plugins B and C; plugin B depends on plugin D, the libraries of all plugins (A, B, C, and D) must be added to the class path.<br />For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Identifying plugin dependencies" /></ac:link> page.<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b219f48f-c5b4-47e9-9921-be356cbf7674"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you launch your own plugin, you need to add all jar files</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> that are required by your plugin</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> from appropriate plugins. The MagicDraw jar files can be found in <em>MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/lib</em> and its sub directories, whereas plugins' jar files can be found in <em>MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/plugins</em> and its sub directories.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b011d8a-dfdc-4cdc-9090-e5b5e3c5fe24"><ac:rich-text-body><p> Even if the plugin descriptor file contains information about the runtime plugin <em>.jar</em> file, it is not necessary to build and deploy this <em>.jar</em> file to a plugin directory while the plugin is developed under Eclipse.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715089 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00440: Development in IntelliJ IDEA

- page_id: `136715089`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715089/Development+in+IntelliJ+IDEA
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:04.798+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Plugins > Developing plugins using IDE
- labels: ['setup-intellij-idea-environment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The preconfigured IntelliJ IDEA project with modules for two sample plugins and a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Running programs in batch mode' space='MD2024x'] (command-line) tool is provided with a program installation directory. The project can be found in *<modeling tool installation directory>/openapi/ide/intellij.zip.*

Let's use MagicDraw as an example to describe the following procedures.

To setup the IntelliJ IDEA environment for the MagicDraw (or other program according to a modeling tool you are using) development

1. In IntelliJ IDEA, add a path variable named MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY and pointing to your version of MagicDraw (CSM, CEA that you have the licence for, as it starts like a normal version and will require an activated licence) installation directory.

2. Extract *<MagicDraw installation directory>/openapi/ide/intellij.zip*to a chosen directory.

3. Open the extracted IntelliJ IDEA project *MagicDraw development.ipr*from that directory.

The launch configuration is designed to load plugins from the program installation directory (see step #1) and two plugins from the IDEA project. Thus, if the *md.plugins.dir* java system property is not defined (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Plugins directories' space='MD2024x']), developing plugins are not loaded.

After projects are imported and the launch configurations are prepared, the IDEA project are ready for the source code development and running/debugging.

The libraries (jar files) of the plugin must be added to the development class path throughout the plugin dependency hierarchy if the developing code depends on that plugin. 
For example, if the code depends on plugin A; plugin A depends on plugins B and C; plugin B depends on plugin D, the libraries of all plugins (A, B, C, and D) must be added to the class path.For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying plugin dependencies' space='MD2024x'] page.

When you launch your own plugin, you need to add all jar filesthat are required by your pluginfrom appropriate plugins. The MagicDraw jar files can be found in *MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/lib* and its sub directories, whereas plugins' jar files can be found in *MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/plugins* and its sub directories.

Even if the plugin descriptor file contains information about a runtime plugin *.jar* file, it is not necessary to build and deploy this *.jar*file to a plugin directory while a plugin is developed under IntelliJ IDEA.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The project can be found in <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">&lt;modeling tool  installation directory&gt;/openapi/ide/intellij.zip.</em></p><p>Let's use MagicDraw as an example to describe the following procedures.</p><p><br /></p><p>To setup the IntelliJ IDEA environment for the MagicDraw (<span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">or other program according to a modeling tool you are using</span>) development</p><hr /><ol><li>In IntelliJ IDEA, add a path variable named MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY and pointing to your version of MagicDraw (CSM, CEA that you have the licence for, as it starts like a normal version and will require an activated licence) installation directory.</li></ol><p>      2. Extract <em style="line-height: 1.4285715;">&lt;MagicDraw installation directory&gt;/openapi/ide/intellij.zip </em>to a chosen <span>directory </span>.</p><p>      3. Open the extracted IntelliJ IDEA project <em>MagicDraw development.ipr </em>from that <span>directory </span>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="979e476a-c332-4829-b545-e533465a22a7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The launch configuration is designed to load plugins from the program installation directory (see step #1) and two plugins from the IDEA project. Thus, if the <em>md.plugins.dir</em> java system property is not defined (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Plugins directories" /></ac:link>), developing plugins are not loaded.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p>After projects are imported and the launch configurations are prepared, the IDEA project are ready for the source code development and running/debugging.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c27f445-666e-4c1f-918f-1b0d5c52c5fa"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The libraries (jar files) of the plugin must be added to the development class path throughout the plugin dependency hierarchy if the developing code depends on that plugin.</span><br /><span>For example, if the code depends on plugin A; plugin A depends on plugins B and C; plugin B depends on plugin D, the libraries of all plugins (A, B, C, and D) must be added to the class path.<br /></span><span>For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Identifying plugin dependencies" /></ac:link> page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="535eba12-13fe-4164-9536-bcf3bc0281c1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you launch your own plugin, you need to add all jar files</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> that are required by your plugin</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> from appropriate plugins. The MagicDraw jar files can be found in <em>MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/lib</em> and its sub directories, whereas plugins' jar files can be found in <em>MAGIC_DRAW_INSTALL_DIRECTORY/plugins</em> and its sub directories.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b206bb9c-fbb2-4cd4-abdf-61dfd0080691"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Even if the plugin descriptor file contains information about a runtime plugin <em>.jar</em> file, it is not necessary to build and deploy this <em>.jar </em>file to a plugin directory while a plugin is developed under IntelliJ IDEA.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711016 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00441: Device

- page_id: `136711016`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711016/Device
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:12.312+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Device is a physical computational resource, such as a piece of hardware (a desktop computer, processor, or server), or a human work unit (a department or team). A device is commonly composed of other devices. Composition may be modeled by placing the contained device in the node icon representing the containing device.

A Devicecan be specified by changing its property values in the Device[Specification window](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Specification+window). Each property is described in the description area on this window.

The Device DeviceSpecification windowconsists of the following specific specification panes:

- Nested Nodes : lists nodes defined (nested) within the device.
- Deployed Artifacts : an artifact or artifact instance that has been deployed to a deployment target.

You can format the Device symbol properties in the[Symbol Properties dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Formatting+symbols).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">A Device is a physical computational resource, such as a piece of hardware (a desktop computer, processor, or server), or a human work unit (a department or team). A device is commonly composed of other devices. Composition may be modeled by placing the contained device in the node icon representing the containing device.      </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">A <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Device </span>can be specified by changing its property values in the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Device</span> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Specification+window">Specification window</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Each property is described in the description area on this window. </span></span></p><p><span class="hcp1">The</span> <span>Device</span> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Device</span> </span>Specification window<span class="hcp1"> consists of the following specific specification panes:</span></p><ul><li class="kadov-p"><span class="hcp2"><span>Nested</span></span> <span><span>Nodes</span></span><span class="hcp1">: lists</span><span class="hcp1"> nodes</span> <span class="kadov-p">defined (nested) within the device.</span></li><li class="kadov-p"><span class="hcp2"><span>Deployed</span></span> <span class="hcp2"><span>Artifacts</span></span><span class="hcp1">:</span> an artifact or artifact instance that has been deployed to a deployment target.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can format the Device symbol properties in the </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Formatting+symbols">Symbol Properties dialog</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.</span></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710756 space=MD2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00442: Diagram aspects

- page_id: `136710756`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710756/Diagram+aspects
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:48:49.942+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: ['diagram-stereotype']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

581017250

581017252

581017251

INLINE

Diagram aspects allow you to create different views, e.g., electrical, mechanical, and optical, for the same system structure. The aspects functionality is driven by stereotypes, which allows you to use predefined aspects (only included in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024x'] or other customizations) or custom aspects for diagrams. This enables you to:

- Set any number of aspects for one diagram.
- Drill down navigation from the Part shape to its internal structure.
- Create an Aspect diagram directly from the Part shape.
- Display parts and ports according to aspects.
- Use aspects with multiple [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Layout templates' space=''].

The figure below demonstrates the usage of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Finding predefined diagram aspects' space='']predefined diagram aspects. Three SysML Internal Block Diagrams of the same *Climate Control Hardware* system are of different aspects: electrical and communication. The Internal Block Diagram with the electrical aspect shows only the electrical structure of the *Climate Control Hardware* system. Correspondingly, the IBD with the communication aspect shows only the communication structure.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Climate Control Hardware views through different aspects. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.

The main workflow to start using the diagrams aspect functionality in your model is the following.

To use diagram aspects

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a new project' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening projects' space=''] one.
2. Enable the diagrams aspect option. Enabling or disabling diagram aspects option>>
3. Make sure that predefined aspects are enough for you. Finding predefined diagram aspects>>
4. If predefined aspects are not what you expect, create your own aspect that you can apply to a diagram. Creating custom aspects>>
5. Create a new aspect diagram: - by creating a new diagram with already applied aspect: Creating an aspect diagram>> 
- by applying predefined (#3) or custom aspects (step #4) for an existing diagram. Applying or removing aspect for preexisting diagrams>>
6. After your model contains diagrams with defined aspects, you can display parts and ports according to aspects and navigate among aspect diagrams.

581017248

****

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='']

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Diagram aspects** sample models that comes with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024x']. To open this sample properly you need to install the SysML plugin in the MagicDraw and:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710512 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00443: Diagram banners

- page_id: `136710512`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710512/Diagram+banners
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:43.163+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

923016198

923016209

923016197

**On this page**

923016199

You can create diagram banners, such as a header and/or footer, to display important information on the diagram. The banners are not included in the diagram frame but are contained within the diagram pane; thus, if you choose to export the diagram ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving as image' space='MD2024x'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Printing' space='MD2024x']), the banners are included. The diagram banners can be created for all diagram types, e.g. tables, matrices, or maps.

##### Creating diagram banners

To create a diagram banner

1. Right-click the diagram. In the shortcut menu, select Stereotype . The Stereotype selection menu opens.
2. In the Stereotype selection menu, select the «banner» stereotype check mark. Click the Apply button. [ATTACHMENT filename='stereotype_selection.png']
3. Right-click the diagram again and open its Specification window.
4. In the Specification window, click the Tags property group.
5. Under the «banner» stereotype, select the text tag and click the Create Value button. [ATTACHMENT filename='banner_creation.png']
6. In the Value box on the right side of the dialog, write the text that you want to have displayed on the diagram banner.
7. In the toolbar of the right-side compartment, next to the Display option, select the position for the diagram banner from the dropdown menu.
  1. Header - the diagram banner will be displayed at the top of the diagram. This is the default option.
  2. Footer - the diagram banner will be displayed at the bottom of the diagram.
  3. Both - the diagram banner will be displayed at the top and bottom of the diagram.
  4. **None** - the diagram banner will not be displayed on the diagram. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. (Optional) To create additional banners for the diagram, click the **Add** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button and follow the steps 6-7.
9. Click **Close**. The diagram banner is created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Editing diagram banners

To edit the diagram banner text and/or display position

1. Right-click the diagram and open its **Specification window**.
2. In the Specification window, click the Tags property group.
3. Select the **text** value under the «banner» stereotype.
4. In the Value box on the right-side compartment, select the text value you want to edit and click the Edit [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
5. Edit the text of the banner and/or the display position via the Display option for the selected text value.
6. Click Close .

##### Removing diagram banners

To remove a diagram banner

1. Right-click the diagram and open its **Specification window**.
2. In the Specification window, click the Tags property group.
3. Select the **text** value under the «banner» stereotype.
4. Do one of the following:
  1. To remove all banners, click the Remove Value button.
  2. To remove a single banner, in the Value box on the right-side compartment, select the text value you want to remove and click the Remove [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
5. Click Close .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The banners are not included in the diagram frame but are contained within the diagram pane; thus, if you choose to export the diagram (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Saving as image" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[save it as an image]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Printing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[print it]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), the banners are included. The diagram banners can be created for all diagram types, e.g. tables, matrices, or maps. </p><h3>Creating diagram banners</h3><p>To create a diagram banner</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the diagram. This is the default option.</li><li><strong>Footer</strong> - the diagram banner will be displayed at the bottom of the diagram.</li><li><strong>Both</strong> - the diagram banner will be displayed at the top and bottom of the diagram.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>None</strong> - the diagram banner will not be displayed on the diagram.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_position_change.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Diagram banners" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">(Optional) To create additional banners for the diagram, click the <strong>Add</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2022-5-24_12-25-51.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Diagram banners" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button and follow the steps 6-7.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Close</strong>. /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3>Editing diagram banners</h3><p>To edit the diagram banner text and/or display position</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click the diagram and open its <strong>Specification window</strong>.</p></li><li>In the Specification window, click the <strong>Tags</strong> property group.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the <strong>text</strong> value under the «banner» stereotype.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Value</strong> box on the right-side compartment, select the <strong>text</strong> value you want to edit and <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710498 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00445: Diagram frame

- page_id: `136710498`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710498/Diagram+frame
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:42.159+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

568201751

568201754

568201753

All UML diagrams have the content area that is restricted by the diagram frame as it is stated in UML specification. The diagram frame is primarily used in cases where the diagrammed element has graphical border elements (like ports for classes and components, entry/exit points on state machines).

By default the diagram frame is displayed on the new diagram pane. The frame is a rectangle in all diagrams, except state machine and activity. State machine and activity diagram frames have rounded corners.

The frame can be re-sized manually by dragging its corners or borders.

To hide the diagram frame, do one of the following

- Right-click the diagram and on the shortcut menu, cancel the selection of the Show Diagram Frame .
- Open the Diagram Properties dialog and set the Show Diagram Frame property to false.

To change the diagram frame properties, do one of the following

- In the Project Options dialog > Symbol styles > Diagram > Diagram Frame options group, change the desired options.
- In the Diagram Properties dialog > Diagram Frame property group, change the properties.

To hide the icon on the diagram frame

- In the Diagram Properties dialog, change the Show Stereotypes property value to Text . [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram_frame.png']

When a new diagram is created, the diagram stereotype icon is displayed on the diagram frame header by default, though you can change it to the context stereotype icon.

To display the context stereotype icon on the diagram frame

- In the **Diagram Properties** dialog > **Diagram Frame** property group, change the **Use Stereotype** property value to **Context**. The **Use Stereotype** property takes effect only in case the **Show Stereotypes** property value is **Text and Icon** or **Icon**. There is a possibility to show the abbreviation of a diagram type instead of the full diagram type in the diagram frame header.

To display the abbreviated diagram type

- In the Diagram Properties dialog > Diagram Frame property group, select the Show Abbreviated Type check box.

The abbreviated diagram types are listed in the following table.

| Diagram name | Abbreviation |
| --- | --- |
| Use Case | uc |
| Communication | comm |
| Sequence | sd |
| State Machine | stm |
| Activity | sct |
| Composite Structure | cs |

568201750

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Note' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710807 space=MD2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00446: Diagram image export

- page_id: `136710807`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710807/Diagram+image+export
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-02-27T08:03:15.986+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

218191344

INLINE

218191365

INLINE

218191343

INLINE

**On this page:**

218191355

INLINE

Before exporting a diagram, you should specify the Image Export options in the **Project Options** dialog. You can also make additional image export changes in the**Environment Options** dialog. These options are applicable to all operations dedicated to exporting or previewing diagrams, such as:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CATIA Systems Synthesis' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating reports from the Containment tree' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving as image' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Printing' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Printing' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing Cameo Collaborator documents' space='MCS2024x']

##### Specifying Project Options for diagram image export

Via the Image Export options in the **Project Options** dialog, you can specify the dimensions for the exported diagram, what should be done if these dimensions are exceeded, and the time it can take to build it (the latter applies only to tables and matrices). The changes you make in the **Project Options** dialog apply only to a specific project.

To specify project options for diagram image export

- In the main menu, select Options > Project > under the General group, select Diagrams > modify the options in the Image Export group. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_options_Image_Export.png']

In the table below, you can see the descriptions of the diagram Image Export options and their default values.

| Project Option | Default Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Time Limit to Build Diagram | 60 | Specify the maximum time (in seconds) allotted for diagram building while exporting a diagram. If the time limit is exceeded, the diagram is not exported. Set to 0 to export diagram without the time limit.The option applies to tables and matrices only. |
| Image Height Limit | 10 000 | Specify the maximum image height (in pixels) for diagram export. If the height limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image height limit. |
| Image Width Limit | 10 000 | Specify the maximum image width (in pixels) for diagram export. If the width limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export diagram without the image width limit. |
| Export Preference When Limits Exceeded | Export Cropped Diagram | Specify the diagram export preference if the diagram image height and/or width limits are exceeded. |

If the **Export Preference When Limits Exceeded** value is set to **Export Cropped Diagram**, the previewed or exported diagram is cropped based on the specified dimensions, displaying an incomplete view of the diagram that looks like the image below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Diagram image export notifications and statistics

The following table displays the expected results of specific diagram image export options.

| Export option | Successful export | Diagram does not comply with Image Export options or the export failed |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Save as Image | All diagrams are saved as they are in the modeling tool.The file location is opened. | Single diagram export | Multiple diagrams export |
| The diagram is saved as it is in the modeling tool.The file location is opened. | A notification is displayed with the information about image export, e.g.: Click Details... to see Diagram Image Export Statistics. |  |  |
| MDZipX Export | All diagrams are exported as they are in the modeling tool.The file location is opened. | A notification is displayed with the information about image export, e.g.: Click Details... to see Diagram Image Export Statistics. |  |
| MDZipX Publish | All diagrams are published as they are in the modeling tool.A notification is displayed with the information about successful publishing, e.g.: | A notification is displayed with the information about image export, e.g.: Click Details... to see Diagram Image Export Statistics. |  |

The **Details...** link in the notifications opens the **Notification Window**, which displays **Diagram Image Export Statistics**with the following information:

- Total : the number of exported diagrams.
- Cropped : the number of diagrams exported with limited dimensions if the Export Preference When Limits Exceeded option is set to Export Cropped Diagram .
- **Failed**: the number of diagrams not exported due to the exceeded building time and/or dimension limits if the **Export Preference When Limits Exceeded** option is set to *Do Not Export*. It also displays the number of diagrams whose export failed. The **Failed**and**Cropped** indications are only displayed if there are any cropped or failed diagram images. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Specifying Environment Options for image export

You can also make additional image export changes in the modeling tool's environment, such as image resolution and compression quality. The modifications you make in the **Environment Options** dialog persist when the application is closed and re-opened and thus apply to all projects.

To specify environment options for image export

- In the main menu, select **Options** > **Environment** > under the **General** group, select**Image Export** > modify the options in the **Image Export** group. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the table below, you can see the descriptions of the environment Image Export options and their default values.

| Environment Option | Default Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Save Diagram Background in Image | false | Set to true to save the diagram together with its background as an image. Otherwise, the diagram background becomes white after saving the diagram as an image. |
| JPEG Compression Quality | 1.0 | Specify the quality for saving an image as a JPEG file. Set the value to 1.0 to define the highest quality. |
| Use SVG <text> Tag for Text Output | false | Set to true to turn all text into SVG shapes during the image conversion. |
| Render SVG Icons as Raster Images in EMF | false | Set to true to render SVG icons as raster images in the EMF output. Otherwise, the SVG icons are rendered as vector graphics. |
| Image Resolution (DPI) | 72 | Specify the measure for spatial printing. |
| Exported Image Size [%] | 100 | Specify exported image scaling ratio in percent. If the set value is higher than 100, then the view is enlarged (zoomed in) before generating an image. Raster image will not lose its quality as additional pixels are introduced. |
| TIFF Color Space | RGB | Specify the color format for the tagged image file format (TIFF). |
| TIFF Compression | LZW | Specify the type for compression of the tagged image file format (TIFF). |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eede975c-b8de-4264-9ec7-5f25e9552cd0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">218191344</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd1dfbbf-816d-4504-9714-99275e734029"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">218191365</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c5bd017-f8eb-4432-af89-e367dc292569"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">218191343</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a58d411f-b80d-44e9-9be9-e7791c540698" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6688e1f6-28e5-4f63-b7a6-37ff16687ec0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">218191355</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before exporting a diagram, you should specify the Image Export options in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog. You can also make additional image export changes in the<strong> Environment Options</strong> dialog. These options are applicable to all operations dedicated to exporting or previewing diagrams, such as:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="CATIA Systems Synthesis" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Publish/Export to MDZipX File]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating reports from the Containment tree" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Saving as image" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Save as Image]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Printing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Print Preview]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Printing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Print as PDF]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Publishing Cameo Collaborator documents" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Publishing to Cameo Collaborator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h3>Specifying Project Options for diagram image export</h3><p>Via the Image Export options in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, you can specify the dimensions for the exported diagram, what should be done if these dimensions are exceeded, and the time it can take to build it (the latter applies only to tables and matrices). The changes you make in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog apply only to a specific project.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify project options for diagram image export</p><hr /><ul><li>In the main menu, select <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Options</strong> &gt; <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Project</strong> &gt; under the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">General</strong> group, select <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Diagrams</strong> &gt; modify the options in the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Image Export</strong> group.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_options_Image_Export.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>In the table below, you can see the descriptions of the diagram Image Export options and their default values.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 99.9333%;"><colgroup> <col style="width: 19.9635%;" /> <col style="width: 14.222%;" /> <col style="width: 65.8261%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Project Option</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Time Limit to Build Diagram</td><td>60</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specify the maximum time (in seconds) allotted for diagram building while exporting a diagram. If the time limit is exceeded, the diagram is not exported. Set to 0 to export diagram without the time limit.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2ca56a7-a9cb-472a-b6a1-09837763f6c3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The option applies to tables and matrices <span>only.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>Image Height Limit<span> </span></td><td>10 000<span> </span></td><td><p>Specify the maximum image height (in pixels) for diagram export. If the height limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image height limit.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Image Width Limit<span> </span></td><td>10 000<span> </span></td><td><p>Specify the maximum image width (in pixels) for diagram export. If the width limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export diagram without the image width limit.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Export Preference When Limits Exceeded</td><td colspan="1"><p>Export Cropped Diagram</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Specify the diagram export preference if the diagram image height and/or width limits are exceeded.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>If the <strong>Export Preference When Limits Exceeded</strong> value is set to <span><strong>Export Cropped Diagram</strong>, the previewed or exported diagram is cropped based on the specified dimensions, displaying an incomplete view of the diagram that looks like the image below.</span></p><p><span> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cropped_image_sample.png" /></ac:image> </span></p><h3><span>Diagram image export notifications and statistics</span></h3><p><span>The following table displays the expected results of specific diagram image export options.</span></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 98.9326%;"><colgroup> <col style="width: 17.68%;" /> <col style="width: 13.1588%;" /> <col style="width: 17.5456%;" /> <col style="width: 23.4841%;" /> <col style="width: 28.1433%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: left;"><span>Export option</span></th><th style="text-align: left;" colspan="2">Successful export</th><th style="text-align: left;" colspan="2"><span>Diagram does not comply with Image Export options or the export failed</span></th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">Save as Image</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="2"><ul><li>All diagrams are saved as they are in the modeling tool.</li><li>The file location is opened.</li></ul><p><br /></p></td><th class="highlight-grey" title="Background colour : Grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span title="">Single diagram export</span></th><th class="highlight-grey" title="Background colour : Grey" colspan="1" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span title="">Multiple diagrams export</span></th></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><ul><li>The diagram is saved as it is in the modeling tool.</li><li>The file location is opened.</li></ul></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li>A notification is displayed with the information about image export, e.g.:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_as_image_notification.png" /></ac:image> <br />Click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Details...</strong> to see <strong>Diagram Image Export Statistics</strong>.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td>MDZipX Export</td><td colspan="2"><ul><li><span>All diagrams are exported as they are in the modeling tool.</span></li><li><span>The file location is opened.</span></li></ul></td><td colspan="2"><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li>A notification is displayed with the information about image export, e.g.:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="mdzipx_export_notification.png" /></ac:image> <br />Click <strong>Details...</strong> to see <strong>Diagram Image Export Statistics</strong>.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">MDZipX Publish</td><td colspan="2"><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li><span>All diagrams are published as they are in the modeling tool.</span></li><li><span>A notification is displayed with the information about successful publishing, e.g.:<br /></span> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="mdzipx_publish_successful_notification.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></div></td><td colspan="2"><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li>A notification is displayed with the information about image export, e.g.:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="mdzipx_publish_issues_notification.png" /></ac:image> <br />Click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Details...</strong> to see <strong>Diagram Image Export Statistics</strong>.</li></ul></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p>The <strong>Details...</strong> link in the notifications opens the <strong>Notification Window</strong>, which displays <span> <strong>Diagram Image Export Statistics </strong>with the following information:</span></p><ul><li><strong>Total</strong>: the number of exported diagrams.</li><li><strong>Cropped</strong>: the number of diagrams exported with limited dimensions if the <strong>Export Preference When Limits Exceeded</strong> option is set to <em>Export Cropped Diagram</em>. </li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Failed</strong>: the number of diagrams not exported due to the exceeded building time and/or dimension limits if the <strong>Export Preference When Limits Exceeded</strong> option is set to <em>Do Not Export</em>. It also displays the number of diagrams whose export failed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7adbb902-2246-47db-90a3-853e75ffcdb6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Failed </strong>and<strong> Cropped</strong> indications are only displayed if there are any cropped or failed diagram images.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Diagram_image_export_notification_window.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ul><h3><span>Specifying Environment Options for image export</span></h3><p><span>You can also make additional image export changes in the modeling tool's environment, such as image resolution and compression quality. The modifications you make in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog persist when the application is closed and re-opened and thus apply to all projects.<br /></span></p><p><span> <br /></span></p><p><span>To specify environment options for image export</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span>In the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong> &gt; under the <strong>General</strong> group, select<strong> Image Export</strong> &gt; modify the options in the <strong>Image Export</strong> group.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="environment_options_Image_Export.png" /></ac:image> <br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></span></li></ul><p><span> <br /></span></p><p><span>In the table below, you can see the descriptions of the environment Image Export options and their default values.</span></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 99.9333%;"><colgroup> <col style="width: 26.1878%;" /> <col style="width: 11.423%;" /> <col style="width: 62.4008%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Environment Option</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Save Diagram Background in Image</td><td>false</td><td>Set to true to save the diagram together with its background as an image. Otherwise, the diagram background becomes white after saving the diagram as an image.</td></tr><tr><td>JPEG Compression Quality</td><td>1.0</td><td>Specify the quality for saving an image as a JPEG file. Set the value to 1.0 to define the highest quality.</td></tr><tr><td>Use SVG &lt;text&gt; Tag for Text Output</td><td>false</td><td>Set to true to turn all text into SVG shapes during the image conversion.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Render SVG Icons as Raster Images in EMF</td><td colspan="1">false</td><td colspan="1">Set to true to render SVG icons as raster images in the EMF output. Otherwise, the SVG icons are rendered as vector graphics.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Image Resolution (DPI)</td><td colspan="1">72</td><td colspan="1">Specify the measure for spatial printing.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Exported Image Size [%]</td><td colspan="1">100</td><td colspan="1">Specify exported image scaling ratio in percent. If the set value is higher than 100, then the view is enlarged (zoomed in) before generating an image. Raster image will not lose its quality as additional pixels are introduced.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">TIFF Color Space</td><td colspan="1">RGB</td><td colspan="1">Specify the color format for the tagged image file format (TIFF).</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">TIFF Compression</td><td colspan="1">LZW</td><td colspan="1">Specify the type for compression of the tagged image file format (TIFF).</td></tr></tbody></table><p><span> <br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710497 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00447: Diagram name and its context name synchronization

- page_id: `136710497`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710497/Diagram+name+and+its+context+name+synchronization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:42.015+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

568146964

568146967

568146965

The diagram name and its context name are synchronized automatically.

Create an Activity diagram. Type a name for the Activity diagram, for example, Receive. The name of the Activity automatically changes to Receive. And conversely - change the name of the Activity and the Activity diagram name will be changed automatically. This is a synchronization of a diagram name and its context name:

Synchronization works in the following cases:

- Activity and Activity diagram inside.
- Interaction and Communication or Sequence diagram inside.
- (Protocol) State Machine and (Protocol) State Machine diagram inside.
- Class and all available inner diagrams inside.

To turn off the synchronization

- In the Environment options dialog, General > Editing options group, cancel the selection of the Synchronize the diagram name with its context name option.

568146963

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Type a name for the Activity diagram, for example, Receive. The name of the Activity automatically changes to Receive. And conversely - change the name of the Activity and the Activity diagram name will be changed automatically. This is a synchronization of a diagram name and its context name:</p><p>Synchronization works in the following cases:</p><ul><li>Activity and Activity diagram inside.</li><li>Interaction and Communication or Sequence diagram inside.</li><li>(Protocol) State Machine and (Protocol) State Machine diagram inside.</li><li>Class and all available inner diagrams inside.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To turn off the synchronization</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Environment</strong> options dialog, <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Editing</strong> options group, cancel the selection of the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Synchronize the diagram name with its context name</strong> option.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cf1d89c-0435-418c-b5ed-a6bc481cb496"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">568146963</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711447 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00448: Diagram Overview

- page_id: `136711447`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711447/Diagram+Overview
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:41.143+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Auxiliary diagram symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Functionality Availability

490535609

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Functionality Availability

490535611

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Functionality Availability

490535610

#### NOTE: Functionality Availability

Functionality Availability

The diagram overview functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

A diagram overview shape provides the ability to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Overviewing other diagrams' space='MD2024x'] on a diagram pane. When using the diagram overview shape, you can overview [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tables' space='MD2024x'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Relation Map' space='MD2024x'] on the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of using the Diagram Overview functionality

You can format the diagram overview representation properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

To open the particular diagram's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'], double-click the diagram overview shape. The description of each diagram property appears in the same window. Descriptions are found in the description area at the bottom of the Specification window.

To create a diagram overview shape from the diagram palette

1. On the diagram palette, under the Common category, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-28 at 5.16.31 PM.png'] Diagram Overview button. The Select Diagram dialog opens.
2. Select the diagram for which you want to create a diagram overview shape and click OK .
3. Click the diagram pane outside the dialog. The diagram overview shape is created and shows the full content of the corresponding diagram.

490535608

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Auxiliary diagram symbols' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024x']

****

****

**Sample model**

A diagram overview sample project in*<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\product features\diagram overview.mdzip*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715201 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00449: Diagram presentation element

- page_id: `136715201`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715201/Diagram+presentation+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:11.951+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols)
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[*com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DiagramPresentationElement*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/symbols/DiagramPresentationElement.html) is a one to one mapping to UML [*com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Diagram.html). It means for every [*Diagram*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Diagram.html)in a model exists one and only one [*DiagramPresentationElement*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/symbols/DiagramPresentationElement.html). Use *[com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getDiagram(](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html#getDiagram-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram-)[Diagram](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Diagram.html)[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html#getDiagram-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram-)* to get it for a Diagram.

The purpose of this presentation element is to manage all other presentation elements used in a Diagram:

- To get a collection of inner presentation elements, use the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement.getPresentationElements()* method. This method returns only direct children of the diagram.
- To open a diagram in the program UI, call [DiagramPresentationElement.open()](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/symbols/DiagramPresentationElement.html#open--) .
- To create/modify/delete inner presentation elements of the diagram, use [com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.PresentationElementsManager](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/openapi/uml/PresentationElementsManager.html) .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It means for every <a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Diagram.html"><em>Diagram</em> </a>in a model exists one and only one <a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/symbols/DiagramPresentationElement.html"><em>DiagramPresentationElement</em></a>. Use <em><a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html#getDiagram-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram-">com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Project.getDiagram(</a><a title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel" href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Diagram.html">Diagram</a><a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/core/Project.html#getDiagram-com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram-">)</a></em> to get it for a Diagram.</p><p>The purpose of this presentation element is to manage all other presentation elements used in a Diagram:</p><ul><li>To get a collection of inner presentation elements, use the <a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/symbols/PresentationElement.html#getPresentationElements--"><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement.getPresentationElements()</em></a> method. ````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715190 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00450: Diagram types

- page_id: `136715190`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715190/Diagram+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:11.614+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Working with diagrams
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two groups of diagrams in our modeling tools – creatable and not creatable. Only the diagrams of a creatable type can be created (instantiated). A not creatable diagram serves as the base for other types of diagrams.

There are 17 predefined types of diagrams (13 creatable and 4 not). Creatable diagrams are:

- Class
- Use Case
- Object
- Communication
- Sequence
- State Machine
- Protocol State Machine
- Activity
- Composite Structure
- Component
- Deployment
- Package
- Profile

Not creatable diagrams:

- Interaction
- Behavior
- Any
- Static

Communication and Sequence diagrams are subdiagrams of the Interaction diagram. Activity, Interaction, State Machine, and Protocol State Machine diagrams are subdiagrams of the Behavior diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The only way to add a new diagram type to the modeling tool is to extend one of the already existing diagram types and register it. This mechanism is described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding new diagram types' space='MD2024x'].

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

A not creatable diagram serves as the base for other types of diagrams.</p><p>There are 17 predefined types of diagrams (13 creatable and 4 not). Creatable diagrams are:</p><ul><li>Class</li><li>Use Case</li><li>Object</li><li>Communication</li><li>Sequence</li><li>State Machine</li><li>Protocol State Machine</li><li>Activity</li><li>Composite Structure</li><li>Component</li><li>Deployment</li><li>Package</li><li>Profile</li></ul><p><br />Not creatable diagrams:</p><ul><li>Interaction</li><li>Behavior</li><li>Any</li><li>Static</li></ul><p>Communication and Sequence diagrams are subdiagrams of the Interaction diagram. This mechanism is described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Adding new diagram types" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d2f565e-f93a-44ee-a965-cd4978a35814"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="eb619759-b916-4ba7-89cb-addce6204b76"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710444 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00451: Diagramming

- page_id: `136710444`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710444/Diagramming
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:39.078+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When working with diagrams it is helpful to keep the following concepts in mind:

- A shape refers to the notation of a model element, such as a package, class, state, use case, object, etc.
- A path refers to the notation for the various kinds of relationships, such as associations, aggregations, dependency, message, and links.
- Both paths and shapes are defined as symbols.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When working with diagrams it is helpful to keep the following concepts in mind:</p><ul><li>A shape refers to the notation of a model element, such as a package, class, state, use case, object, etc.</li><li>A path refers to the notation for the various kinds of relationships, such as associations, aggregations, dependency, message, and links.</li><li>Both paths and shapes are defined as symbols.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709247 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00453: Diagrams tab

- page_id: `136709247`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709247/Diagrams+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:39.283+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > MagicDraw getting started > Understanding the user interface > Model Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425670349

1425670351

1425670350

INLINE

The Diagrams tab in the Model Browser represents diagrams created in your project. 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the **Diagrams** tab, diagrams are by default grouped by their types (see the image above).

To open the Diagrams tab, do one of the following

- At the top of the Model Browser, click the Diagrams tab.
- If the Diagrams tab is hidden, from the **Window** menu, select **Diagrams**. You can change some Diagrams tab properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MD2024x']**Environment Options** dialog in the **Browser** options group.

The Diagrams tab consists of the following two components:

- Diagrams tab toolbar
- Diagrams tree

##### Diagrams tab toolbar

The Diagrams tab toolbar contains the following button:

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Collapse All | Collapses all of the current elements in the Containment tree. |
|  | Collapse Selected Recursively | Collapses only the selected node and all subnodes in that node. |
|  | Group by Diagram Name | Groups diagrams into packages by the first symbol of their name when the model contains more than 99 diagrams. |
|  | Favorites | Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:Add a selected element to favoritesOpen the dialog for managing favoritesNavigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree |
|  | Quick Find | Opens the Quick Find dialog. |

Some of these actions may be performed right in the shortcut menu of the Diagrams tree.

##### Working with model elements in the Diagrams tree

You can work with diagrams in the Diagrams tree using the commands available in the selected diagram's shortcut menu. These are the commonly used commands:

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Open | Opens the selected diagram. |
| Print | Opens the Print dialog. Active for the open diagram only. |
| Select in Containment Tree | Opens the Containment tab and selects the current element in the Containment tree. |
| Group by Diagram Name | Groups diagrams into packages by the first symbol of their name when the model contains more than 99 diagrams. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709304 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00454: Dialog for customizing selected modeling tool area

- page_id: `136709304`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709304/Dialog+for+customizing+selected+modeling+tool+area
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-26T15:37:54.656+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > MagicDraw getting started > Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The **Customize <Area>** dialog enables you to customize your own environment, from simple to complex or vice versa. You choose which menus and commands should be displayed and which should be hidden. Customization allows you to make your own working environment.

The name of this dialog depends on the area you select. For example, if you select Main Menu, the dialog's name is **Customize Main Menu**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the open dialog, select each environment element individually and choose the mode this element should be available in. There are three modes:

- Standard and Expert . The selected element will be visible in the Standard as well as in the Expert mode. The Standard mode is less complicated and is meant for standard users.
- Expert . The selected elements will be visible only in the Expert mode.
- Hidden . The selected element will not be shown in any mode.

To assign an element to the mode

1. Select an element.
2. Click the radio button under the desired mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f7e2e90-e5be-4f55-82d0-3bbc01036363"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Customize &lt;Area&gt;</strong> dialog enables you to customize your own environment, from simple to complex or vice versa. You choose which menus and commands should be displayed and which should be hidden. Customization allows you to make your own working environment.</p><p>The name of this dialog depends on the area you select. For example, if you select Main Menu, the dialog's name is <strong>Customize Main Menu</strong>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Customize Main Menu" ac:alt="Customize Main Menu"><ri:attachment ri:filename="customize_main_menu.png" /></ac:image></p><p>In the open dialog, select each environment element individually and choose the mode this element should be available in. There are three modes:</p><ul><li><strong>Standard and Expert</strong>. The selected element will be visible in the <strong>Standard</strong> as well as in the <strong>Expert</strong> mode. The Standard mode is less complicated and is meant for standard users.</li><li><strong>Expert</strong>. The selected elements will be visible only in the Expert mode.</li><li><strong>Hidden</strong>. The selected element will not be shown in any mode.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To assign an element to the mode</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element.</li><li>Click the radio button under the desired mode.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714424 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00455: Dialog tool

- page_id: `136714424`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714424/Dialog+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:36.501+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1358524304

1358524305

1358524305

The purpose of a Dialog tool is to enable report templates to call functions for creating dialogs to interact with users during the report generation process. To modify the report templates, you can use the Dialog tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Class Diagram: DialogTool.

The Dialog tool uses the following methods:

- message This method will create message dialogs.

- confirm This method will create confirmation dialogs and return the Boolean value ‘true’ when you click the OK button or return ‘false’ when you click the Cancel button.

- input This method will create input dialogs and return the input value as a string.

- **sort** 
This method will create Sort and Enable dialogs and return a collection of newly-sorted results.

- **select** 
This method will create a Selection dialog and return a collection of the selected elements in the dialog.

Like other Custom tools, the Dialog tool (*dialogtool.jar*) must be installed in the ‘extensions’ folder of the Report Wizard plugin.

To import a Dialog tool to a template, type the following:

```text

```

1358524303

**Related page**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="053f0292-5914-4a9f-87d9-99342165b1dc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358524304</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="122a71ed-4dca-4ce9-8259-00f56e4be32c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358524305</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96107810-9159-4da6-9554-f6a146ca9239"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358524305</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The purpose of a Dialog tool is to enable report templates to call functions for creating dialogs to interact with users during the report generation process. To modify the report templates, you can use the Dialog tool.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Dialog Tool.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Class Diagram: DialogTool.</h6><p>The Dialog tool uses the following methods:</p><ul><li><strong>message</strong> <br />This method will create message dialogs.</li></ul><ul><li><strong>confirm</strong> <br />This method will create confirmation dialogs and return the Boolean value ‘true’ when you click the OK button or return ‘false’ when you click the Cancel button.</li></ul><ul><li><strong>input</strong> <br />This method will create input dialogs and return the input value as a string.</li></ul><ul><li><p><strong>sort</strong><br />This method will create Sort and Enable dialogs and return a collection of newly-sorted results.</p></li></ul><ul><li><p><strong>select</strong><br />This method will create a Selection dialog and return a collection of the selected elements in the dialog.</p></li></ul><p>Like other Custom tools, the Dialog tool (<em>dialogtool.jar</em>) must be installed in the ‘extensions’ folder of the Report Wizard plugin.</p><p>To import a Dialog tool to a template, type the following:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="645da148-8fa6-4842-b75c-5d2bbfc806b8"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('dialog','com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.DialogTool')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7a8b3aa-3ce1-434b-bbca-5b360ce1bd0d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358524303</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ceac5dc3-fa55-4562-91e8-0c3e759cddd1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714427 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00456: Dialog tool API

- page_id: `136714427`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714427/Dialog+tool+API
