#### ‘message’ Method

**message(String message) : void**. This method will create a **Message** dialog.

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Message dialog.

#### ‘confirm’ Method

**confirm(String message) : boolean**. This method will create a **Confirmation** dialog. It will return the Boolean value ‘true’ when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or ‘false’ when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the dialog.

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Confirmation dialog.

#### ‘input’ Method

##### Input dialog with text

**input(String message) : String**. This method will create an **Input** dialog from a message. It will return the input values as Strings when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or ‘null’ when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the dialog.

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Input dialog with text.

##### Input dialog with text and initial value

**input(String message, String initialValue)** : String. This method will create a message and add an initial value in an **Input** dialog. It will return the input values as Strings when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or ‘null’ when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Input dialog with text and an initial value.

##### Input dialog with text and initial value Array

**input(String message, Collection options)** : String. This method will create a message and add initial value arrays in an **Input** dialog. It will return the input values as Strings when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or ‘null’ when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Input dialog with text and initial value arrays.

#### ‘sort’ Method

##### Sort and Enable dialog

**sort(Collection list) : Collection**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from collections. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Sort and Enable dialog.

##### Sort and Enable dialog with text

**sort(String message, Collection list)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Sort and Enable dialog with text.

##### Sort and Enable dialog with a default element selection option

**sort(Collection list, Boolean defaultEnabled)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

##### Sort and Enable dialog with a dialog title

**sort(Collection list, String title, defaultEnabled : Boolean)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with a dialog title. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

##### Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description

**sort(String message, Collection list)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with dialog description. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

##### Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description and a default element selection option

**sort(String message, Collection list, Boolean defaultEnabled)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with dialog description and a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

##### Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description, a dialog title, and a default element selection option

**sort(String message, Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultEnabled)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with dialog description, a dialog title, and a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

##### Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description, a dialog title, a default element selection option, and an option for hiding element icons

**sort(String message, Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultEnabled, Boolean showElementIcon)**. This method will create a **Sort and Enable** dialog from text and collections with dialog description, a dialog title, a default element selection option, and an option for hiding element icons. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or return the original collection when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

#### 'select' Method

##### Default Selection dialog

**select(Collection list) : Collection**. This method will create a **Selection** dialog from a collection. Items in the collection will be listed in the **Selection** dialog. You can select the items and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to return a collection of the selected items or null when you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Selection dialog.

##### Selection dialog with a title

**select(Collection list, String title) : Collection**. This method will create a **Selection** dialog with a specified dialog title. The default **Selection** dialog title is **Selection**. You can select the items and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**.

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Selection dialog with title.

##### Selection dialog without selection

**select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected) : Collection**. This method will create a **Selection** dialog from the collection without selecting the items in the dialog. You can specify whether all listed items will be selected or not. The default value for defaultSelected is true. Otherwise, all listed items will not be selected. You can select the items and click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Selection dialog without selection.

##### Selection dialog with description text

**select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected, String dialogText) : Collection**. This method will create a **Selection** dialog from the collection. You can insert description text in the dialog to show the dialog behavior or more details of the listed items. The default value for the dialogText String is null. You can select the items and click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The code example is shown below:

```text

```

The output will be as follows:

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Selection dialog with text.

##### Selection dialog with an option for hiding element icons

**select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected, String dialogText, Boolean showElementIcon)**. This method will create a **Selection** dialog from the collection. You can insert description text in the dialog to show the dialog behavior or more details of the listed items. The default value for the dialogText String is null. You can select the items and click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. With this method, you can also hide element icons in the dialog.

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c004677-7e7f-4d5c-ad0f-6041d127cb3f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">815916921</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62fc515f-d9cd-4ffc-b71e-c3c344e852a0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">815916923</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae66343e-dc33-476f-8447-53f654f95b19"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">815916919</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5788a78-023d-417a-bb7d-7e98e39b332d"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91e9ae09-de8b-4f61-a45a-8446283c85cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">815916922</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h2>‘message’ Method</h2><p><strong>message(String message) : void</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Message</strong> dialog. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b8245b9-9013-4a69-acbe-cafa02079d8e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$dialog.message("Click OK to close dialog")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 1- Message Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Message dialog.</h6><h2>‘confirm’ Method</h2><p><strong>confirm(String message) : boolean</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Confirmation</strong> dialog. It will return the Boolean value ‘true’ when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or ‘false’ when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the dialog. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e588332-753e-421d-838c-29368f7edbbd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#if($dialog.confirm("Do you want to generate section A?"))
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 2- Confirmation Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Confirmation dialog.</h6><h2>‘input’ Method</h2><h3>Input dialog with text</h3><p><strong>input(String message) : String</strong>. This method will create an <strong>Input</strong> dialog from a message. It will return the input values as Strings when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or ‘null’ when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the dialog. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e8e5c24-a4eb-4a58-8b7e-e7600c0d1129"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($userText = $dialog.input("Enter your name"))
#if ($userText)
Your name is $userText
#else
Please specify your name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 3-Input Dialog with Text.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Input dialog with text.</h6><h3>Input dialog with text and initial value</h3><p><strong>input(String message, String initialValue)</strong> : String. This method will create a message and add an initial value in an <strong>Input</strong> dialog. It will return the input values as Strings when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or ‘null’ when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac29d2bc-4673-44ee-a29b-c0e2e2c305f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($userText = $dialog.input("Enter a date", "Sep28,2016"))
Date is $userText]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 4- The Input Dialog with Text and an Initial Value.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Input dialog with text and an initial value.</h6><h3>Input dialog with text and initial value Array</h3><p><strong>input(String message, Collection options)</strong> : String. This method will create a message and add initial value arrays in an <strong>Input</strong> dialog. It will return the input values as Strings when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or ‘null’ when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f13fca1d-36c0-41e2-8943-88c619afc6a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($selectedOption = $dialog.input("Choose your favorite fruit", ["Apple", "Orange", "Banana"]))
Your favorite fruit is $selectedOption]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 5- Input Dialog with Text and Initial Value Array.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Input dialog with text and initial value arrays.</h6><h2>‘sort’ Method</h2><h3>Sort and Enable dialog</h3><p><strong>sort(Collection list) : Collection</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from collections. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64ff03e1-5242-4f63-867c-a137bab037fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $dialog.sort($Diagram))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 6- Sort and Enable Dialog..png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Sort and Enable dialog.</h6><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with text</h3><p><strong>sort(String message, Collection list)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. </p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09fb2a92-95c1-4689-aba8-e1bcf52457b6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $dialog.sort("Rearrange diagram for presentation report",$Diagram))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 7- Sort and Enable Dialog with Text..png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Sort and Enable dialog with text.</h6><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with a default element selection option</h3><p><strong>sort(Collection list, Boolean defaultEnabled)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with a dialog title</h3><p><strong>sort(Collection list, String title, defaultEnabled : Boolean)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with a dialog title. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description</h3><p><strong>sort(String message, Collection list)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with dialog description. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description and a default element selection option</h3><p><strong>sort(String message, Collection list, Boolean defaultEnabled)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with dialog description and a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description, a dialog title, and a default element selection option</h3><p><strong>sort(String message, Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultEnabled)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with dialog description, a dialog title, and a default element selection option. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><h3>Sort and Enable dialog with dialog description, a dialog title, a default element selection option, and an option for hiding element icons</h3><p><strong>sort(String message, Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultEnabled, Boolean showElementIcon)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Sort and Enable</strong> dialog from text and collections with dialog description, a dialog title, a default element selection option, and an option for hiding element icons. It will return a collection of the newly sorted results when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or return the original collection when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p><br /></p><h2>'select' Method</h2><h3>Default Selection dialog</h3><p><strong>select(Collection list) : Collection</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Selection</strong> dialog from a collection. Items in the collection will be listed in the <strong>Selection</strong> dialog. You can select the items and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to return a collection of the selected items or null when you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7e72fea-9047-4630-89f9-318454f58595"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($class in $dialog.select($Diagram))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select Dialog 1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Selection dialog.</h6><h3>Selection dialog with a title</h3><p><strong>select(Collection list, String title) : Collection</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Selection</strong> dialog with a specified dialog title. The default <strong>Selection</strong> dialog title is <strong>Selection</strong>. You can select the items and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>.</p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aea59cd0-54b8-4b8a-bd4f-9a742dbdef27"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $dialog.select($Diagram, "Selection Dialog"))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select Dialog 2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Selection dialog with title.</h6><h3>Selection dialog without selection</h3><p><strong>select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected) : Collection</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Selection</strong> dialog from the collection without selecting the items in the dialog. You can specify whether all listed items will be selected or not. The default value for defaultSelected is true. Otherwise, all listed items will not be selected. You can select the items and click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef295ca9-3dd5-4f57-8a06-269337e8a62b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $dialog.select($Diagram, "Selection unchecked Dialog", false))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select Unchecked.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> The Selection dialog without selection.</h6><h3>Selection dialog with description text</h3><p><strong>select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected, String dialogText) : Collection</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Selection</strong> dialog from the collection. You can insert description text in the dialog to show the dialog behavior or more details of the listed items. The default value for the dialogText String is null. You can select the items and click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p>The code example is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="283f7f60-238c-446a-ac2f-04dce126994d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $dialog.select($Diagram,“Select all feature”, false, "Please select your required element"))
$diagram.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The output will be as follows:</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select All.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Selection dialog with text.</h6><h3>Selection dialog with an option for hiding element icons</h3><p><strong>select(Collection list, String title, Boolean defaultSelected, String dialogText, Boolean showElementIcon)</strong>. This method will create a <strong>Selection</strong> dialog from the collection. You can insert description text in the dialog to show the dialog behavior or more details of the listed items. The default value for the dialogText String is null. You can select the items and click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> to return a collection of the selected items or null if you click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dialog tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. With this method, you can also hide element icons in the dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709307 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00457: Disabling inbound network activity

- page_id: `136709307`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709307/Disabling+inbound+network+activity
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-26T15:35:08.737+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > MagicDraw getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Useful information

1427255413

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Useful information

1427255415

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Useful information

1427255414

#### EXCERPT: Useful information

INLINE

Note that after disabling inbound network activity, you will not be able to open the modeling tool Help or submit an issue.

#### TIP: Useful information

Useful information

If you disable inbound network activity, double-clicking a project file (.mdzip) opens the project in a new modeling tool window.

To disable inbound network activity

1. Go to <modeling tool installation directory>\bin and open the properties file, e.g., magicdraw.properties .
2. In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following argument:
3. Save and close the file.

1427255411

ac:name="id">1427255415</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2faac660-d733-41f4-80f0-d04330db2ce9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427255414</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="095cf619-6ac8-49d6-a47c-4b0140bd0e2b"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Note that after disabling inbound network activity, you will not be able to open the modeling tool Help or submit an issue.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4602c53-adae-4ca0-9514-aa04dc3c211f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Useful information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you disable inbound network activity, double-clicking a project file (.mdzip) opens the project in a new modeling tool window.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To disable inbound network activity</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin</em> and open the properties file, e.g., <em>magicdraw.properties</em>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following argument:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7702a6ab-72a0-4154-bcc8-c64d588df4f1"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-DDISABLEINBOUNDCON=true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save and close the file.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f2c776d6-884e-4846-8bdd-114d9f901e28"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427255411</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711954 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00458: Displaying and navigating through changes

- page_id: `136711954`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711954/Displaying+and+navigating+through+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:02.417+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Merge > Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['displaying-changes', 'merged-results-navigation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1421694493

1421694496

1421694494

You can customize the content of the **Merged Result** tree/list as well as navigate through changes in both the **Merged Results** tree/list and the **Specification** panel using the toolbar that is located at the top of the **Merge** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The toolbar buttons for customizing the **Merged** **Results** tree/list and navigating through changes are described in the following tables.

| Button / Shortcut keys | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Accept the selected change and go to the next change | Click to accept the selected element change and go to the next change (might be a property or the element). |
|  | Accept the selected change, its property changes and go to next change | Click to accept the selected element change together with the element property changes and go to the next changed element. |
|  | Accept the selected change, its property changes, all subelement changes and go to next change | Click to accept all changes from both contributors starting from the selected element and go to the next changed element (which is not a subelement). For example, if the root package Model selected, then all changes for the whole project will be accepted. |
|  | Reject the selected change and go to the next change | Click to reject the selected element change and go to the next change (might be a property or the element). |
|  | Reject the selected change, its property changes and go to next change | Click to reject the selected element change together with the element property changes and go to the next changed element. |
|  | Reject the selected change, its property changes, all subelement changes and go to next change | Click to reject all changes from both contributors starting from the selected element and go to the next changed element (which is not a subelement). For example, if the root package Model is selected, then all changes for the whole project will be rejected. |
| Alt + Home | Go To First Change | Click to select the first change.The button is not available if the first change is already selected. |
| Alt + Up arrow | Go To Previous Change | Click to select the previous change. |
| Alt + Down arrow | Go To Next Change | Click to select the next change. |
| Alt + End | Go To Last Change | Click to select the last change. The button is not available if the last change is already selected. |
| Alt + Page Down | Go To Next Conflict | Click to select the next conflict. The button is not available if there are no conflicts. |
|  | Annotate Merged Diagram | Click to turn on showing merged diagram annotations. |
|  | Report | Click to generate a Project Merge differences report in Microsoft Word format. |
| Options menu |  |  |
| Icon | Option | Description |
| > | Filter | Click to open the Select Element Type dialog. Clear the checkbox next to an element that you want to hide in the Merged Result tree/list. |
| > | Show Elements from Used projects | Click to show or hide elements from used projects in the Merged Result tree/list. |
| Show Source | Click to show or hide Source column in the Specification panel. By default, this column is shown. |  |
| Show Ancestor | Click to show or hide Ancestor column in the Specification panel. By default, this column is hidden. |  |
| Show Target | Click to show or hide Target column in the Specification panel. By default, this column is shown. |  |
| Show Merge Result Preview | Click to show or hide Show Merge Result Preview column in the Specification panel. This column shows final changes in the merged project and by default, this column is shown. |  |
| Summary/Legend | Click to show or hide the Summary/Legend panel. |  |
| Reset Windows Configuration | Select to place all tabs and windows to their default positions. |  |

1421694492

This column shows final changes in the merged project and by default, this column is shown.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Summary/Legend</span></td><td colspan="1">Click to show or hide the <strong>Summary/Legend</strong> panel.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Reset Windows <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Configuration</span></p></td><td colspan="1"><p><span style="color: their default positions.</span></p></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712763 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00459: Displaying and navigating through differences

- page_id: `136712763`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712763/Displaying+and+navigating+through+differences
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:51.059+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Comparing projects > Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['displaying-changes', 'comparison-result-navigation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize the content of the **Differences** tree as well as navigate through differences in both the **Difference**tree and the **Specification** panel using the toolbar that is located at the top of the **Difference Viewer**window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The toolbar buttons for customizing the **Differences** tree and navigating through differences are described in the following tables.

| Button / Shortcut keys | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Expand | Click to expand all nodes in the Differences tree. |
|  | Collapse | Click to collapse all nodes in the Differences tree. |
| Alt+Home | Go To First Change | Click to select the first change. The button is not available, if the first change is already selected. |
| Alt+ up arrow | Go To Previous Change | Click to select the previous change. |
| Alt + down arrow | Go To Next Change | Click to select the next change. |
| Alt+End | Go To Last Change | Click to select the last change. The button is not available, if the last change is already selected. |
|  | Report | Click to generate a project comparison differences report in Microsoft Word format. |
| > | Filter | Click to open the Items Filter dialog. Clear the check-box next to an element that you need to hide in the Differences tree. |
| > | Reset Windows Configuration | Click to place all tabs and windows to their default positions. |

You can also filter the elements displayed in the **Differences** tree by change type. Use the **Display** drop-down list to specify a change type. Be advised that filtering elements by a particular change type displays both elements with the particular changes and elements with the particular owned element changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710673 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00460: Displaying and suppressing compartments on shapes

- page_id: `136710673`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710673/Displaying+and+suppressing+compartments+on+shapes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:52.768+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Compartments
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To display or suppress compartments, do one of the following

- On the diagram, click the shape and click the *Compartments* smart manipulator. Then select or cancel the selection of the particular compartment, for example, Attributes, Operations. The Compartments smart manipulator exists on shapes that have compartment boxes. For example, pins, and initial nodes do not have the Compartments smart manipulator.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- On the diagram, select a shape and click the Suppress <compartment name> smart manipulator. [ATTACHMENT filename='supress_attributes.png']
- On the diagram, right-click the shape and select Symbol Properties . Then select or cancel the selection of the Suppress <compartment name> check box.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To display or suppress compartments, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li><p>On the diagram, click the shape and click the <em>Compartments</em> smart manipulator. Then select or cancel the selection of the particular compartment, for example, Attributes, Operations.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9656a37f-5122-411f-9ed7-4b5acae41ad2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Compartments smart manipulator exists on shapes that have compartment boxes. For example, pins, and initial nodes do not have the Compartments smart manipulator.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compartment_smart_manipulator.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying and suppressing compartments on shapes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>On the diagram, select a shape and click the <em>Suppress &lt;compartment name&gt;</em> smart manipulator. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="supress_attributes.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying and suppressing compartments on shapes" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>On the diagram, right-click the shape and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>. Then select or cancel the selection of the <strong>Suppress </strong>&lt;compartment name&gt; check box.</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712954 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00461: Displaying dependency details

- page_id: `136712954`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712954/Displaying+dependency+details
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:10.070+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Model Visualizer > Package Dependency Diagram Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

637548468

637548470

637548469

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package diagram' space='MD190'], the virtual dependencies (dependency links) are created. All virtual dependencies are stored in the **Virtual dependencies** package, which you can find in the model browser.

You can use the **Show Dependency Details** command to see the dependencies of a particular package. Dependency results are displayed in the dependency details panel (see the following example).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display the dependency details

1. In the Model Browser or on the diagram pane, right-click the dependency with the details you want to display.
2. In the shortcut menu, select Related Elements > Show Dependency Details . The dependency details panel opens.

637548467

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package diagram' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a5a1a12c-b458-495c-af4e-26afa8b4cf54"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">637548468</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1628e6f6-9b5f-4f4e-892d-81bd055313d7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">637548470</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="191e2aa5-76bd-485a-8db1-a75512a9e7c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">637548469</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD190" ri:content-title="Package diagram" /></ac:link>, the virtual dependencies (dependency links) are created. All virtual dependencies are stored in the <strong>Virtual dependencies</strong> package, which you can find in the model browser.</p><p>You can use the <strong>Show Dependency Details</strong> command to see the dependencies of a particular package. Dependency results are displayed in the dependency details panel (see the following example).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Package_diagram_dep.details.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying dependency details" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>To display the dependency details</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser or on the diagram pane, right-click the dependency with the details you want to display.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, select <strong>Related Elements</strong> &gt; <strong>Show Dependency Details</strong>. The dependency details panel opens.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe535a32-a1d5-49ac-99f9-f22b8fde4159"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">637548467</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dependency" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Package diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709963 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00462: Displaying element numbering

- page_id: `136709963`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709963/Displaying+element+numbering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:18.390+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-element-numbering']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To show element numbering

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button.png'] in the Diagram toolbar.
2. Select the Show Element Numbers option.

The element numbering appears before its name.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To show element numbering</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Options" ac:alt="Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Diagram toolbar.</li><li>Select the <strong>Show Element Numbers </strong>option.</li></ol><p>The element numbering appears before its name.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Element numbering in the relation map" ac:alt="Element numbering in the relation map"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_element_numbering.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying element numbering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710454 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00463: Displaying elements

- page_id: `136710454`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710454/Displaying+elements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:55:53.137+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

210053124

210053139

210053129

MagicDraw includes a number of display commands that allow you to represent elements in different diagrams. However, displaying elements is only possible if they are already created somewhere in the model.The display commandsare interactive, and depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. The table below lists all of the commands which are available, and a short description of each. The table also provides a link to a more in depth description of its functionality, and lists the diagrams in which they can be used.

| Command names | Description | Usage in diagrams |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Display Related Elements | Opens the Display Related Elements dialog in which you can specify the relation type(s), their direction, scope, depth, and other options. All specified criteria define which elements and relations will be displayed on the diagram pane of the selected element. ]]> | All diagrams |
| Display Paths | Displays paths among shapes that already exist in the model. ]]> | All diagrams |
| Display Internal Structure | Displays internal structure inside a selected classifier shape that contains its own internal structure. ]]> | All diagrams |
| Display Parts/Ports | Opens the Display Parts/Ports dialog from where you can specify the exact properties and/or ports you want to display on the diagram pane. Read more > | Class, Composite Structure, Component, Package, Deployment, Object diagrams |
| Hide All Ports | Hides all ports of the selected shape(s) or diagram frame. ]]> | Class, Composite Structure, Component, Package, Deployment, Object diagrams |
| Display Inner Elements | Displays internal elements and paths inside selected shape or diagram frame. There are different procedures when trying to display inner elements in:The Package diagram. ]]>The Activity diagram. ]]>The State Machine diagram. ]]> | All diagrams |
| Display Provided/Required Interfaces | Displays provided or required interfaces of the selected port shape. ]]> | Class, Composite Structure, Component, Package, Deployment, Object diagrams |
| Display Pins | Opens the Select Pins dialog from where you can select the exact pins you want to display on the selected shape. ]]> | Activity diagram |
| Display Parameter Nodes | Opens the Select Parameters dialog from where you can select exactly which Activity Parameter Nodes you want to display on the frame of the Activity Diagram. ]]> | Activity diagram |
| Display Expansion Nodes | Displays the input or output Expansion Nodes on the Expansion Region border. ]]> | Activity diagram |
| Display Points | Opens the Select Points dialog in which you can select which Connection Point References you want to display on a submachine State shape. ]]> | State Machine diagram |
| Display Lifelines | Opens the Display Lifelines dialog from where you can select which lifelines you want to display. ]]> | Sequence diagramCommunication diagram |
| Display Messages | Opens the Select Messages dialog from where you can select which messages of the selected connector you want to display. ]]> | Communication diagram |

210053122

**Related pages**

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07466949-f83a-4556-a738-575f65c14623"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">210053124</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c56382d-eb61-4bbc-8831-8ed90904ebbe"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">210053139</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="573a070d-87ca-464c-910e-6daee7a355bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">210053129</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>MagicDraw includes a number of display commands that allow you to represent elements in different diagrams. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Read more >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td><td colspan="1"><span There are different procedures when trying to display inner elements in:</p><ul><li>The Package diagram. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Working with packages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Read more >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>The Activity diagram. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Activity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Read more >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>The State Machine diagram. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Working with State Machine diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Read more >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></td><td colspan="1">All diagrams</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Display <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711655 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00464: Displaying icon or image

- page_id: `136711655`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711655/Displaying+icon+or+image
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:48.812+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display icon or image on the symbol or instead of the symbol.

The descriptions given in the following table will help you to understand this functionality better.

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Stereotype | Every element can be extended by applying a . For more information about applying stereotype properties, see . |
| Icon | Icon of stereotype.Icon is a small image displayed in the top-right corner of shape.To assign an icon to a stereotype in the window, define the Icon property. |
| Text | Stereotype name, displayed on the symbol. |
| Image | Image which can be assigned to element and displayed as icon or instead of element shape. To assign image to element in the element , assign the Image property. |

To change the icon visibility mode on the element shape, do one of the following

- Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x']**Symbol Properties** dialog and in the Show Stereotypes property specification cell, select the icon visibility mode.
- Select an element shape and click the Compartments button. From the menu, select Stereotypes and choose the icon visibility mode.

The stereotype visibility modes are described in the following table:

| Mode | Example | Icon and name visibility | Image visibility |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Icon and Text |  | Icon of stereotype displayed.Name of stereotype displayed. | Image of element is displayed in the corner of shape. |
| Icon |  | Icon of stereotype displayed in the corner of symbol.Name of stereotype is not displayed. | Image of element is displayed in the corner of shape. |
| Text Only |  | Icon of stereotype is not displayed.Name of stereotype is displayed. | Image of element is not displayed. |
| Shape Image and Text* |  | Icon of stereotype is displayed instead of shape.Name of stereotype is displayed. | Image of element is displayed instead of shape. |
| Shape Image* |  | Icon of stereotype is displayed instead of shape.Name of stereotype is not displayed. | Image of element is displayed instead of the shape. |
| Do Not Display |  | Icon of stereotype is not displayed.Name of stereotype is not displayed. | Image of element is not displayed. |

* - When the **Shape Image and Text** or **Shape Image** property is selected, all symbol compartmentsshould be suppressed in orderto display the icon of a stereotype instead of the element symbol. If the symbol contains port or pins, these ports or pins are displayed around that symbol.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Shape Image and Text** and **Shape Image** properties are not added to the **Path** element properties list.

If element has assigned both - image and stereotype icon - then image of element will be displayed on the shape.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710473 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00465: Displaying internal structure on structured classifiers

- page_id: `136710473`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710473/Displaying+internal+structure+on+structured+classifiers
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:40.472+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Displaying elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

567827949

567827951

567827950

You can represent the internal structure of the structured classifier on the diagram pane in all diagrams. The internal structure of the selected shape can be represented only if the selected element contains its own internal structure. For example, in a Composite Structure diagram on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MD2024x'] shape or in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x'] on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x'] shape, you can display its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x'], parts, and connectors.

The following procedure will show you how to display the internal structure of a selected classifier.

To display the internal structure of a selected classifier

1. On the diagram pane, select the classifier shape in which you want to display the internal structure.
2. Do one of the following: 
- Open the selected shape's shortcut menu, click **Display** > **Display Internal Structure**, select the name of the classifier, and then select a diagram containing the internal structure. 
- On the diagram toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], choose the **Display Internal Structure** command, select the name of the classifier, and then select a diagram containing the internal structure. After those steps are completed, the internal structure is displayed inside the selected classifier (see figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

567827948

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements' space='MD2024x']

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Composite structure diagram** sample model that comes with MagicDraw. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='composite structure diagram.mdzip'] .
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The internal structure of the selected shape can be represented only if the selected element contains its own internal structure. For example, in a Composite Structure diagram on a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Part" /></ac:link> shape or in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link> on a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> shape, you can display its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, parts, and connectors.</p><p>The following procedure will show you how to display the internal structure of a selected classifier.</p><p>To display the internal structure of a selected classifier</p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram pane, select the classifier shape in which you want to display the internal structure.</li><li><p>Do one of the following:<br />- Open the selected shape's shortcut menu, click <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Internal Structure</strong>, select the name of the classifier, and then select a diagram containing the internal structure.<br />- On the diagram toolbar click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying internal structure on structured classifiers" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, choose the <strong>Display Internal Structure</strong> command, select the name of the classifier, and then select a diagram containing the internal structure.</p><p>After those steps are completed, the internal structure is displayed inside the selected classifier (see figure below).</p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="The Room Class owns an internal structure which is displayed on its shape."><ri:attachment ri:filename="class_with_internal_structure.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying internal structure on structured classifiers" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b4a391a9-5d52-4465-8c49-798c3919ae7a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567827948</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying elements" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Composite structure diagram</strong> sample model that comes with MagicDraw. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="composite structure diagram.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.</li><li>Find in modeling tool <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.</em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
