<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710676 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00466: Displaying or hiding elements in compartments on shapes

- page_id: `136710676`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710676/Displaying+or+hiding+elements+in+compartments+on+shapes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:53.017+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Compartments
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the **Compartment Edit**dialog to control the list of elements and properties visible in compartments on shapes. You can display or hide stereotypes, constraints, tagged values, attributes, operations, and others. If there are hidden properties on the boxable compartment, the **...** is displayed.

You can also display or hide the element specification properties, such as *Name*, *Is Active*, *Owner*, *To Do* in the **Compartment Edit**dialog under the **Element Properties** tab.

To open the **Compartment Edit** dialog from the diagram pane

- Select a shape and click the Compartments smart manipulator. On the menu, click the arrow to expand and then select Edit Compartments .
- Right-click a single or any number of shapes or classifier compartments (e.g. Properties, Operations, Receptions, Ports, and etc.), and from the shortcut menu, select **Edit Compartments**. Compartment Edit dialog of multiple shapesWhen you edit compartments of more than one shape at once, the**Compartment Edit** dialog works with the following rules:- If you select multiple shapes of the same element, the **Compartment Edit** dialog is the same as for a single shape, but changes are applied for all selected shapes after clicking **OK**. 
 - If you select multiple shapes of different elements, the **Compartment Edit** dialog shows a union of compartments and their items. 
 - If you select multiple shapes, but at least one of them hides the common compartment item while another shows it, the **Compartment Edit** dialog displays that item in italics in the **Element Properties** tab > Selected area. If you move the conflicting item to the Hidden area, it becomes non-italicized, and changes are applied for both shapes after clicking **OK**.
- On the Note shape, click the Edit Element Properties smart manipulator.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Use the <strong>Compartment Edit </strong>dialog to control the list of elements and properties visible in compartments on shapes. You can display or hide stereotypes, constraints, tagged values, attributes, operations, and others. If there are hidden properties on the boxable compartment, the <strong>...</strong> is displayed.</p><p>You can also display or hide the element specification properties, such as <em>Name</em>, <em>Is Active</em>, <em>Owner</em>, <em>To Do</em> in the <strong>Compartment Edit </strong>dialog under the <strong>Element Properties</strong> tab.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog from the diagram pane</p><hr /><ul><li>Select a shape and click the <em>Compartments</em> smart manipulator. On the menu, click the arrow to expand and then select <strong>Edit Compartments</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click a single or any number of shapes or classifier compartments (e.g. Properties, Operations, Receptions, Ports, and etc.), and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Compartments</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8be6e385-af39-4175-a17e-56adb613602a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Compartment Edit dialog of multiple shapes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you edit compartments of more than one shape at once, the<strong> Compartment Edit</strong> dialog works with the following rules:</p><p> - If you select multiple shapes of the same element, the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog is the same as for a single shape, but changes are applied for all selected shapes after clicking <strong>OK</strong>.<br /> - If you select multiple shapes of different elements, the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog shows a union of compartments and their items.<br /> - If you select multiple shapes, but at least one of them hides the common compartment item while another shows it, the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog displays that item in italics in the <strong>Element Properties</strong> tab &gt; Selected area. If you move the conflicting item to the Hidden area, it becomes non-italicized, and changes are applied for both shapes after clicking <strong>OK</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>On the Note shape, click the <em>Edit Element Properties</em> smart manipulator.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710455 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00467: Displaying parts and ports

- page_id: `136710455`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710455/Displaying+parts+and+ports
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:39.873+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Displaying elements
- labels: ['display-parts', 'display-ports', 'hide-ports']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example 3

567711113

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example 3

567711119

210127422

**On this page**

****

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example 3

567711117

##### Introduction

You can display ports, properties, and deep nested properties that have already been created in all [ATTACHMENT filename='uml_diagrams.png'].This functionality is driven by stereotypes, which allow you to display properties on which [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x'] have been applied.If you have applied your own [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x'] for the elements (e.g., electrical, mechanical, optical), you can use those stereotypeswhen selecting the properties you want to display on the diagram pane.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

For more information about how to create custom stereotypes and apply them, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML Profiling and DSL Guide' space='MD2024x'].

##### Ways to display parts and ports

You can display ports and properties in the following ways:

44

###### Using the Display Parts/Ports dialog

You can use the**Display Parts/Ports**dialog to control how much of a structure should be displayed. The **Display Parts/Ports**dialog****opens automatically when creating a new[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024x'] for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x'] element that owns properties. It can also be accessed and opened in thestructurediagrams that have already been created.The**Display Parts/Ports** dialog allows you to:

- Preview all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MD2024x'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x'] , and other properties in one place.
- Manually or automatically select required [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MD2024x'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x'] , and other properties you want to display.
- Manually or automatically choose deeply [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Nesting parts that are association ends' space='MD2024x'] [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MD2024x'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x'] , and other properties you want to display.
- Define layout options for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MD2024x'] .
- Specify layout options for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x'] .

The **Display Parts/Ports**dialog consists of the following areas:

- *Dialog toolbar* allows you to show or hide additional information about listed elements.
- *Element tree* displays all owned internal structure of context [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x'].
- *Panel* is a context-sensitive area thatdepends on the selected property from the Element tree. It allows you to batch select [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MD2024x']/[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x']Portsin the Element tree. You canselect all, clear all, or choose only properties by type, stereotype, or aspect.
- *Options* area allows you to choose the layout of the selected parts and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x']when they appear in the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

The following procedure will guide you in working with the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog when you want to display required properties on the diagram pane.

To display Ports and Properties using the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog

1. Open the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog:
  - When creating a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024x'] , the dialog opens automatically.
  - When editing an already created [ATTACHMENT filename='uml_diagrams.png']structure diagram , the dialog can be opened in the following ways: - Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and, o n the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and choose **Display Parts/Ports**. - Right-click either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and select **Display** > **Display Parts/Ports**.
2. (Optional) Enable buttons from the Display Parts/Ports dialog toolbar if you want to show or hide additional information about listed elements:
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the full path of elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the full types of elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the inherited elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the inherited properties.
3. Choose the properties you want to display by using one or more of the following methods: WarningIf some check boxesare inactive in the element list,those elements are already displayed in the diagram.
  - Use the element treelocated on the left side of the dialog:-Manually select individual check boxesone by one. 
- Select recursively nested elements by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box for the element. Example 1All properties (*socket1, socket2, socket3, powerIn*) of the *Ee* Property are selected recursively by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box from the element treein the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Use the context-sensitive panellocated on the right side of the dialog:- **Select Properties** group - displays all property types (including your own stereotypes) that are owned by the selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. 
- **Select Ports** group - displays all Port types (including your own stereotypes) that are owned by a selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.- **Select All** - selects all check boxes that are expanded from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. 
- **Clear All** - clears all check boxes, even those that are collapsed (deep nested), from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. Example 2All ports (*socket1, socket2, socket3, powerIn*) of the *Ee* Property are selected in the element tree by using the **Select Ports** group in the context-sensitive panelof the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Specify the layout options in the Options area:
  - Parts layout: select the Layout Parts check box if you want the Part shapes to be arranged in the automatic layout.
  - Usage of layout template: select the Use Layout Template check box if you want the layout template to be applied. When this check box is selected, the Part, which is included in the layout template, and all its Ports from the layout template are selected automatically.
  - Showing connectors: select the Show Connectors check box if you want the connectors to be displayed.
  - Ports layout on Parts shape: - Top/Bottom - displays Ports on the Part shape from top to bottom. - Left/Right - displays Ports on the Part shape from left to right.
5. Click **OK**.

After those steps, all parts, ports, and other properties that were selected in the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog are displayed on the diagram pane.

###### Using the diagram toolbar and the shortcut menu

In the structure diagrams that have already been created, you can display [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties' space='MD2024x'], and hide all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x'] by using the commands from:

- The Display button on the diagram toolbar.
- The **Display** command group under the shortcut menu.

You can also use the**Display** button and **Display** command group from the shortcut menu as their functions are identical. All those commandsare interactive and depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. For example, if the Part shape is selected on the diagram pane, both menu contains onlythe commands that can be applied to the selected Part shape. If you select nothing in the diagram pane, both the menu showscommands that can be applied to the whole diagram.

These commands under the **Display** button and shortcut menu are comprised ofelements that are still not displayed on the diagram according to their:

- Metatypes.
- Stereotypes.
- Types stereotypes.

The following procedures will show you how to:

- How to display Ports, Properties using the **Display** button.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x']

**button**To display Ports, Properties using the **Display** button

1. Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes.
2. O n the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='display_related_elements_button_MD.png']
3. From the opened command menu, select the required properties to display: 
 - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the **Display Ports** command group. ImportantIf nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the**Display Ports**command group will contain commands that allow you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts. - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the **Display Properties** command group. After selecting the required command from the **Display** button, the appropriate properties are displayed on the diagram pane.

**shortcut**To display Ports, Properties using the shortcut menu

1. Right-click either the diagram pane or single/multiple element shapes.
2. From the shortcut menu, select the Display command group. [ATTACHMENT filename='display_command_group_MD.png']
3. From the opened command menu, select the required properties to display: 
 - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the **Display Ports** command group. ImportantIf nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the **Display Ports** command group allows you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts. - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the **Display Properties** command group. 
After selecting the required command from the Display command menu, the appropriate properties, Ports are displayed on the diagram pane.

**hide**To hide Ports

- Select either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and, o n the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and choose**Hide All Ports**.
- Right-click either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and select **Display** >**Hide All Ports**. 
After selecting the command, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x']of the selected shape will be hidden from the diagram pane but not removed from the model. WarningIf the **Hide All Ports** command doesn't appear, the selection or diagramdoesn't contain any [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x']that can be hidden. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the smart manipulator toolbar

When the properties are already displayed on the[ATTACHMENT filename='uml_diagrams.png']structure diagrams, you can display their direct ports using the **Display All Ports** button from the smart manipulator toolbar. It does not allow you to display deeply nested ports, only first-level ports. To display deeply nested ports, you should use the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog. Follow the steps below to learn how to display ports of a selected property shape.

To display Ports on shapes

1. Select the required element shape or a diagram frame.
2. From the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
All first-level Ports of the selected shape will be displayed on the diagram pane. WarningIf the **Display All Ports** button is not available, the selected element or diagramdoesn't own any Port. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the drag-and-drop operationin the Composite Structure diagram

The most efficient way to create a new property and display it with all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x'] in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024x'] is by dragging the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x'] or Property elements. This method does not allow you to display deeply nested properties, only first-level properties. To display deeply nested elements, use the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog. The following procedures will show you:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x']
- How to display a Property shape and all its Ports by dragging the Property element.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x']

drag_classYou can create a new Property by dragging the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x']element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x']Model Browserto the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024x'] pane. The newly created Property will be typed by the dragged [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x'] and displayed on the diagram pane. If this [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MD2024x'] has [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x'] defined, you can display them by using the long drag and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 3).

****

To create and display a Property by dragging a Class element

- Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.
- Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag).Drop it, and from the opened menu, select the **Create Property Symbol**command. 
The new Property is created in the model and displayed on the diagram pane. The newly created Property is typed by the dragged Class automatically.

To create and display a Property and display all its Ports by dragging a Class element

- Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag).
- Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the **Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports** command. The new Property is created in the model and displayed with all its Ports on the diagram pane. The newly created Property is typed by the dragged Class automatically.

#### INFO: Tip

Tip

After creating an element, it is beneficial to find its location in the model tree by performing one of the following: 
 - Right-click the shape, and, from the shortcut menu, choose the **Select in Containment Tree** command. 
 - Select the shape and press Alt+B.

#### TIP: Example 3

Example 3

After selecting the **Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports** command anddragging (long drag) the Class element to *Internal Structure of Workplace* diagram pane, itcreates a new Property typed by a*Duplex Power Outlet*Class and displays all Ports on it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

drag_propertyYou can display a Property shape by dragging a Property element from the Model Browser to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024x'] pane. If this property has [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x'] defined, you can display them by using the long drag and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 4).

The following procedures describe how to display Property shape and all its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space='MD2024x'] by dragging the Property element.

To display a Property by dragging a Property element

- Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.
- Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag) . Drop it, and from the opened menu, select the**Create Property Symbol**command. The Property shape is displayed on the diagram pane.

To display a Property with all of its Ports by dragging a Property element

- Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag).
- Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the**Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports** command. The Property shape is displayed with all its Ports on that shape.

After selecting the**Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports**command, and dragging (long drag) the Property element to*Internal Structure of Workplace*diagram, it displays Property shape and all Ports on it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After completing the steps outlined above, the Property shape and/or all its Ports are displayed on the Composite Structure diagram.

##### Displaying properties of a Port

If you display properties of a Port, all properties of its type will be displayed inside the Port shape and part compartments will be enabled for that Port.

To enable Part compartments on the Port shape

1. Select a Port shape whose type has properties.
2. Do one of the following:
  - O n the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] .
  - From the shortcut menu, point to the Display command group.
3. Select the required properties to display.

The properties are displayed inside the Port shape. Part compartments are enabled for the Port.

The example below shows the *p1* Port both before and after displaying its type properties. The properties displayed on the Port shape are the *Pressure Status* Flow Property and the *suspensionSensor* Part Property, which are the properties of the selected Port type *iSuspension* Block. After the display of properties has been selected, compartments are automatically enabled for the *p1* Port.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Sample model**

The models used in the figures of this page are:

- The Composite structure diagram sample model that comes with MagicDraw. To open this sample do one of the following: download *[ATTACHMENT filename='composite structure diagram.mdzip']*or f ind in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.
- The Vehicle parts under ports sample model which is available only with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP185'] . To open this sample d *ownload [ATTACHMENT filename='Vehicle parts under ports.mdzip'].*

rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><ul><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display the full path of the elements"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_full_path_of_the_elements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> to show the full path of elements.<span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /></span></span></li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display the qualified name of type in the typed elements text"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_qualified_name_of_type_in_the typed_elements_text.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> to show the full types of elements.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_inherited_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> to show the inherited elements.<span class="confluence-link"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Private Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show_private_inherited.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> to show the inherited properties.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li></ul></li><li><p>Choose the properties you want to display by using one or more of the following methods:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ebed955-61f3-499b-9133-06607c2df4c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>If some <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>check boxes</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span> are inactive in the element list, </span></span></span>those elements are already <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>displayed</span></span></span> in the diagram.<br /></span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use the element tree<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> located on <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the left side of the dialog:</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">-</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Manually select individual check boxes<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">one by one.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><br />- Select recursively nested elements by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box for the element.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /></span></span></span></span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="927433d1-8a3b-4ca2-ad2e-d567bde15d2a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">All properties (<em>socket1, socket2, socket3, powerIn</em>) of the <em>Ee</em> Property are selected recursively <span>by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box</span> from the element tree</span></span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> in the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog.</span></span></p><ac:image ac:title="Recursively selected nested elements in the Display Parts/Ports dialog."><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursively_selected_nested_elements_MD.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use the context-sensitive panel<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> located on the right side of the dialog:</span></span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- <strong>Select Properties</strong> group - displays all property types <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">(including <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> your own</span></span></span> stereotypes)</span> that are owned by the selected element <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog</span>.<br />- <strong>Select Ports</strong> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">group - displays all Port types <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">(including your own stereotypes)</span> that are owned by a selected element <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog</span>.</span><br /></span>- <strong>Select All</strong> - selects all check boxes that are expanded <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.</span><br />- <strong>Clear All</strong> - clears all check boxes, even those that are collapsed (deep nested), <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree</span> located on <span>the left side of the dialog.<br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9a648a8-07dd-4136-9c82-c33cbd61bacb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">All ports (<em>socket1, socket2, socket3, powerIn</em>) of the <em>Ee</em> Property are selected <span>in <span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the element tree</span></span></span> by using the <span><strong>Select Ports</strong> <span>group</span></span> in the context-sensitive panel </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">of the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog.</span></p><ac:image ac:title="Using the context-sensitive panel of the Display Parts/Ports dialog when trying to select required properties."><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_ports_using_panel.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>Specify the layout options in the <strong>Options</strong> area:<br /><ul><li>Parts layout: select the <strong>Layout Parts</strong> check box if you want the Part shapes to be arranged in the automatic layout.</li><li>Usage of layout template: select the <strong>Use Layout Template</strong> check box if you want the layout template to be applied. When this check box is selected, the Part, which is included in the layout template, and all its Ports from the layout template are selected automatically.</li><li>Showing connectors: select the <strong>Show Connectors</strong> check box if you want the connectors to be displayed.</li><li>Ports layout on Parts shape:<br />- <strong>Top/Bottom</strong> - displays Ports on the Part shape from top to bottom.<br />- <strong>Left/Right </strong> - displays Ports on the Part shape from left to right.</li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After those steps, all parts, ports, and other properties that were selected in the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /></span></span></p><h4>Using the diagram toolbar and the shortcut menu</h4><p class="confluence-link">In the structure diagrams that have already been created, you can display <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Properties" /></ac:link>, and hide all <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> by using the commands from:</p><ul><li class="confluence-link">The <strong>Display</strong> button on the diagram toolbar.</li><li class="confluence-link">The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Display</strong> command group under the shortcut menu.</span></li></ul><p>You can also use <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>the </span><strong>Display</strong> button</span></span></span> and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Display</strong> command group from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the shortcut menu as</span></span> their functions are identical. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">All those commands</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>are interactive and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. For example, if the Part shape is selected on the diagram pane, both menu contains <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">only </span>the commands that can be applied to the selected <span class="confluence-link">Part</span> shape. If you select nothing in the diagram pane, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">both the menu</span> shows<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> commands that can be applied</span></span></span> to the whole diagram.</span></span></span></span></span></span></p><p>These <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">commands under the <strong>Display</strong> button and shortcut menu are <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">comprised of </span></span></span></span></span>elements that are still not displayed on the diagram according to their:</p><ul><li>Metatypes.</li><li>Stereotypes.</li><li>Types stereotypes.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">The following procedures will show you how to:</span></span></p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="button"><ac:link-body>How to display Ports, Properties using the <strong>Display</strong> button.</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="shortcut"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to display Ports, Properties using the shortcut menu.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="hide"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to hide Ports.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="151e0ef2-cf5f-42c8-9b7b-b70191dd1dcd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">button</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display Ports, Properties using the <strong>Display</strong> button</p><hr /><ol><li>Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li>O<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="After selecting the Internal Structure of Workplace diagram pane and clicking the Display button, opened menu allows to display Properties and/or Ports."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements_button_MD.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>From the opened command menu, select the required properties to display:<br />        - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ef07d2a-8928-47ac-9bee-363460e3bac9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7319cc0e-6a3c-4e44-9809-e9817c6a997b">If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7319cc0e-6a3c-4e44-9809-e9817c6a997b">Display Ports</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7319cc0e-6a3c-4e44-9809-e9817c6a997b"> command group will contain commands that allow you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.</p><p><span><span>After selecting the required command from the</span></span> <strong>Display</strong> button<span><span>, the appropriate properties are displayed on the diagram pane.</span></span></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f27456dd-5abb-464b-b57a-d14bee0cbd81"><ac:parameter ac:name="">shortcut</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display Ports, Properties using the shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click either the diagram pane or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select the <strong>Display</strong> command group.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="The Display command group menu allows to display Properties and Ports of the PC Part shape." ac:alt="The Display command group menu allows to display Properties and Ports of the PC Part shape."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_command_group_MD.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>From the opened command menu, select the required properties to display:<br />        - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="327c3ff9-f8cc-4d9e-a0b5-0c763e6c790e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group allows you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.<br />After selecting the required command from the Display command menu, the appropriate properties, Ports are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4794c2e6-db49-41b6-ac94-03bcb79efedc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">hide</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To hide Ports</p><hr /><ul><li>Select either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and, o<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>  and choose<strong> Hide All Ports</strong>.</span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Right-click either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt;<strong> Hide All Ports</strong>.<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After selecting the command, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of the selected shape will be hidden from the diagram pane but not removed from the model</span>.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff629db0-f28c-4b9f-aa61-48e542d03bba"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Hide All Ports</strong> command doesn't appear, the selection or <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram </span>doesn't contain any <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>that can be hidden.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:title="The Hide All Ports command under the Display command group from the shortcut menu of the PC Part shape. " ac:alt="The Hide All Ports command under the Display command group from the shortcut menu of the PC Part shape. "><ri:attachment ri:filename="hide_all_ports_shortcut_menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p></li></ul><h4>Using the smart manipulator toolbar<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>When the properties are already displayed on the<span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="uml_diagrams.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">structure diagram</span>s</ac:link-body></ac:link>, you can display their direct <span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">ports</span></span> using the <strong>Display All Ports</strong> button from the smart manipulator toolbar. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">It does not allow you to display deeply nested <span class="confluence-link">ports</span>, only <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">first-level</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> ports. To display deeply nested ports, you should use the <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog</span>.</span> Follow the steps below to learn how to display ports of a selected property shape.</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></p><p>To display Ports on shapes</p><hr /><ol><li>Select<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span> the required</span></span> element shape or a diagram frame.</span> </span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">From the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display All Ports" ac:alt="Display All Ports"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_ports.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>All <span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">first-level</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> Ports of <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the selected shape</span></span></span> will be displayed on the diagram pane.</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></span><br /></span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="feb6590f-3710-4154-9154-2c9ce8edfa7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> If the <strong>Display All Ports</strong> button is not available, the selected element or <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram </span>doesn't own any Port.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <ac:image ac:title="The Display All Ports button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the PC Part shape. " ac:alt="The Display All Ports button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the PC Part shape. "><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_ports_SM_MD.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /></span></span></span></p></li></ol><h4>Using the drag-and-drop operation<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> in the Composite Structure diagram</span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The most efficient way to create a new property and display it with all <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> is by dragging the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> or Property elements. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">This method does not allow you to display deeply nested properties, only <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">first-level</span></span></span> properties. To display deeply nested elements, use the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The following procedures will show you:</span></span></span></span></span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link ac:anchor="drag_class"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create a Property shape and how to display all its Ports when dragging the Class element.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link ac:anchor="drag_property"><ac:link-body>How to display a Property shape and all its <span class="confluence-link">Ports</span> by dragging the Property element.</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></span></span></span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9f47650-536d-46c6-8f68-c5881f221387"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag_class</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can create a new Property by dragging the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>element <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Model Browser</span> </ac:link-body></ac:link>to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> pane</span>. The newly created Property will be typed by the dragged <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> and displayed on the diagram pane. If this <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> has <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> defined, you can display them by using the long drag and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 3). <br /></span></span></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p>To create and display a Property by dragging a Class element</p><hr /><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane</span></span></span>, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">and hold it down <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>Drop it, and from the opened menu, select the <strong>Create Property Symbol </strong>command.</span></span></span><br />The new Property is created in the model and displayed on the diagram pane. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The newly created Property is typed by the dragged Class automatically.</span></span></span></span> </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p></li></ul><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d33f1ab5-07e0-42c6-8681-25e8e07df1d5">To create and display a Property and display all its Ports by dragging a Class element</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command.</span></span></span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The new Property is created in the model and displayed with all its Ports on the diagram pane. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The newly created Property is typed by the dragged Class automatically.</span></span></span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35a694c8-df84-455d-8e7f-eaa249205f99"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After creating an element, it is beneficial to find its location in the model tree by performing one of the following:<br />        - Right-click the shape, and, from the shortcut menu, choose the <strong>Select in Containment Tree</strong> command.<br />        - Select the shape and press Alt+B.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d407cdbc-1a05-485b-9d7d-bd7aec91f448"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 3</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After selecting the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command and </span></span>dragging (<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">long drag</span></span>) the Class element to <em>Internal Structure of Workplace</em> diagram pane, it </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">creates a new Property typed by a </span><em>Duplex Power Outlet</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Class and displays all Ports on it.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragging_class_on-diagram_pane.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="593bf531-1a65-4783-ac36-35a678036e74"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag_property</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can display a Property shape by dragging a Property element from the Model Browser to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> pane. If this property has <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> defined, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">you can display them by using the long drag and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 4).</span></span><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The following procedures describe how to display Property shape and all its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> by dragging the Property element.</span></p><p>To display a Property by dragging a Property element</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.</li><li>Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>. <span>Drop it, and from the opened menu, select the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Create Property Symbol </strong></span>command.</span><br />The Property shape is displayed on the diagram pane.<br /><br /></li></ul><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="21aea531-8fce-41e3-a048-c759c72d59e2">To display a Property with all of its Ports by dragging a Property element</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag). </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the<strong> Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Property shape is displayed with all its Ports on that shape.</span><br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00be8f45-9eb5-4f84-b3c5-e89d3731db2e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After selecting the </span><strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> command, and dragging (long drag) the Property element to</span><em> Internal Structure of Workplace</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> diagram, it displays Property shape and all Ports on it.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragging_property.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After completing the steps outlined above, the Property shape and/or all its Ports are displayed on the Composite Structure diagram.</span></p><h3>Displaying properties of a Port</h3><p>If you display properties of a Port, all properties of its type will be displayed inside the Port shape and part compartments will be enabled for that Port.</p><p>To enable Part compartments on the Port shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Port shape whose type has properties.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>O<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, point to the <strong>Display</strong> command group.</li></ul></li><li>Select the required properties to display.</li></ol><p>The properties are displayed inside the Port shape. Part compartments are enabled for the Port.</p><p>The example below shows the <em>p1</em> Port both before and after displaying its type properties. The properties displayed on the Port shape are the <em>Pressure Status</em> Flow Property and the <em>suspensionSensor</em> Part Property, which are the properties of the selected Port type <em>iSuspension</em> Block. After the display of properties has been selected, compartments are automatically enabled for the <em>p1</em> Port.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_compartments_on_port_shape.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b0ddcd7-92fb-4701-8469-1008c417c477"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567711109</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying elements" Stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Nesting parts that are association ends" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Mapping ports on a Part type change" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link>  </li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Component diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Package diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Deployment diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Object diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Object diagram ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The models used in the figures of this page are:</p><ul><li>The <strong>Composite structure diagram </strong>sample model that comes with MagicDraw. To open this sample do one of the following: download<span class="confluence-link"><em> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="composite structure diagram.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link> </em>or f</span>ind in modeling tool <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.</em></li><li>The <strong>Vehicle parts under ports</strong> sample model which is available only with <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP185" ri:content-title="SysML Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. To open this sample d<span class="confluence-link"><em>ownload <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Vehicle parts under ports.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.</em></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
