<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710789 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00468: Displaying parts and ports according to aspects

- page_id: `136710789`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710789/Displaying+parts+and+ports+according+to+aspects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:01.196+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

581472387

581472391

581472390

Before using this feature, make sure:

- The diagram with aspect is created. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram aspects' space='MD2024x']>]]>
- The part and/or port stereotype names matches the aspect name. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='How aspects match with parts and ports' space='MD2024x']>]]>

When displaying Parts/Ports in the aspect diagram, e.g., Electrical *Data Transfer System* Internal Block Diagram, you can display all the parts/ports of an appropriate aspect, e.g., Electrical, by using the **All By Aspect** button. After selecting this button, all parts or ports that match the diagram's aspect can be displayed. The figure below demonstrates the usage of this button in the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog when you are trying to display all electrical aspect ports of the selected *control Panel* part.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The All By Aspect button usage in the Display Parts/Ports dialog. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.

581472386

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Diagram aspects** sample models that comes with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024x']. To open this sample properly you need to install the SysML plugin in the MagicDraw and:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bcd8e89b-1f9f-408a-b6f6-affd9427300f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472387</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3df8ea34-f6f7-4cf8-a5ad-f360b21222b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472391</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4cee8920-b70e-421a-aeb8-05dcb0da208a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472390</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before using this feature, make sure:</p><ul><li>The diagram with aspect is created. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Diagram aspects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[The main workflow of using aspects >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>The part and/or port stereotype names matches the aspect name. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="How aspects match with parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Matching rules >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p>When displaying Parts/Ports in the aspect diagram, e.g., Electrical <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Data Transfer System</em> Internal Block Diagram, you can display all the parts/ports of an appropriate aspect, e.g., Electrical, by using the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">All By Aspect</strong> button. After selecting this button, all parts or ports that match the diagram's aspect can be displayed. The figure below demonstrates the usage of this button in the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog when you are trying to display all electrical aspect ports of the selected <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">control Panel</em> part.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="all_by_aspect.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports according to aspects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The All By Aspect button usage in the Display Parts/Ports dialog. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef3db962-07f7-4abc-8cde-dca9d60a9916"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472386</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Diagram aspects</strong> sample models that comes with <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024x" ri:content-title="SysML Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. To open this sample properly you need to install the SysML plugin in the MagicDraw and:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram aspects.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>. </span></span></span></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Find in the modeling tool: <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">samples\SysML</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram aspects</span>.mdzip.</span></span></em></span></span></span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710484 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00469: Displaying paths

- page_id: `136710484`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710484/Displaying+paths
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-01T11:39:15.475+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Displaying elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Warning

1107335505

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Warning

1107335508

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Warning

1107335507

This functionality allows you to visualize relationships that are created between elements in the model, but are not displayed on the diagram pane. There are two options to display paths among the element shapes:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying paths' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying paths' space='MD2024x']

displayedTo display paths between selected element shapes

1. On a diagram pane, select two or more elements.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display In-Between Paths .
  - On the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] and select Display In-Between Paths .

- 
  - On the main menu, click the **Analyze** > **Display In-Between Paths**. The **Display In-Between Paths** command is available only when two or more shapes are selected.

The existing paths between selected element shapes are displayed on the diagram pane.

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

The paths won't be displayed if: 
 - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram. 
 - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane. 
Paths to self also will not be displayed.

not_displayedTo display all existing paths of the selected shape

1. On a diagram pane, select one or more elements.
2. Do one of the following: All the existing paths among the selected shape and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed. If the element has a path to self, this path will be displayed as well.
  1. 
    - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display All Paths .
    - On the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] and select Display All Paths .
  - 
    - On the main menu, click the Analyze > Display All Paths .

#### TIP: Example of the Display All Paths command

Example of the Display All Paths command

This example presents the workflow on representing the paths between shapes on the diagram pane when they are already created in the model.

The Class shapes without any paths between them are represented in the *Room infrastructure model* Class diagram. In this case, paths between those classes exist in the Containment tree under the Relations node (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you want to visualize how the *Workplace* Class is related with other shapes displayed on the diagram pane, you can do so easily by using the**Display All Paths** command. One way to reach this command is from the shortcut menu of the selected shape (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After selecting the**Display All Paths** command, all paths between the *Workplace* Class and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed (see the figure below). The diagram shows that the *Room* consists of between one and an arbitrary number of *Workplaces*. The *Workplace* consists of *Electric Extension* and *Works Station*. Also, the *Workplace* can contain *Duplex Power Outlet*, *External HDD2*, *Digital Camera*, and *External_HDD*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display paths among element shapes automatically when displaying elements

1. On the main menu, select Options > Environment .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment Options' space='MD2024x']**Environment Options** dialog , find the Display Paths on Element Drop option and set it's value to true .
3. Click OK .
4. Select the elements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x'] and drag them on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024x']. The shapes and paths between them are displayed. WarningThe paths won't be displayed if: 
 - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram. 
 - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane.

1107335503

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with paths and relationships' space='']

**Related page in Developer Guide**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Paths' space='']

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Composite structure diagram** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='composite structure diagram.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37dd8375-c197-4b6f-bf42-a27572384477"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1107335505</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="176da447-7b42-4d75-bab2-b4002a3a64ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1107335508</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="073d1c89-9923-40a6-a69a-5d355d870cf2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1107335507</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality allows you to visualize relationships that are created between elements in the model, but are not displayed on the diagram pane. There are two options to display paths among the element shapes:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="displayed"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Displaying paths only between selected element shapes.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="not_displayed"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Displaying all existing paths among the shapes.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a5c1a99-dc96-41e3-bbf0-54960c3255f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">displayed</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display paths between selected element shapes</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On a diagram pane, select two or more elements.</p></li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong>.</li></ul></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the main menu, click the <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df015cf7-dece-418b-bf0b-e0e34099a919"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Display In-Between Paths</strong> command is available only when two or more shapes are selected.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">The existing paths between selected element shapes are displayed on the diagram pane. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13128f02-7f4d-4518-a6cf-efd94ab17c80"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The paths won't be displayed if:<br />       - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram.<br />       - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane.<br />Paths to self also will not be displayed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5110f86-ce4d-44a9-86c7-8b422d41d784"><ac:parameter ac:name="">not_displayed</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display all existing paths of the selected shape</p><hr /><ol><li>On a diagram pane, select one or more elements.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display All Paths</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Display All Paths</strong>.</li></ul></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>On the main menu, click the <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Display All Paths</strong>.</li></ul></li></ul>All the existing paths among the selected shape and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed. If the element has a path to self, this path will be displayed as well.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe1f11cf-477d-4351-82cb-af2526300618"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example of the Display All Paths command</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This example presents the workflow on representing the paths between shapes on the diagram pane when they are already created in the model.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The Class shapes without any paths between them are represented in the <em>Room infrastructure model</em> Class diagram. In this case, paths between those classes exist in the Containment tree under the Relations node (see the figure below).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="relations_node_and_class_shapes.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">If you want to visualize how the <em>Workplace</em> Class is related with other shapes displayed on the diagram pane, you can do so easily by using the<strong> Display All Paths</strong> command. One way to reach this command is from the shortcut menu of the selected shape (see the figure below).</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-11-3 18:13:4.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">After selecting the<strong> Display All Paths</strong> command, all paths between the <em>Workplace</em> Class and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed (see the figure below). The diagram shows that the <em>Room</em> consists of between one and an arbitrary number of <em>Workplaces</em>. The <em>Workplace</em> consists of <em>Electric Extension</em> and <em>Works Station</em>. Also, the <em>Workplace</em> can contain <em>Duplex Power Outlet</em>, <em>External HDD2</em>, <em>Digital Camera</em>, and <em>External_HDD</em>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2016-11-3 18:13:18.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To display paths among element shapes automatically when displaying elements</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Environment Options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, find the <strong>Display Paths on Element Drop</strong> option and set it's value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select the elements in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> and drag them on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target">The shapes and paths between them are displayed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99aac734-b4b5-4ee4-afa4-790fc249f3f4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The paths won't be displayed if:<br />       - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram.<br />       - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a0c4733-c437-48d5-b722-2f97c1d0353e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1107335503</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="three_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements" /></ac:link></p></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with paths and relationships" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related page in Developer Guide</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Paths" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Composite structure diagram</span></strong> sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download<span class="confluence-link"><em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="composite structure diagram.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022x" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.<br /></span></span></em></span></li><li>Find in modeling tool <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">samples\diagrams</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">composite structure diagram</span>.mdzip.</span></span></em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711663 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00470: Displaying rake icon

- page_id: `136711663`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711663/Displaying+rake+icon
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:49.046+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols > Displaying icon or image
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use [IMAGE alt='' src=''] for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element is decomposed and has an inner structure. Double-click the element with rake icon to open the internal diagram.

The rake icon is shown on:

- Call Behavior actions that can refer to another Activity diagram.
- Interaction Uses that can refer to another Sequence diagram.
- Packages that can refer to another Package diagrams.
- Parts that can refer to another Internal Block diagram.
- Use cases can be realized by other behavior diagrams such as Activity, State Machine and Sequence.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide rake icon

1. Right-click the symbol and select Symbol Properties or press Alt+Enter.
2. Set the Show Rake Icon property value to false .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can use <ac:image ac:title="Rake" ac:alt="Rake"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rake.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying rake icon" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>  for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element is decomposed and has an inner structure. Double-click the element with rake icon to open the internal diagram.</p><p>The rake icon is shown on:</p><ul><li>Call Behavior actions that can refer to another Activity diagram.</li><li>Interaction Uses that can refer to another Sequence diagram.</li><li>Packages that can refer to another Package diagrams.</li><li>Parts that can refer to another Internal Block diagram.</li><li>Use cases can be realized by other behavior diagrams such as Activity, State Machine and Sequence.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Rake icon on symbol" ac:alt="Rake icon on symbol"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rake_icon.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying rake icon" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>To hide rake icon</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the symbol and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> or press Alt+Enter.</li><li>Set the <strong>Show Rake Icon</strong> property value to <em>false</em>.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710477 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00471: Displaying related elements

- page_id: `136710477`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710477/Displaying+related+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:40.663+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Displaying elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example 3

567913020

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example 3

567913022

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example 3

567913021

When trying to extend your model or visualize additional elements on the diagram pane, you can represent symbols that are related to a selected symbol via relationships.This functionality allows for displaying shapes and paths among those shapes which have already been created in the model.

After selecting the **Display Related Elements** command, the **Display Related Elements** dialog opens. This dialog consists of the following sections (see the figure below):

- Element box (1) shows the name of the selected element shape of which you are going to display its related elements.
- Relations area (2) lists all relationship types which connect the selected element shape with other elements.
- Expand Relations area (3) allows you to select the direction of the relationships which you want to display on the diagram pane.
- Scope area (4) allows you to select the search scope of the elements related to the selected element.
- Depth area (5) allows you to specify the search range for the selected relationships.
- Always create new symbols option (6) allows you to create a symbol even if a related element already has a symbol in the diagram.
- Layout related elements option (7) lays out new symbols by using the default diagram layout.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The following procedure will guide you through using the **Display Related Elements** command and dialog when you want to represent all related elements via selected relationship types of the selected shape on the diagram pane. The functionality works for the entire diagram, single shapes, or numerous selected shapes.

To display related elements

1. Select a diagram frame or any number of shapes on the diagram pane.
2. Open the **Display Related Elements** dialog in one of the following ways:
  - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display Related Elements .
  - On the diagram toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_related_elements.png'] , and select Display Related Elements .
  - On the main menu, click **Analyze** > **Display Related Elements**. ImportantThe **Display Related Elements** dialog cannot be opened if the selected element shape or diagram doesn't contain any related elements. Example 1A quicker way to display related elements of the selected shape is to use its shortcut menu, and select **Display** > **Display Related Elements**. The example below demonstrates the shortcut menu of the Class named *Shipment*. It is showing the need to display related elements of the *Shipment* Class in the *Product Shipment object diagram*.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the opened dialog, specify:
  - In the Relations area (2), select check boxes of the listed relationship types.
  - In the Expand Relations area (3), select the direction of the relationships: - Incoming - in which the element participates as client. - Outgoing - in which the element participates as supplier. - Both- in which the element participates as both client and supplier.
  - In the Scope area (4), choose the location in which the related elements should be found: - Whole Project - search in the whole model. - Package - search in the package under which the selected element is created.
  - In the Depth area (5), select the search range for the relationships: - Indefinite - search for all possible relationships. - Definite - search for a specified level of relationships.
4. Select the check box of the Always create new symbols option to create a symbol even when the related element already has a symbol displayed on the diagram pane.
5. Select the check box of the **Layout related elements**option to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Layout' space='MD2024x'] new symbols by using the default diagram [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Layout' space='MD2024x']. NoteThe position of the selected element shape can be changed after selecting the **Layout related elements** check box. Example 2For example, if you would like to display all related elements of the *Shipment* Class that are connected through the Generalization relationship, you can find them according to the following criteria:- The direction of the generalization relation is both incoming and outgoing. 
- The related elements to be searched for are in the whole project. 
- The search depth of the relationship is one level.If you want to layout related elements after the display, but don't want to create new symbols which are already displayed in the *Product Shipment object diagram*.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Click **OK**. 
According to the specified relationship type, scope, depth, direction, layout, and creation options, all related elements of the selected shape are represented as shapes and paths on the diagram pane. Example 3After opening the **Display Related Elements** dialog (Example 1), and defining the search criteria in it (Example 2), two Classes (*VendorShipment* and *CustomerShipment*) related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the *Product Shipment object diagram* pane. The figure below is the result of following the steps described in Example 1 and Example 2.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

567913019

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Layout' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements in the diagram' space='MD2024x'] (Open API)

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Inventory Control System** sample model that comes with MagicDraw. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='Inventory Control System.mdzip']
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\case studies\Inventory Control System.mdzip.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd0de44d-5c52-4f80-86ba-5a1f0ff0f08d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567913020</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3219c483-b7a5-487a-a500-a3747eab5873"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567913022</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aaf9f520-5faf-4bec-8e92-1e996989277e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567913021</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>When trying to extend your model or visualize additional elements on the diagram pane, you can represent symbols that are related to a selected symbol via relationships.This functionality allows for displaying shapes and paths among those shapes which have already been created in the model.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>After selecting the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> command, the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog opens. This dialog consists of the following sections (see the figure below):</p><ul><li><em>Element</em> box (1) shows the name of the selected element shape of which you are going to display its related elements.</li><li><em>Relations</em> area (2) lists all relationship types which connect the selected element shape with other elements.</li><li><em>Expand Relations</em> area (3) allows you to select the direction of the relationships which you want to display on the diagram pane.</li><li><em>Scope</em> area (4) allows you to select the search scope of the elements related to the selected element.</li><li><em>Depth</em> area (5) allows you to specify the search range for the selected relationships.</li><li>Always create new symbols option (6) allows you to create a symbol even if a related element already has a symbol in the diagram.</li><li><em>Layout related elements</em> option (7) lays out new symbols by using the default diagram layout.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="The seven areas in the Display Related Elements dialog." ac:alt="The seven areas in the Display Related Elements dialog."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements_dialog_areas.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The following procedure will guide you through using the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> command and dialog when you want to represent all related elements via selected relationship types of the selected shape on the diagram pane. The functionality works for the entire diagram, single shapes, or numerous selected shapes.</p><p> </p><p>To display related elements</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a diagram frame or any number of shapes on the diagram pane.</li><li><p>Open the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li>Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select<strong> Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, and select <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</li><li><p>On the main menu, click <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8506d135-0ccf-4672-bb3e-67a2276a84cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog cannot be opened if the selected element shape or diagram doesn't contain any related elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71ae0b19-4229-488a-b7c0-35a3f7dd9599"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A quicker way to display related elements of the selected shape is to use its shortcut menu, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>. The example below demonstrates the shortcut menu of the Class named <em>Shipment</em>. It is showing the need to display related elements of the <em>Shipment</em> Class in the <em>Product Shipment object diagram</em>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Display Related Elements command under the Display command group in the shortcut menu of the Shipment Class." ac:alt="The Display Related Elements command under the Display command group in the shortcut menu of the Shipment Class."><ri:attachment ri:filename="shortcut_menu_display_related_elements.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li><li>In the opened dialog, specify:<br /><ul><li>In the Relations area (2), select check boxes of the listed relationship types.</li><li>In the Expand Relations area (3), select the direction of the relationships:<br />- <strong>Incoming</strong> - in which the element participates as client.<br />- <strong>Outgoing</strong> - in which the element participates as supplier.<br />- <strong>Both-</strong>in which the element participates as both client and supplier.</li><li>In the Scope area (4), choose the location in which the related elements should be found:<br />-<strong> Whole Project</strong> - search in the whole model.<br />- <strong>Package </strong>- search in the package under which the selected element is created.</li><li>In the Depth area (5), select the search range for the relationships:<br />-<strong> Indefinite</strong> - search for all possible relationships.<br />- <strong>Definite</strong> - search for a specified level of relationships.</li></ul></li><li>Select the check box of the <strong>Always create new symbols</strong> option to create a symbol even when the related element already has a symbol displayed on the diagram pane.</li><li><p>Select the check box of the <strong>Layout related elements </strong>option to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Layout" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[layout]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> new symbols by using the default diagram <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Layout" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[layout]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca55d086-99f0-4658-b091-e71aff3855e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The position of the selected element shape can be changed after selecting the <strong>Layout related elements</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d05eaf9-951d-4f91-a2e3-71340aa6934f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For example, if you would like to display all related elements of the <em>Shipment</em> Class that are connected through the Generalization relationship, you can find them according to the following criteria:</p><p>- The direction of the generalization relation is both incoming and outgoing.<br />- The related elements to be searched for are in the whole project.<br />- The search depth of the relationship is one level.</p><p>If you want to layout related elements after the display, but don't want to create new symbols which are already displayed in the <em>Product Shipment object diagram</em>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Display Related Elements dialog of the Shipment Class." ac:alt="The Display Related Elements dialog of the Shipment Class."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements_dialog_of_class_shipment.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />According to the specified relationship type, scope, depth, direction, layout, and creation options, all related elements of the selected shape are represented as shapes and paths on the diagram pane.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d1e5e9e-6684-4fd6-b528-046e6f11e36c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 3</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After opening the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> dialog (Example 1), and defining the search criteria in it (Example 2), two Classes (<em>VendorShipment</em> and <em>CustomerShipment</em>) related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the <em>Product Shipment object diagram</em> pane. The figure below is the result of following the steps described in Example 1 and Example 2.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="VendorShipment and CustomerShipment Classes related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the the Product Shipment object diagram pane." ac:alt="VendorShipment and CustomerShipment Classes related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the the Product Shipment object diagram pane."><ri:attachment ri:filename="related_elements_of_the_shipment_class_displayed_on_the_diagram_pane.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e1d8f1c-eff3-4889-adef-06188794b05c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567913019</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying elements" /></ac:link></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Layout" /></ac:link></p></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements in the diagram" /></ac:link> (Open API)</p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Inventory Control System</strong> sample model that comes with MagicDraw. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Inventory Control System.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Inventory Control System.mdzip.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><em> </em></li><li>Find in modeling tool<em> &lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\case studies\Inventory Control System.mdzip.</em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715223 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00472: Displaying related elements in the diagram

- page_id: `136715223`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715223/Displaying+related+elements+in+the+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:13.461+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Working with diagrams > Presentation elements (symbols) > Working with presentation elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools allow displaying symbols that are related to a given symbol via relationships. The [*com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayRelatedSymbols*](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/symbols/DisplayRelatedSymbols.html) class provides API methods for this. Using this class you can control any of the following behaviors for displaying the related symbols logic:

- What relationship types should be included.
- The depth of a relationship tree.
- Whether or not existing symbols should be reused.

###### **Example: Displaying related generalizations and interface realizations**

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\displayrelated

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Session management' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Our modeling tools allow displaying symbols that are related to a given symbol via relationships. The <a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/uml/symbols/DisplayRelatedSymbols.html"><em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.DisplayRelatedSymbols</em></a> class provides API methods for this. Using this class you can control any of the following behaviors for displaying the related symbols logic:</p><ul><li>What relationship types should be included.</li><li>The depth of a relationship tree.</li><li>Whether or not existing symbols should be reused.</li></ul><p> </p><h4><strong>Example: Displaying related generalizations and interface realizations</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="27d9045e-ee43-4a27-b18b-9e033face031"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    SessionManager sessionManager = SessionManager.getInstance();
    sessionManager.createSession(project, "Display related");
 
    Set linkTypes = new HashSet();
    linkTypes.add(new LinkType(Generalization.class));
    linkTypes.add(new LinkType(InterfaceRealization.class));
    
    DisplayRelatedSymbolsInfo info = new DisplayRelatedSymbolsInfo(linkTypes);
    info.setDepthLimited(true);
    info.setDepthLimit(3);
 
    PresentationElement view = ...; // A symbol for which you need to invoke the displaying related symbols action.
    DisplayRelatedSymbols.displayRelatedSymbols(view, info);
    sessionManager.closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\displayrelated</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58e45d31-88de-4344-8caa-c6382236cc70"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Session management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710251 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00473: Displaying row elements hierarchically

- page_id: `136710251`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710251/Displaying+row+elements+hierarchically
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:34.054+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables > Hierarchy in tables
- labels: ['nested-instances', 'display-nested-elements', 'display-nested-instances', 'display-mode', 'complete-tree', 'comapct-tree', 'specify-query', 'hierarchy-in-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Hierarchical tables are very useful for managing and reviewing data. Hierarchy in a table is a tree-like structure where all the rows are listed according to the elements containment.

**Important**

The hierarchy in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space='MD2024x'] is displayed according to an instance value that is a slot value of another Instance Specification. Composite instances are displayed.

##### Displaying elements hierarchically in a tableDisplaying elements hierarchically

To display elements hierarchically

1. In the table toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
2. Under the Display Mode option choose: The hierarchy is created in the table by listing all the elements as they are shown in the Containment tree.
  - **Complete tree** to display row elements in a hierarchy where all the owners are represented in separate rows. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - **Compact tree**to display row elements in a hierarchy where the common owners are grouped together in a separate row. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Displaying elements in custom hierarchyDisplaying row elements in a custom hierarchy

To display elements in a custom hierarchy

1. In the Criteria area, next to the Scope box, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='scope_query.png'] icon . [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_query_button.png']
2. Specify the expression to collect table elements:

- Select Containment to display the decomposition of elements related through the Containment relationship.
- Select Structure to display the decomposition of Part Properties related through the Composition relationship.
- Select Custom to display the decomposition of elements related through any criteria of your choice.

If you have selected Custom, please [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='MD2024x'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specifying expression in the Query dialog

##### Expanding/collapsing nodes in a hierarchical table

To expand/collapse nodes in a hierarchical table

- Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] / [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the name column. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Select the specific row, hold Ctrl, and then press the right/left arrow key on the keyboard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710248 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00474: Displaying/hiding element numbers in a row

- page_id: `136710248`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710248/Displaying+hiding+element+numbers+in+a+row
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:33.993+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables > Working with rows
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a new table, elements represented as rows are automatically numbered in the name column. The following procedure is important for those who modify existing tables created before Version 18.4.

To display/hide element numbers in the table

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. Set the **Show Element Number** property value to *true* to display element numbers, or *false* to hide them*.*The element numbers are displayed in the name columns before the element name (see the following example). [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When creating a new table, elements represented as rows are automatically numbered in the name column. The following procedure is important for those who modify existing tables created before Version 18.4.</p><p>To display/hide element numbers in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Specification</strong> window of the table.</li><li><p>Set the <strong>Show Element Number</strong> property value to <em>true</em> to display element numbers, or <em>false</em> to hide them<em>.<br /></em>The element numbers are displayed in the name columns before the element name (see the following example).</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Requirement numbers are displayed in the Magic Library Requirement table." ac:alt="The Requirement numbers are displayed in the Magic Library Requirement table."><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_number_in_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying/hiding element numbers in a row" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715045 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00475: Distributing custom diagrams

- page_id: `136715045`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715045/Distributing+custom+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:01.745+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Warning

1523698130

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Warning

1523698132

1523698127

**On this page**

**3**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Warning

1523698131

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing diagrams' space='MD2024x'] can be exchanged with other usersin a *.zip* file format, as any other resource, for manual import into the modeling tool using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Manager' space='MD2024x']. Alternatively, you can export/import the custom diagram descriptor (the .*xml*file) using the **Customize Diagrams** dialog.

##### Distributing custom diagrams using Resource Manager

You need to build a*.zip*file containing a diagram descriptor file (e.g., *descriptor.xml*) and a Resource Manager descriptor file (e.g., *MDR_Custom Diagram_MagicDraw_74842_descriptor.xm**l*.)to be able to share your own custom diagram. For this, you need to use the **Development Tools** plugin. Once you generate and share the file, the diagram customization can then be imported into the modeling tool by another user via the **Resource Manager**for future use and accessibility.

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

You can build a *.zip* file for custom diagram distribution only after you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new diagram type' space='MD2024x']created a newdiagram type or edited an existing standard diagram.

To create a *.zip* file using the **Development Tools** plugin

1. On your local file system, create the following folders structure: ...\<Custom Diagram Resource>\data\defaults\data\diagrams . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Launch the modeling tool.
3. Do one of the following:
  - 
    1. In the main menu, go to **Help** > **About**.
    2. Next to Configuration Files , click the file location path. The configuration files folder opens.
    3. Under <configuration files>\data\diagrams, locate your custom diagram folder containing a descriptor .
  - 
    1. In the main menu, go to**Diagrams** >**Customize**.
    2. In the **Customize Diagrams** dialog, select the custom diagram whose descriptor file you want to export and click the**Export** button. WarningBefore the export operation, create a new folder on your local file system and give it the name of your custom diagram, such as *Custom Diagram*. The exported descriptor file needs to be renamed to *descriptor.xml*and saved in this newly-created folder.
4. Copy and paste the custom diagram folder together with the descriptor file into the folders structure you created in thefirststep. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Distributing ProfilesIf you have specified Profiles when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing diagrams' space='MD2024x'], it is highly recommended that you distribute them together with your custom diagram. For this, create the *Profiles* folder under the custom diagram resource root folder and place all required Profiles in it before generating a *.zip*archive.You need to mark each Profile distributed together with a custom resource as**Standard/System Profile** (**File** >**Project Properties** > **More** >**Standard/System Profile**) to store it locally. Profiles stored on the server cannot be used for a custom diagram.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Download and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing modeling tools and plugins' space='IL2024x'] the Development Tools plugin.
6. Restart the modeling tool.
7. In the main menu, click Tools > Development Tools > Build Custom Resource .
8. In the Custom Resource Building Wizard , set the custom diagram folder as the Resource Folder .
9. Click Next to skip the other steps and then click Finish . The resource ( . zip file) is built. It can now be distributed and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Manager' space='MD2024x'].

#### INFO: Info

Info

A created custom diagram resource (.*zip*) can be bundled into the resource distribution file (.*rdzip*) using the **Development Tools** plugin for distribution alongside other resources. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building a resource distribution file' space='MD2024x']>]]>

##### Distributing custom diagrams using Customize Diagrams dialog

You can export the custom diagram descriptor (.*xml*) file directly into the**Customize Diagrams** dialog. The exported file can then be shared with others and imported into the modeling tool by another user via the same **Customize Diagrams** dialog using the **Import**command.

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

You can export an .*xml*file for custom diagram distribution only after you have[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new diagram type' space='MD2024x']or edited an existing standard diagram.

To export a custom diagram descriptor

1. In the main menu, go to Diagrams > Customize .
2. Select a custom diagram from the list and click the Export button. [ATTACHMENT filename='exporting_diagram_descriptor.png']

The .*xml* file is exported. You can now distribute it.

To import a custom diagram descriptor into the modeling tool

1. In the main menu, go to Diagrams > Customize .
2. Click the **Import** button and point to a descriptor (.*xml*)**on your local file system. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

The *.xml* file is imported, and a new diagram type appears under **Custom** **Diagrams**in the **Customize Diagrams** dialog.****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b277f74b-e3a3-427b-a2c3-cacc84dd1cd6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1523698130</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5065f0a-667b-4025-a1ec-446a822b81e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1523698132</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1ab2bcd-1e4b-44c3-b64e-a0452d78669c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1523698127</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0c159d6-10b5-4b2a-a6db-feb87db6c1ef"><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97826136-737d-4280-9c80-6a6996e8c1b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1523698131</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="885cb35b-65e1-4587-be5b-555938d081bc">can be exchanged with other users </ac:inline-comment-marker>in a <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.zip</em> file format, as any other resource, for manual import into the modeling tool using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Manager" /></ac:link>. Alternatively, you can export/import the custom diagram descriptor (the .<em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">xml </em>file) using the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Customize Diagrams</strong> dialog. </p><h3>Distributing custom diagrams using Resource Manager</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60547a15-a3ad-448d-af43-5e98a7e8f3ff">You need to build a </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60547a15-a3ad-448d-af43-5e98a7e8f3ff">.zip</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60547a15-a3ad-448d-af43-5e98a7e8f3ff"> file containing a diagram descriptor file (e.g., <em>descriptor.xml</em></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60547a15-a3ad-448d-af43-5e98a7e8f3ff">) and a Resource Manager descriptor file (e.g., <em>MDR_Custom Diagram_MagicDraw_74842_descriptor.xm</em><em>l</em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.) </span>to be able to share your own custom diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker> For this, you need to use the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin. Once you generate and share the file, the diagram customization can then be imported into the modeling tool by another user via the <strong>Resource Manager </strong>for future use and accessibility. </p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf5b8bcc-78e7-416b-a481-a4cb9bb09c9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can build a <em>.zip</em> file for custom diagram distribution <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e138a9a9-2a19-4a0a-b561-37fa6c4738c6">only after</ac:inline-comment-marker> you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating new diagram type" /><ac:link-body>created a new<span> diagram type</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> or edited an existing standard diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a <em>.zip</em> file using the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4ad3836a-5451-4c2d-9781-c716582b08f0">On your local file system,</ac:inline-comment-marker> create the following folders structure: <em>...\&lt;Custom Diagram Resource&gt;\data\default<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="176a7421-9e59-4b0a-8345-332a8a3bd37b">s\data\diagrams</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="176a7421-9e59-4b0a-8345-332a8a3bd37b">.</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="176a7421-9e59-4b0a-8345-332a8a3bd37b"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_diagram_resource_folders_structure.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Distributing custom diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Launch the modeling tool. </li><li>Do one of the following: <ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44"><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44">In the main menu, go to <strong>Help</strong> &gt; <strong>About</strong>. </ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44">Next to </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44">Configuration Files</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44">,</ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44">click the file location path. The configuration files folder opens.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Under <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">&lt;configuration files&gt;\data\diagrams,</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> locate your custom diagram folder containing a descriptor</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></p><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the main menu, go to<strong> Diagrams</strong> &gt;<strong> Customize</strong>.</span></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the <strong>Customize Diagrams</strong> dialog, select the custom diagram whose descriptor file you want to export and click the<strong> Export</strong> button. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="04cb718a-579a-47d8-98e3-1635280cb9e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before the export operation, create a new folder on your local file system and give it the name of your custom diagram, such as <em>Custom Diagram</em>. The exported descriptor file needs to be renamed to <em>descriptor.xml </em>and saved in this newly-created folder.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44">Copy and paste the custom diagram folder together with the descriptor file into the folders structure you created in the </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44">first</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c2d772e-abb8-40d8-a463-f017fa5bbb44"> step.</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><br /><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_diagram_folder_with_a_descriptor.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Distributing custom diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ed1c88c-36c9-4093-ba1b-89777c42636b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Distributing Profiles</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have specified P<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="63b4a73c-08ff-4f59-8a60-db89fba517a6">rofiles w</ac:inline-comment-marker>hen <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[customizing a diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, it is highly recommended that you distribute them together with your custom diagram. For this, create the <em>Profiles</em> folder under the custom diagram resource root folder and place all required Profiles in it before generating a <em>.zip </em>archive.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="520947aa-aa71-4079-9342-e66b046aa82b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You need to mark each Profile distributed together with a custom resource as<strong> Standard/System Profile</strong> (<strong>File</strong> &gt;<strong> Project Properties</strong> &gt; <strong>More</strong> &gt;<strong> Standard/System Profile</strong>) to store it locally. Profiles stored on the server cannot be used for a custom diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="profiles_folder.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Distributing custom diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Download and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2024x" ri:content-title="Installing modeling tools and plugins" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[install]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin. </li><li>Restart the modeling tool.</li><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Development Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Build Custom Resource</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Custom Resource Building Wizard</strong>, set the custom diagram<em> </em>folder as the <strong>Resource </strong><strong>Folder</strong>. </li><li>Click<strong> Next</strong> to skip the other steps and then click <strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e7e5bbb4-c15b-4440-bf69-d213b65a12ea">Finish</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e7e5bbb4-c15b-4440-bf69-d213b65a12ea">.</ac:inline-comment-marker> <br /><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6f76b360-0567-4631-8448-0e5ddfef8e2c"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="75f63a8f-837b-433a-9320-a72728e616a7">The resource (</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6f76b360-0567-4631-8448-0e5ddfef8e2c"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="75f63a8f-837b-433a-9320-a72728e616a7">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></em><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6f76b360-0567-4631-8448-0e5ddfef8e2c"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="75f63a8f-837b-433a-9320-a72728e616a7">zip</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6f76b360-0567-4631-8448-0e5ddfef8e2c"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="75f63a8f-837b-433a-9320-a72728e616a7"> file) is built.</ac:inline-comment-marker> It can now be distributed and <ac:link ac:anchor="Importing resource using Resource Manager"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Manager" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[imported into the modeling tool using the Resource Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66a4e43d-0041-4421-82f5-7b4164fa33cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Info</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A created custom diagram resource (.<em>zip</em>) can be bundled into the resource distribution file (.<em>rdzip</em>) using the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin for distribution alongside other resources. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Building a resource distribution file" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about building a resource distribution file>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Distributing custom diagrams using Customize Diagrams dialog</h3><p>You can export the custom diagram descriptor (.<em>xml</em>) file directly into the<strong> Customize Diagrams</strong> dialog. The exported file can then be shared with others and imported into the modeling tool by another user via the same <strong>Customize Diagrams</strong> dialog using the <strong>Import </strong>command. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71ad1e09-7273-4ba2-9f50-9333cfe89384"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can export an .<em>xml </em>file for custom diagram distribution only after <span>you have </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating new diagram type" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[created a new diagram type]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> or edited an existing standard diagram.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To export a custom diagram descriptor </p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, go to <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Customize</strong>.</li><li>Select a custom diagram from the list and click the <strong>Export </strong>button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exporting_diagram_descriptor.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Distributing custom diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>The .<em>xml</em> file is exported. You can now distribute it.</p><p><br /></p><p>To import a custom diagram descriptor into the modeling tool</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, go to <strong>Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Customize</strong>. </li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Import</strong> button and point to a descriptor (.<em>xml</em>)<em> </em>on your local file system.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="importing_descriptor_file.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Distributing custom diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><p>The <em>.xml</em> file is imported, and a new diagram type appears under <strong>Custom</strong> <strong>Diagrams </strong>in the <strong>Customize Diagrams</strong> dialog.<strong> </strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715052 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00476: Distributing Profiles

- page_id: `136715052`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715052/Distributing+Profiles
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:02.066+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1777779865

1777779876

1777779866

You can distribute profiles with included customization data in the following two ways:

- Exchanging Profiles via XMI
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Manager' space='MD2024x']

###### Exchanging Profiles via XMI

Using UML XMI interchange mechanisms it is possible to interchange profiles between tools, together with models to which they have been applied. A profile must therefore be defined as an interchangeable UML model. In addition to exchanging profiles together with models between tools, profile application should also be definable “by reference”; that is, a profile does not need to be interchanged if it is already present in the importing tool.

1777779864

**Related pages**

- Resource Manager
- Distributing Resources

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

A profile must therefore be defined as an interchangeable UML model. In addition to exchanging profiles together with models between tools, profile application should also be definable “by reference”; that is, a profile does not need to be interchanged if it is already present in the importing tool.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d9b39b1-01b7-482f-aeab-0c421c83f8d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1777779864</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Resource+Manager">Resource Manager</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Distributing+Resources">Distributing Resources</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715092 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00477: Distributing Resources

- page_id: `136715092`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715092/Distributing+Resources
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:04.957+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide
- labels: ['resource-manager']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

515448144

515448146

515448145

In this section, you will find information about how to share your created resources with other users. The easiest way to distribute data is to store resources in the one zip archive and import files to your modeling tool with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Manager' space='MD2024x'].

You can distribute the following types of resources:

- Custom Diagrams
- Profiles
- Templates
- Samples and Documentation
- Plugins

You can distribute the one resource type or you may distribute the whole set of resources.

515448143

**Related pages**

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bdadbbc1-c9d6-4692-bc1f-de4dc018a0ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">515448144</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c743ac6a-f7ae-4b77-b96d-d985e61c7c73"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">515448146</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7728de44-828f-4429-b587-1f9a3799986b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">515448145</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In this section, you will find information about how to share your created resources with other users. The easiest way to distribute data is to store resources in the one zip archive and import files to your modeling tool with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Manager" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[MagicDraw Resource Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>You can distribute the following types of resources:</p><ul><li>Custom Diagrams</li><li>Profiles</li><li>Templates</li><li>Samples and Documentation</li><li>Plugins</li></ul><p>You can distribute the one resource type or you may distribute the whole set of resources.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2ad058d-79f2-49eb-93af-1eaa6bc1ce11"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">515448143</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="174d99c1-c509-4eeb-a549-04e0b9ee479d"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714911 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00478: Do Not Suggest As Type

- page_id: `136714911`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714911/Do+Not+Suggest+As+Type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:57.099+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Creating Customization Data > Customization Elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Excludes elements from being suggested as a type in the list dialogs.

To exclude a DSL element from the list dialogs

1. In the Customization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] , click the Customization Target property.
2. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selection in diagrams' space='MD2024x'] dialog appears.
3. Select a stereotype or a metaclass.
4. Click OK to close the dialog.
5. Set the Hide Metatype property to true .
6. Set the Do Not Suggest As Type property to true .
7. Click Close .
8. Reopen the project. Elements or metaclasses selected in the third step of this procedure are not listed in the list dialogs. For example, in the Property [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'], click the Type property and then the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. In the element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selection in diagrams' space='MD2024x'] dialog, the elements or metaclasses selected as **customization targets** will not be displayed.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Customization Data' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Customization Data' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Element Properties Description' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customization Target' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating your First Customization' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and specifying derived properties' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining expressions' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hide Metatype' space='MD2024x']
