<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715153 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00495: Editing model elements

- page_id: `136715153`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715153/Editing+model+elements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:10:13.161+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > UML model > Working with UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To edit some *com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element*, a session with the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager***must be created.

All changes in the UML model will be registered and, on the session closing, will be added into the command history.

```java

```

It is the programmer responsibility to ensure that the modified element is not read-only by checking element editing permissions.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Session management' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Checking element editing permissions' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><div class="WordSection1"><p>To edit some <em>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</em>, a session with the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.openapi.uml.SessionManager</em><em> </em>must be created. </p><p>All changes in the UML model will be registered and, on the session closing, will be added into the command history.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9cb57726-9021-46e3-a746-c9f00cb8d206"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    Project project = ...;
    SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Edit class A");
    if (classA.isEditable())
    {
        classA.setName(newName);
    }
    SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><div class="WordSection1">It is the programmer responsibility to ensure that the modified element is not read-only by checking element editing permissions.</div></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f80fb8f-62ab-4a95-b916-c5df66cb9b23"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Session management" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Checking element editing permissions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711552 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00496: Editing multiline textual values

- page_id: `136711552`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711552/Editing+multiline+textual+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:44.742+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Editing textual properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Some properties can have more than one textual value, for example, the **Body**property of an opaque expression, the **Pre Condition**and **Post Condition**properties of a use case. You can use a special editor to edit multiline textual values.

Using the editor for multiline textual values, you can perform the following actions:

- Create a new value.
- Remove a selected value.
- Edit each value in the HTML editor separately.
- Reorder values.
- Strip long values (available only in the Specification window).

To strip long values, make sure that the Strip Multiline Text mode isturned on in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].

The following table describes functions of buttons used in the editor.

| Button | Shortcut Key | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | Select the HTML check box to edit a selected row in the HTML mode.The HTML editor toolbar will appear. For more information about theHTML toolbar, refer to "HTML editor toolbar" on page 428. |
|  | ALT+ENTER | Click to add an empty row after the selected one to type a new value. |
|  | ALT+BACKSPACE | Click to remove a selected row. |
|  | ALT+SHIFT+UPARROW | Click to moves up a selected row. |
|  | ALT+SHIFT+DOWNARROW | Click to moves down a selected row. |

Mac OS users should use MAC shortcut key as an alternative for the ALT key.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711549 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00497: Editing property values

- page_id: `136711549`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711549/Editing+property+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:44.549+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can edit property values in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tables' space='MD2024x']
- Element tab on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties tab' space='MD2024x']**Properties**tabin the Model Browser

Property values in gray are non-editable. They are derived from other elements used in your model.

Learn about editing values of different property types in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing textual properties' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing logical values' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting values' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cases of advanced editing' space='MD2024x']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true1

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711573 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00498: Editing property values in property groups

- page_id: `136711573`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711573/Editing+property+values+in+property+groups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:45.577+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Cases of advanced editing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Some elements can be related to each other as the owner and the owned one, for example, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MD2024x'] can be an owner of a trigger, and the trigger can be owned by the transition.

MagicDraw allows the handy editing of owned element property values via owner’s properties. Owned element properties are available as appropriately named property groups, for example, the *Entry*, *Do Activity*, and *Exit*property groups in the state’s Specification window (see the following figure).

When editing owned element properties, keep in mind that you create a new element in your model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

A property setin the property group may vary before and after owned element specification. For example, let’s say,we have an activity assigned as a behavior type in the **Entry**property group. As a result the additional properties appear in the **Entry** property group (**Name** and **Owned Diagram**) after creating a new activity (see the following figure).

Keep in mind that the owner’s specification contains only ownership-relevant properties of an owned element. 
To view and edit all other properties of the owned element, open its Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an owned element as a property value

1. In the property group, wherein you want to create the owned element, select either a type or an elementproperty specification cell.
2. From the list, select an element you need to create.
3. Specify additional properties corresponding to the created element.

To remove an owned element, do one of the following

- Either

1. Select the **<owned element type>**property specification cell.
2. In the drop-down list, select *<UNSPECIFIED>*.

- Or

1. Select the <owned element> property cell and click **...** button.
2. From the list, select **Delete <element>**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711371 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00499: Editing tagged value

- page_id: `136711371`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711371/Editing+tagged+value
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:35.682+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Tag
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a new tagged value, do one of the following

- In the element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] , Tags property group, select an available tag definition and click the Create Value button.
- In the Containment tree, select an element and drag it to the Specification window tag value area.

To create default tag values

1. In the stereotype's Specification window, expand the Tag Definitions property group and select the tag definition.
2. Assign a value for the Default Value property.
3. Create an element and assign a stereotype for this element. The element will have tags with assigned default values.

##### Setting a default tag value to tag with empty value

MagicDraw allows for setting default tag values to a tag with an empty value. This functionality is needed when a stereotype is already assigned to an element and a new mandatory tag definition with a default value is created for the stereotype. After creating such a tag definition, the model elements that have the modified stereotype applied will have the newly created tags unset.

To set default values instead of empty values

1. In the main menu, select Tools > Set Empty Tags to Defaults . The Select Package dialog opens.
2. Select the scope of elements to which you want to set the default tag values. If you want to assign default values to all project tags, in the *All* data tree select the root package *Data* and click the **Add All** button. The root package *Data* is added to the **Selected** objects list. Click **OK**.
3. In the opened dialog, click Yes . Now, the element with stereotype tags has been assigned default values.

The following conditions are required to set the default tag values

- The element should have an assigned stereotype with a specified default value property.
- The tag definition, to which you want to assign a default value, should contain no value.

The default tag values are not set when the stereotype property (tag definition) multiplicity is equal to 0, 0..1, 0..*, *.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tag' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a new tagged value, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the element <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, <strong>Tags</strong> property group, select an available tag definition and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button.</li><li>In the Containment tree, select an element and drag it to the Specification window tag value area.</li></ul><p> </p><p>To create default tag values</p><hr /><ol><li>In the stereotype's Specification window, expand the <strong>Tag Definitions</strong> property group and select the tag definition.</li><li>Assign a value for the <strong>Default Value</strong> property.</li><li>Create an element and assign a stereotype for this element. The element will have tags with assigned default values.</li></ol><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="66458d2c-4a05-4c4b-a183-c28d967c3755">Setting a default tag value to tag with empty value</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p>MagicDraw allows for setting default tag values to a tag with an empty value. This functionality is needed when a stereotype is already assigned to an element and a new mandatory tag definition with a default value is created for the stereotype. After creating such a tag definition, the model elements that have the modified stereotype applied will have the newly created tags unset.</p><p> </p><p>To set default values instead of empty values</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Set Empty Tags to Defaults</strong>. The <strong>Select Package</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p>Select the scope of elements to which you want to set the default tag values.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b9a5e19f-c7a9-498a-89c3-bf18e7f08f41"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to assign default values to all project tags, in the <em>All</em> data tree select the root package <em>Data</em> and click the <strong>Add All</strong> button. The root package <em>Data</em> is added to the <strong>Selected</strong> objects list. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>In the opened dialog, click <strong>Yes</strong>. Now, the element with stereotype tags has been assigned default values.</li></ol><p> </p><p>The following conditions are required to set the default tag values</p><ul><li>The element should have an assigned stereotype with a specified default value property.</li><li>The tag definition, to which you want to assign a default value, should contain no value.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6cde589-7fae-4130-931b-7f8d66886565"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default tag values are not set when the stereotype property (tag definition) multiplicity is equal to 0, 0..1, 0..*, *.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1cb6614-da6a-4a7b-adf1-3ec18e11a18c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Tag" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711550 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00500: Editing textual properties

- page_id: `136711550`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711550/Editing+textual+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:44.611+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Textual type property values are usually simply typed in a property value cell. In some specific cases you can also use advanced editing features, i.e., long textual values editor and multiline textual values editor (both available in HTML). 
Examples of textual type properties: *Name*, *Body*.

To edit a textual property value

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Type a value.

Learn about advanced cases of editing textual values in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing long textual values' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing multiline textual values' space='MD2024x']

#### PANEL: Related pages

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713296 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00501: Editing the content of a used project

- page_id: `136713296`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713296/Editing+the+content+of+a+used+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:24.436+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To switch versions of the used projects you must have an**Administer Resources** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud185'].

Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) projects can only use other projects in the read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as stand-alone.

To open a used project for editing

1. In the Model Browser, right-click the project to open a shortcut menu under the package Project Usages .
2. Click Project Usages and select:
  - **Open Latest Version** to open the latest version of the used project for editing. Note that the version used in the project does **not** change after editing the used project. Updating used project versionsIf you select the check-box **Notify on New Version** in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing the used project version' space='MD2024x']**Project Usages**dialog for a particular used project, you will be notified about the existence of a later used project version while reloading the main project.
  - Open This version to open the actual project version for editing. If the used version is not the latest one, the project will open in the Read-only mode.

Now you can modify the structure and content of the project.

#### WARNING: Updating used project version

Updating used project version

Please note, the used project version in the main project remains the same. You should [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing the used project version' space='MD2024x'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as stand-alone.</p><p> </p><p>To open a used project for editing</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, right-click the project to open a shortcut menu under the package <em>Project Usages</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>Project Usages</strong> and select:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Open Latest Version</strong> to open the latest version of the used project for editing. If the used version is not the latest one, the project will open in the Read-only mode.</li></ul></li></ol><p>Now you can modify the structure and content of the project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ecc64b9-4974-4f09-ab53-3e90c70944ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Updating used project version</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please note, the used project version in the main project remains the same. To open a used project for editing

1. In the Model Browser, right-click the project to open a shortcut menu under the package Project Usages .
2. Click Project Usages and select:
  - **Open Latest Version** to open the latest version of the used project for editing. Note that the version used in the project does **not** change after editing the used project. Updating used project versionsIf you select the check-box **Notify on New Version** in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing used project version in 3DEXPERIENCE' space='']**Project Usages**dialog for a particular used project, you will be notified about the existence of a later used project version while reloading the main project.
  - Open This version to open the actual project version for editing. If the used version is not the latest one, the project will open in read-only mode.

Now you can modify the structure and content of the project.

#### WARNING: Updating used project version

Updating used project version

Please note: the used project version in the main project remains the same. You should [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing used project version in 3DEXPERIENCE' space=''].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712800 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00503: Element not in diagram

- page_id: `136712800`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712800/Element+not+in+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:54.163+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Validation > UML validation rules > UML completeness constraints rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

Element(s) not in diagram

**Description**

This validation rule checks if the element is displayed on the diagram pane when that element and the diagram have the same owner (behavior context). This rule applies to Sequence, Activity, State Machine, and Communication diagrams.

**Severity**

warning

**Constrained Element**

Diagram

**Solvers**

- Display Inner Elements - displays the element that is not displayed in the diagram that belongs to the same owner as the element.
- Remove Element - removes the element that is not displayed in any of the diagrams that belong to the same owner as the element from the Model.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Activity Parameter Node *: Hot Air* and the diagram *Cool Down Cabin Air*belong to the same owner, Activity *Cool Down Cabin Air*, but the Activity Parameter Node *: Hot Air* is not displayed on the diagram *Cool Down Cabin Air*. 
The error is resolved by displaying the Activity Parameter Node *: Hot Air*via the **Display Inner Elements**solver.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>Element(s) not in diagram</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if the element is displayed on the diagram pane when that element and the diagram have the same owner (behavior context). This rule applies to Sequence, Activity, State Machine, and Communication diagrams.</p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning</p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Diagram</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Display Inner Elements </strong>- displays the element that is not displayed in the diagram that belongs to the same owner as the element.</li><li><strong>Remove Element</strong> - removes the element that is not displayed in any of the diagrams that belong to the same owner as the element from the Model.</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element_not_in_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Element not in diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Activity Parameter Node <em>: Hot Air</em> and the diagram <em>Cool Down Cabin Air </em>belong to the same owner, Activity <em>Cool Down Cabin Air</em>, but the Activity Parameter Node <em>: Hot Air</em> is not displayed on the diagram <em>Cool Down Cabin Air</em>. <br />The error is resolved by displaying the Activity Parameter Node <em>: Hot Air </em>via the <strong>Display Inner Elements </strong>solver.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711791 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00504: Element Numbering dialog

- page_id: `136711791`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711791/Element+Numbering+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:55.859+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Generic numbering mechanism
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The**Element Numbering** dialog is designed to number elements by setting the ID, select different numbering schemes, or modify numbering properties defined by the numbering customization. Using this dialog, you can change, remove, and update the element ID, as well as change the number separator, prefix, and the numbering scheme.

Numbering changes you make for the selected element will be applied to all elements owned by the selected element.

To open the**Element Numbering** dialog

1. Select the model elemen t you want to number.
2. Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click **Element Numbering**. The command **Element Numbering** is available, if [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Numbering Customizations' space='MD2024x'] for the selected element type is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Your model is represented in the Model browser on the left side of the dialog. If the element of the type filtered in the**Filter Elements by Element Type** list is selected in the Model browser, owned elements are listed on the right side of the dialog.

In the **Filter Elements by Element Type** list, you can select which type of elements you want to number. The selected element type separator, prefix, and numbering scheme are displayed in the numbering properties list.

The usability of the numbering properties list is described in the following table:

| Item name | Item type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Separator | Text box | Enter your custom separator, which can be any symbol. The separator is changed for all numbers of the selected element type.All previously defined separators will be overridden. |
| Prefix | Text box | Enter your custom prefix, which can be any symbol. Just the one prefix can be entered for the selected numbering scheme. The defined prefix is displayed before the element number.All previously defined prefixes will be overridden. |
| Numbering Scheme | Drop-down list | Select one of the available numbering schemes for the selected element type. Numbering schemes are defined in numbering customization. |
| Renumber from Initial Value | Check box | Select if you want elements to be renumbered from the initial value after you have changed it.Available in the renumbering options area. |

For more information on numbering properties, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating your first numbering customization' space='MD2024x'].

You can have several numbering schemes defined for one type of elements. 
All defined numbering schemes for the selected element type are displayed in the numbered properties list.

The **Element Numbering** dialog buttons are described in the following table.

| Button name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Edit | Click to change the numbering symbol for the selected element. The button is available when the selected element has a number and the element is editable. It has two options:Click Edit to change the last ID symbol of the selected element.Click Set Custom ID to change the whole ID (including the prefix) of the selected element. |
| Create / Remove | The Create button is available if a selected element in the element list is not numbered. Click the Create button to number the selected element. A unique successive number will be created for the selected element.The Remove button is available if a selected element in the element list is numbered and editable. This button has two options:Click Remove to delete the selected element’s number.Click Remove Recursively. Numbers are removed from the selected element and all elements owned by the selected element. |
| Increase | Click to increase an element number.The button is available if the selected element is numbered and editable.The element’s place in the list changes after increasing the number. |
| Decrease | Click to decrease an element number. The button is available if the element is editable, is numbered, and that number is not the first one.The element’s place in the list changes after decreasing the number. |
| Details | Click to open the renumbering options area. |
| Update Last ID | Click to set the maximum ID in the numbered sequence as the last one. |
| Renumber | Click to update the element numbers. The numbering is updated for all elements in the list. |
| Renumber Recursively | Click to update all element numbers recursively. The numbering is updated for all elements in the element list, including their owning elements. |

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df6e716a-8d23-4f91-81bb-7d8841fadaa7"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The<strong> Element Numbering</strong> dialog is designed to number elements by setting the ID, select different numbering schemes, or modify numbering properties defined by the numbering customization. Using this dialog, you can change, remove, and update the element ID, as well as change the number separator, prefix, and the numbering scheme.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f1b4026-cb7e-426a-8e33-d204031b33a4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Numbering changes you make for the selected element will be applied to all elements owned by the selected element.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To open the<strong> Element Numbering</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the model elemen<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">t you want to number.</span></li><li><p>Open the selected element’s shortcut menu and click <strong>Element Numbering</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19231356-1036-4d88-8fcc-174f541184a7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The command <strong>Element Numbering</strong> is available, if <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Numbering Customizations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[numbering customization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the selected element type is created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Element Numbering dialog" ac:alt="Element Numbering dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element_numbering_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Element Numbering dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Your model is represented in the Model browser on the left side of the dialog. If the element of the type filtered in the<strong> Filter Elements by Element Type</strong> list is selected in the Model browser, owned elements are listed on the right side of the dialog.</p><p>In the <strong>Filter Elements by Element Type</strong> list, you can select which type of elements you want to number. The selected element type separator, prefix, and numbering scheme are displayed in the numbering properties list.</p><p>The usability of the numbering properties list is described in the following table:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Item name</th><th>Item type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Separator</td><td>Text box</td><td><p>Enter your custom separator, which can be any symbol. The separator is changed for all numbers of the selected element type.</p><p>All previously defined separators will be overridden.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Prefix</td><td>Text box</td><td><p>Enter your custom prefix, which can be any symbol. Just the one prefix can be entered for the selected numbering scheme. The defined prefix is displayed before the element number.</p><p>All previously defined prefixes will be overridden.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Numbering Scheme</td><td>Drop-down list</td><td><p>Select one of the available numbering schemes for the selected element type. Numbering schemes are defined in numbering customization.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Renumber from Initial Value</td><td colspan="1">Check box</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Select if you want elements to be renumbered from the initial value after you have changed it.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9106b8bd-84fa-4d32-acfa-f92ff79a33d6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available in the renumbering options area.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9de6950-d950-4f1a-9c25-a5ed14ffd383"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information on numbering properties, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating your first numbering customization" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ddc9c3bf-cc3a-481a-b918-5169e5e1e37a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can have several numbering schemes defined for one type of elements.<br />All defined numbering schemes for the selected element type are displayed in the numbered properties list.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>The <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog buttons are described in the following table.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Edit</td><td><p>Click to change the numbering symbol for the selected element. The button is available when the selected element has a number and the element is editable. It has two options:</p><ul><li>Click <strong>Edit</strong> to change the last ID symbol of the selected element.</li><li>Click <strong>Set Custom ID </strong> to change the whole ID (including the prefix) of the selected element.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Create / Remove</td><td><p>The <strong>Create</strong> button is available if a selected element in the element list is not numbered. Click the <strong>Create</strong> button to number the selected element. A unique successive number will be created for the selected element.</p><p>The <strong>Remove</strong> button is available if a selected element in the element list is numbered and editable. This button has two options:</p><ul><li>Click <strong>Remove</strong> to delete the selected element’s number.</li><li>Click <strong>Remove Recursively</strong>. Numbers are removed from the selected element and all elements owned by the selected element.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Increase</td><td><p>Click to increase an element number.</p><p>The button is available if the selected element is numbered and editable.</p><p>The element’s place in the list changes after increasing the number.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Decrease</td><td><p>Click to decrease an element number. The button is available if the element is editable, is numbered, and that number is not the first one.</p><p>The element’s place in the list changes after decreasing the number.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Details</td><td colspan="1">Click to open the renumbering options area.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Update Last ID</td><td colspan="1">Click to set the maximum ID in the numbered sequence as the last one.</td></tr><tr><td>Renumber</td><td><p>Click <span>to update the element numbers. The numbering is updated for all elements in the list.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Renumber Recursively</span></td><td colspan="1">Click <span>to update all element numbers recursively. The numbering is updated for all elements in the element list, including their owning elements.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a8f617d-f9a0-4dc1-ab94-5728ce7190ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e63ac504-51bc-4f1f-b122-416b4516fd14"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with generic numbering mechanism" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715359 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00505: Element Referencing in Texts

- page_id: `136715359`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715359/Element+Referencing+in+Texts
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:41:02.187+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Open API provides the *com.nomagic.magicdraw.elementreferenceintext.ElementReferencingInTexts* class for creating text with element references, setting, and getting text from elements.

#### INFO: Examples

Examples

****You can find the code examples in <*modeling_tool_installation_directory>\openapi\examples\elementreferencingintext.*

##### Creating text that refers an element

To create text that refers an element, use:

- ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(BaseElement, java.lang.String),
- *ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(BaseElement, java.lang.String, DisplayMode[)](http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/elementreferenceintext/ElementReferencingInTexts.html#createReferencingText-com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement-java.lang.String-com.nomagic.magicdraw.elementreferenceintext.DisplayMode-)*,
- ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText (BaseElement, java.lang.String, DisplayMode, UpdateMode) **

```text

```

##### **Setting text with element references**

Some element properties support text with references, e.g., a comment body, constraint specification, opaque behavior body, etc. In order to set the text with element references, you must convert it using *com.nomagic.text.TextUtils.toLightHtml(java.lang.String)*.

```java

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Open API provides the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.elementreferenceintext.ElementReferencingInTexts</em> class for creating text with element references, setting, and getting text from elements.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8115ff91-29d0-40a0-88a0-8ce2d270a13f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Examples</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in &lt;<em>modeling_tool_installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\elementreferencingintext.</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating text that refers an element</h3><p>To create text that refers an element, use:</p><ul><li><em>ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(BaseElement, java.lang.String), </em></li><li><em><em>ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(BaseElement, java.lang.String, DisplayMode<a href="http://jdocs.nomagic.com/2024x/com/nomagic/magicdraw/elementreferenceintext/ElementReferencingInTexts.html#createReferencingText-com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement-java.lang.String-com.nomagic.magicdraw.elementreferenceintext.DisplayMode-">)</a></em>, </em></li><li><em>ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText</em><em>(BaseElement, java.lang.String, DisplayMode, UpdateMode)</em><br /><em><em><br /></em></em></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="73066c2a-f742-4f9f-9f82-237e888ddd4b"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[//creates a text that references an element with default options
ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(element, "Link");

//creates a text that is updated automatically and is displayed using its representation text. 
//This means the elements representation text will be displayed rather than "Link"
ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(element, "Link", DisplayMode.REPRESENTATION_TEXT, 
UpdateMode.AUTOMATIC_UPDATE);

//create a referencing text with a provided display mode and with a default update mode
ElementReferencingInTexts.createReferencingText(element, "Link", DisplayMode.REPRESENTATION_TEXT);

]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><strong>Setting text with element references</strong></h3><p>Some element properties support text with references, e.g., a comment body, constraint specification, opaque behavior body, etc. In order to set the text with element references, you must convert it using <em>com.nomagic.text.TextUtils.toLightHtml(java.lang.String)</em>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a0b83594-eaad-44c1-b5ff-fc3d8884f6be"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Setting text for comment body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[//create a text with an element reference
String constructedText = "This text refers an element: " + createReferencingText(elementToRefer);
//convert it to a light html. Light html can be used in a comment body, constraint specification, etc. to show th etext containing references
String textForCommentBody = TextUtils.toLightHtml(constructedText);

Project project = Project.getProject(comment);
SessionManager.getInstance().createSession(project, "Set comment text with reference");
comment.setBody(textForCommentBody);
SessionManager.getInstance().closeSession(project);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711725 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00506: Element Symbol tab

- page_id: `136711725`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711725/Element+Symbol+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:51.970+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Manage Navigation > Defining hyperlinks > Edit Hyperlink dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Active check box rule

62007185

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Active check box rule

62007187

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Active check box rule

62007186

The**Element/Symbol** tab is dedicated to adding, editing, or deleting hyperlinks to elements in your model.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Element/Symbol tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog where the text "Car" refers to the Car Class from the model. It is represented with an icon and will be automatically updated after changes.

The table below describes the boxes and buttons of the **Element/Symbol** tab.

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Text box (highlighted in orange in the figure) | Shows the text that will be displayed as a hyperlink. It dynamically changes according to the selected option in the Text to display drop-down box and the specified element/symbol in the Select element/symbol or paste element URL (mdel://) box.It is available only when adding a hyperlink in text. |
| Text to display drop-down box | Allows you to select the hyperlink representation options of the specified text in the text box:Name. Displays the element name from its .Representation text. Displays the full element title which is visible in .Icon+Representation text. Displays the element icon together with the full element title which is visible in .Custom text. Allows you to type your own text in the text box; however, then you cannot specify the Update mode option.It is available only when adding a hyperlink in text. |
| Update mode drop-down box | Allows you to select when the hyperlink will be updated if the referenced element and the hyperlink become inconsistent. This option depends on the option selected in the Text to display box and the element type specified in the Select element/symbol or paste element URL (mdel://) box.Automatically updated. Updates the hyperlink text or the referenced element name automatically.Automatically checked. Enables active validation rules that check if the hyperlink text and the referenced element are coherent.Manually checked. Allows you to run validation manually to check if the hyperlink text and the referenced element are coherent.Do not update. Does not update if the hyperlink text and the referenced element name become inconsistent. Validation will not find any inconsistencies.It is available only when adding a hyperlink in text.Conditions of bidirectional update modeIf the hyperlink is inserted on text referring to LiteralString, LiteralInteger, LiteralReal, or LiteralBoolean Value Specification (e.g. default value in Value property), the update is bidirectional (from value to text and from text to value) when the following options are set in the Edit Hyperlink dialog or the Project Options dialog: - the Text to display/Display Mode is set to Representation text or Icon + Representation text. - the Update Mode is set to Automatically updated, Automatically checked, or Manually checked.In all other situations, the update is only one direction: from text to value. |
| Select element/symbol or paste element URL (mdel://) box | Displays the unique URL of the selected element from the Select Model Elements/Symbol dialog. |
| Select from list box | Displays all previously used elements/symbols from your model. You can select them to fill in the Select element/symbol or paste element URL (mdel://) box. |
|  | Opens the the Select Model Elements/Symbol dialog where you can select an element from your model to add it as hyperlink. |
| The Clear button | Deletes all previously selected elements/symbols from the Select from list box. |
| The Active check box | Activates the hyperlink if selected.It is available only when adding a hyperlink on an element or entire diagram.Active check box ruleOnly one hyperlink can be active. By default the last added hyperlink is active. |

62007183

**Related pages**

3[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='']

- Managing hyperlinks
- Linking model elements to OSLC resources

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdc4d8df-3274-406b-ab94-b7d107d4a33e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">62007185</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b4cb2c4e-5c55-4a55-a236-0427fe8fd0e5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">62007187</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9de6929c-c36e-427c-be8d-db075727f70b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">62007186</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>The<strong> Element/Symbol</strong> tab is dedicated to adding, editing, or deleting hyperlinks to elements in your model.<br class="inline-comment-marker" /></span></span></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_hyperlink_element_symbol_tab.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Element Symbol tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>The Element/Symbol tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog where the text &quot;Car&quot; refers to the Car Class from the model. It is represented with an icon and will be automatically updated after changes.</h6><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><br /></span></span></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>The table below describes the boxes and buttons of the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><strong>Element/Symbol</strong> tab</span></span></span>.</span></span></span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;"><strong>Text</strong> box (highlighted in orange in the figure)</td><td style="text-align: center;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p style="text-align: left;">Shows the text that will be displayed as a hyperlink. It dynamically changes according to the selected option in the <strong>Text to display</strong> drop-down box and the specified element/symbol in the <strong>Select element/symbol or paste element URL (mdel://)</strong> box.</p><p style="text-align: left;">It is available only when adding a hyperlink in text.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Text to display</strong> drop-down box</td><td>Allows you to select the hyperlink representation options of the specified text in the text box:<ul><li><strong>Name</strong>. D<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">isplays the element name from its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><strong>Representation text</strong>. D<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">isplays the full element title which is visible in <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</span></li><li><strong>Icon+Representation text</strong><em>.</em> Displays the element icon together with <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">the full element title which is visible in <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></span></span>.</li><li><strong>Custom text</strong>. Allows you to <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">type</span> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">your own text in the text box; however, then you cannot specify the <strong>Update mode</strong> option.</span></span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">It is available only when adding a hyperlink in text.<br /></span></span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Update mode</strong> drop-down box</td><td><div class="content-wrapper">Allows you to select when the hyperlink will be updated if the referenced element and the hyperlink become inconsistent.<span> This option depends on the option selected in the <strong>Text to display</strong> box and the element type specified in the <strong>Select element/symbol or paste element URL (mdel://)</strong> box.</span><ul><li><strong>Automatically updated</strong>. Updates the hyperlink text or the referenced element name automatically.</li><li><strong>Automatically checked</strong>. Enables active validation rules that check if the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="992307b7-b372-4f38-8acc-5cbe638dd296">hyperlink</ac:inline-comment-marker> text and the referenced element are coherent.</li><li><strong>Manually checked</strong>. Allows you to run validation manually to check if the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8e65dc0e-5f2e-449a-97fa-d44b389a6c17">hyperlink</ac:inline-comment-marker> text and the referenced element are coherent.</li><li><strong>Do not update</strong>. Does not update if the hyperlink text and the referenced element name become inconsistent. Validation will not find any inconsistencies.</li></ul><p>It is available only when adding a hyperlink in text.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f609a5b-b916-4a49-8b69-319ea6a839c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Conditions of bidirectional update mode</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the hyperlink is inserted on text referring to LiteralString, LiteralInteger, LiteralReal, or LiteralBoolean Value Specification (e.g. default value in <strong>Value</strong> property), the update is bidirectional (from value to text and from text to value) when the following options are set in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Edit Hyperlink dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Edit Hyperlink</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="86de06d3-b447-4deb-9059-781952933dca">or the </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Default settings of hyperlinks to model elements" /><ac:link-body><strong><span class="inline-comment-marker">Project Options</span></strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>:<br /> - the <strong>Text to display</strong>/<strong>Display Mode</strong> is set to <strong>Representation text</strong> or <strong>Icon + Representation text</strong>.<br /> - the <strong>Update Mode</strong> is set to <strong>Automatically updated</strong>, <strong>Automatically checked</strong>, or <strong>Manually checked</strong>.</p><p>In all other situations, the update is only one direction: from text to value.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Select element/symbol or paste element URL (mdel://)</strong> box</td><td><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Displays the unique URL of the selected element from the <strong>Select Model Elements/Symbol</strong> dialog.</span></span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Select from list</strong> box</td><td>Displays all previously used elements/symbols from your model. You can select them to fill in the <strong>Select element/symbol or paste element URL (mdel://)</strong> box.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="27"><ri:attachment ri:filename="go_to_hyperlink_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Element Symbol tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Opens the the <strong>Select Model Elements/Symbol</strong> dialog where you can select an element from your model to add it as hyperlink.</td></tr><tr><td>The <strong>Clear</strong> button</td><td>Deletes all previously selected elements/symbols from the <strong>Select from list</strong> box.</td></tr><tr><td>The <strong>Active</strong> check box</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Activates the hyperlink if selected.</p><p>It is available only when adding a hyperlink on an element or entire diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f140e2ee-0594-4348-b022-a9ffbd7ca9e2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Active check box rule</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only one hyperlink can be active. By default the last added hyperlink is active.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c52fdd74-896f-4896-b20d-03e995f8fd7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">62007183</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="cf3987bf-6e19-4dc8-b94c-79f24adc3246"><ac:parameter ac:name="depth">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Defining hyperlinks" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Managing+hyperlinks" rel="nofollow">Managing hyperlinks</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Linking+model+elements+to+OSLC+resources" class="current" rel="nofollow">Linking model elements to OSLC resources</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712711 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00507: Element usage in diagrams

- page_id: `136712711`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712711/Element+usage+in+diagrams
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T15:04:53.550+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Additional information

1181916137

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Additional information

1181916139

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Additional information

1181916138

An element symbol is a visual representation of a model element in a diagram. There are two types of symbols: shapes and paths. In a diagram, all types of relationships are represented as paths. Model elements that are not relationships are represented as shapes.

No Magic modeling tools allow you to see element usages in diagrams by listing all diagrams that display the symbols of a particular model element. You can access the diagrams in which an element is used from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] of that element, the element's shortcut menu, or the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Main menu' space='MD2024x'].

#### TIP: Additional information

Additional information

For more information about element usages and relations between model elements, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing usages and dependencies' space='MD2024x'].

To search for element usage in diagrams from the Specification window

1. Double-click the desired element to open its Specification window.
2. On the left side of the Specification window, in the property group list, select the Usage in Diagrams node. The area on the right side of the window shows all diagrams in which the element is represented as a symbol. To list other types of diagrams (e.g., Generic Table) the selected element is used in, click the Check Other Diagrams button.
3. Depending on what you want to do, complete one of the following actions:
  - To open one of the listed diagrams, select the desired diagram and click the Open button or double-click the desired diagram. Alternatively, you can right-click the diagram and select Open from the drop-down menu
  - To open the Specification window of one of the listed diagrams, click [ATTACHMENT filename='open_specification.png'] near the desired diagram in the list.

If you open a diagram (described in the first option of step 3), the symbol of the related element is selected in that diagram. If the diagram includes several symbols of the same element, all of those symbols are selected in the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Specification window of a model element displaying all the diagrams it is used in.

To search for element usage in diagrams from the element’s shortcut menu

1. Right-click either the desired element in the model browser or its symbol in a diagram.
2. From the element’s shortcut menu, select Go To > Usage in Diagrams .
3. Select either Diagrams with Symbols or Other Diagrams .
4. Depending on what you want to do, complete one of the following actions:
  - To open one of the listed diagrams, select the desired diagram.
  - To open all listed diagrams, click Open All Diagrams .

Depending on the action you performed in step 3, one or all of the diagrams in which the related element is used are opened with the selected element symbol(s).

To search for element usage in diagrams from the main menu

1. Select either the desired element in the model browser or its symbol in a diagram.
2. In the main menu, select Analyze > Go To > Usage in Diagrams .
3. Select either Diagrams with Symbols or Other Diagrams .
4. Depending on what you want to do, complete one of the following actions:
  - To open one of the listed diagrams, select the desired diagram.
  - To open all listed diagrams, click Open All Diagrams .

The selected diagram or all of the diagrams in which the related element is used are opened with the element symbol(s) selected.

1183619084

##### Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model analysis' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711760 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00508: Elements multiple selection

- page_id: `136711760`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711760/Elements+multiple+selection
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:42:21.025+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Selecting elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

402498987

402498990

402498989

The **Multiple Selection** button is available only when it is allowed to select more than one element.

To select more than one element

1. Click the Multiple Selection button. The Selected elements area appears on the right side of the element selection dialog.
2. In the model tree or list, select one or more elements at a time by holding the Ctrl or Shift keys.
3. Move selected elements to the Selected elements area.

To return to the single selection

- Click the Single Selection button.

When more than one element is selected in the tree or in the list in the multiple selection mode, the selection will be reduced to one element (the first one based on alphabetical order), when switching to the single selection mode.

402498986

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136713431 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00509: Enabling 3DEXPERIENCE collaboration in a modeling tool

- page_id: `136713431`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136713431/Enabling+3DEXPERIENCE+collaboration+in+a+modeling+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:15:35.306+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To start collaborating on projects in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, first you need to enable the collaboration environment powered by the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform in your modeling tool.

If you launch your modeling tool through the platform, these steps are not necessary because you will be automatically logged in to the platform.

To enable collaboration powered by the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

1. In the main menu of your modeling tool, select Options > Environment .
2. On the left side of the Environment Options dialog, select the Collaboration option group.
3. In the option specification pane, set the Enable Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform option to true . [ATTACHMENT filename='collaboration_in_environment_options.png']
4. Click OK to close the Environment Options dialog.
5. Click OK in the message dialog that pops up: [ATTACHMENT filename='environment_options_message.png']
6. Restart the modeling tool to apply changes.

After enabling the collaboration environment, you can log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform and start a collaboration session.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To start collaborating on projects in the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7ec893ba-977d-4ca6-946b-10f51b726632"><strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE</ac:inline-comment-marker> platform, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7d1d0d4c-e1ae-4456-a31e-32ff39e8cdaf">first you need to enable</ac:inline-comment-marker> the collaboration environment powered by the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform in your modeling tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a37167b5-86fb-4633-8209-9aa1dd83d319"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If you launch your modeling tool through the platform, these steps are not necessary because you will be automatically logged in to the platform.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To enable collaboration powered by the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of your modeling tool, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>On the left side of the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Collaboration</strong> option group.</li><li>In the option specification pane, set the <strong>Enable Collaboration Powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform</strong> option to <em>true</em>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaboration_in_environment_options.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Enabling 3DEXPERIENCE collaboration in a modeling tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> in the message dialog that pops up: <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="environment_options_message.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Enabling 3DEXPERIENCE collaboration in a modeling tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Restart the modeling tool to apply changes.</li></ol><p><br />After enabling the collaboration environment, you can log in to the <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform and start a collaboration session.</p>
````

## PAGE 00510: Enabling and disabling warning messages

- page_id: `136714389`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714389/Enabling+and+disabling+warning+messages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:29.934+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Debug report template
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can enable or disable any invalid properties, references, and exception messages except invalid syntax messages by editing the **config.xml** file or through Report Environment options. Invalid syntax messages are always enabled.

##### Enabling or disabling warning messages by editing the **config.xml** file

To enable or disable a warning message by editing the **config.xml** file, do one of the following

- Edit the config.xml file in the ...\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\data folder to enable or disable all templates’ warning messages.
- Edit the config.xml file and create a file in the ...\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\data\templatefolder folder to enable or disable each template's warning message.

For example, to enable or disable a warning message of the Class Specification report, you need to edit the **config.xml** file in the *...\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\data\Class Specification Report*folder.

**Adding tags and values to enable or disable warning messages**

The table below lists the warning message types and their tags.

| Warning message type | Tag |
| --- | --- |
| Invalid property | <template.warn.invalid.property> |
| Invalid reference | <template.warn.invalid.reference> |
| Invalid method reference | <template.warn.invalid.method> |
| Exception | <template.warn.exception> |

To enable or disable a warning message by adding its tags and values in the **config.xml** file

1. Apply the following XML with a warning message type and its tags: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Set the boolean value to true to enable the warning message or false to disable the warning message.

An example of how to enable an invalid reference warning message of the Class Specification report is shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Editing the config.xml file with tags and values to enable an invalid reference warning message of the Class Specification report.

##### Enabling or disabling warning messages through the Report Environment options

To enable or disable a warning message through the Report Environment options

1. On the main menu, select Options > Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. In the left pane, click Report Wizard .
3. Set the value of the following properties in the table under the **Configure report properties** section to**true** to enable the warning message or **false** to disable the warning message. **Property****Description**template.warn.invalid.propertyEnable or disable the invalid property warning message.template.warn.invalid.referenceEnable or disable the invalid reference warning message.template.warn.invalid.methodEnable or disable the invalid method reference.template.warn.exceptionEnable or disable the exception message. [ATTACHMENT filename='Report Wizard Environment Options Dialog.png']
4. Click OK to apply the updates.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

To allow the updates set in the Report Wizard **Environment Options** dialog to take effect, you must close the UI before running Report command line.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710772 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00511: Enabling or disabling diagram aspects option

- page_id: `136710772`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710772/Enabling+or+disabling+diagram+aspects+option
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:00.530+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Use Aspects**option allows you to use definable diagram aspects in your project. By default, its value is *false* in the **Project Options** dialog. The procedures below describe how to enable/disable the diagram aspects option in your project.

To enable/disable the diagram aspects option in your project

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, expand the General property group, and select Diagrams .
3. On the right side of the Project Options dialog, set the Use Aspects option value to true to enable the functionality, false - to disable it.
4. Click OK .

After enabling the diagram aspect functionality, you are able to create new aspect diagrams, and apply aspects for preexisting diagrams. The following procedures describe how to perform these functions:

- Specifying or removing ignored aspects
- Finding predefined diagram aspects
- Creating custom aspects
- Creating an aspect diagram
- Applying or removing aspect for preexisting diagrams
- Navigation among aspect diagrams
- Displaying parts and ports according to aspects

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Use Aspects </strong>option allows you to use definable diagram aspects in your project. By default, its value is <em>false</em> in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog. The procedures below describe how to enable/disable the diagram aspects option in your project.</p><p>To enable/disable the diagram aspects option in your project</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, expand the <strong>General</strong> property group, and select <strong>Diagrams</strong>.</li><li>On the right side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, set the <strong>Use Aspects </strong>option value to <em>true</em> to enable the functionality, <em>false</em> - to disable it.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>After enabling the diagram aspect functionality, you are able to create new aspect diagrams, and apply aspects for preexisting diagrams. The following procedures describe how to perform these functions:</p><ul class="list-children not-expandable"><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Specifying+or+removing+ignored+aspects">Specifying or removing ignored aspects</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Finding+predefined+diagram+aspects">Finding predefined diagram aspects</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Creating+custom+aspects">Creating custom aspects</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Creating+an+aspect+diagram">Creating an aspect diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Applying+or+removing+aspect+for+preexisting+diagrams">Applying or removing aspect for preexisting diagrams</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Navigation+among+aspect+diagrams">Navigation among aspect diagrams</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Displaying+parts+and+ports+according+to+aspects">Displaying parts and ports according to aspects</a></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709365 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00512: Enterprise Architect Import

- page_id: `136709365`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709365/Enterprise+Architect+Import
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:45.771+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats
- labels: ['enterprise-architect-import', 'ea-import', 'importing-xmi']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

478499527

478499529

478499528

The Enterprise Architect Import has the functionality to import UML models conforming to various XMI versions (including XMI 2.1) from Sparx Systems Enterprise Architect (EA), a modeling and visualization tool based on the UML 2.3 standard. EA has the ability to import and export XMI compliant models, so you can use EA to import UML2.1(XMI2.1). However, XMI models exported from EA contain some XMI conflicts and EA-specific data that do not conform to UML standards.

Enterprise Architect Import allows you to migrate XMI models from EA to a modeling tool flawlessly by using an additional transformation process with a set of mapping rules.

The main purpose of Enterprise Architect Import is to help users who need to import models from EA by managing conflicts that can cause problems while loading the XMI models in the modeling tool, as well as transforming some EA-specific data into UML elements with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x'].

Enterprise Architect Import supports Enterprise Architect Versions 7.1, 7.5, and 8.0 (most of the testing procedures performed on EA 7.1.833 and EA 7.5.847). The feature helps you import and transform an EA exported XMI using the UML2.1 (XMI2.1) option into a model file (*.mdxml).

In addition to the ability to import model elements, the feature also allows for the import of diagrams. The feature supports:

- Class diagrams
- Package diagrams
- Object diagrams
- Component diagrams
- Deployment diagrams
- Use Case diagrams
- Activity diagrams
- Sequence diagrams
- Communication diagrams
- StateMachine diagrams
- CompositeStructure diagrams
- InteractionOverview diagrams

478499525

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024x']

EA has the ability to import and export XMI compliant models, so you can use EA to import UML2.1(XMI2.1). However, XMI models exported from EA contain some XMI conflicts and EA-specific data that do not conform to UML standards.</p><p>Enterprise Architect Import allows you to migrate XMI models from EA to a modeling tool flawlessly by using an additional transformation process with a set of mapping rules.</p><p>The main purpose of Enterprise Architect Import is to help users who need to import models from EA by managing conflicts that can cause problems while loading the XMI models in the modeling tool, as well as transforming some EA-specific data into UML elements with <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>Enterprise Architect Import supports Enterprise Architect Versions 7.1, 7.5, and 8.0 (most of the testing procedures performed on EA 7.1.833 and EA 7.5.847). The feature helps you import and transform an EA exported XMI using the UML2.1 (XMI2.1) option into a model file (*.mdxml).</p><p>In addition to the ability to import model elements, the feature also allows for the import of diagrams. The feature supports:</p><ul><li>Class diagrams </li><li>Package diagrams </li><li>Object diagrams</li><li>Component diagrams</li><li>Deployment diagrams</li><li>Use Case diagrams </li><li>Activity diagrams</li><li>Sequence diagrams</li><li>Communication diagrams</li><li>StateMachine diagrams</li><li>CompositeStructure diagrams</li><li>InteractionOverview diagrams</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9660b0ef-a2f2-4182-a09f-f9b5a0db7c40"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">478499525</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711322 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00513: Entry Point

- page_id: `136711322`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711322/Entry+Point
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:32.402+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An entry point connection point reference as the target of a transition implies that the target of the transition is the entry point pseudo state as defined in the submachine of the submachine state. As a result, the regions of the submachine state machine are entered at the corresponding entry point pseudo states.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710987 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00514: Enumeration

- page_id: `136710987`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710987/Enumeration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:10.637+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Data Type
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Enumeration defines a kind of data type whose range is a list of predefined values called Enumeration Literals. An Enumeration can contain Operations, but they must be pure functions (this is the rule for all data type elements).

An Enumeration can be specified by changing its property values in the Enumeration [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

##### Enumeration Literals

Enumeration Literal defines an element of the run-time extension of an Enumeration data type. It has no relevant substructure, therefore, it is atomic.

To create an Enumeration Literal, do one of the following

- Select the Enumeration shape, click the Create Element button [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and then select **Enumeration Literal**.
- In the Containment tree, right-click the Enumeration and select Create Element > Enumeration Literal .

##### Enumeration Attributes

To create an Attribute for the Enumeration, do one of the following

- Select the Enumeration shape, click the Create Element button [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and then select **Property**.
- In the Containment tree, right-click the Enumeration and select Create Element > Property .

As you can see, the Attribute is by default read-only.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Type' space='MD2024x']
