<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715276 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00516: Environment Options

- page_id: `136715276`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715276/Environment+Options
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-02-13T08:33:21.781+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Application-related options are referred as environment options. If you are using MagicDraw, they are saved in the *global.opt*file that is located in*<user home directory>\AppData\Local\.magicdraw\<version number>\data.*You can find this file for other modeling tools using the same location pattern.

You can add custom environment options for your modeling tool.

To add your own environment options

1. Extend the com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.options.AbstractPropertyOptionsGroup class.
2. Add the extending class to application environment options.

###### **Example of adding custom environment options**

```java

```

###### **Example of accessing environment options**

```java

```

****You can find the code examples in

- <installation_directory>\openapi\examples\environmentoptions

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Application-related options are referred as environment options. If you are using MagicDraw, they are saved in the <em>global.opt </em>file that is located in<em> &lt;user home directory&gt;\AppData\Local\.magicdraw\&lt;version number&gt;\data. </em>You can find this file for other modeling tools using the same location pattern. </p><p>You can add custom environment options for your modeling tool.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add your own environment options</p><hr /><ol><li>Extend the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.options.AbstractPropertyOptionsGroup</em> class.</li><li>Add the extending class to application environment options.</li></ol><h4><strong>Example of adding custom environment options</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92870dc5-f5fc-4dc4-a0e0-eecab4ec361b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	class MyOptionsGroup extends AbstractPropertyOptionsGroup
    {
        ...
    }
    Application application = Application.getInstance(); 
    EnvironmentOptions options = application.getEnvironmentOptions(); 
    options.addGroup(new MyOptionsGroup());]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Example of accessing environment options</strong></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3e6aa9b-33ff-47cc-bd74-07c542fc3afb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">java</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	Application application = Application.getInstance(); 
	EnvironmentOptions options = application.getEnvironmentOptions(); 
	int imageDpi = options.getGeneralOptions().getImageResolutionDpi();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149c6619-33cd-40ac-aa63-ffaeb054ed5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong> </strong>You can find the code examples in</p><ul><li><em>&lt;installation_directory&gt;\openapi\examples\environmentoptions</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712124 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00517: Evaluating operation performance

- page_id: `136712124`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712124/Evaluating+operation+performance
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:10.645+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

33447654

33447665

33455046

**On this page**

33447655

You can use the Evaluate Operation Performance functionality to see the performance diagnostics that help determine the reasons behind degraded tool performanceif it is caused by the execution of expressions (e.g., derived properties, smart packages, legends, validation rules) defined in the model. Matrix and table building can also be analyzed.

700349

##### Evaluating operation performance

To evaluate operation performance

1. In the Main menu, click **Tools** and select **Evaluate Operation Performance**.
2. Click the **Start**button.
3. Run an operation whose performance you want to evaluate.
4. Click the **Stop**button when the operation is finished and you want to stop the evaluation.The performance diagnostics are displayed in the dialog, indicating:
  - The time taken to execute an expression in percentages.
  - The time taken to execute an expression in milliseconds.
  - The number of times the expression was executed.
  - The name of the executed expression.
  - The type of the executed expression. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Evaluate Operation Performance dialog buttons

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Start the evaluation. |
|  | Stop the evaluation. |
|  | Expand the selected result recursively. |
|  | Collapse the selected result recursively. |
|  | Add the element associated with the tested expression to the search results tab. |
|  | Select the element associated with the tested expression in the Containment tree. |
|  | Export the diagnostics to a log file. |

##### Evaluate Operation Performance example

For example, you noticed that opening a particular diagram took longer than expected. A progress bar titled "Adorning" popped up for a few seconds before the diagram opened. To know what caused this performance issue, run the Evaluate Operation Performance tool. In its dialog, clickthe 'Start' button, then open the diagram again reproducing the slow operation, and once it is done, click the 'Stop' button to see the results.You can use this information to investigate the particular expression and improve its efficiency.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ri:filename="Evaluating_operation_performance_button_stop.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Evaluating operation performance" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Stop the evaluation.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Evaluating_operation_performance_button_expand.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" colspan="1">Export the diagnostics to a log file.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Evaluate Operation Performance e</span>xample</h3><p><span>For example, you noticed that opening a particular diagram took longer than expected. A progress bar titled &quot;Adorning&quot; popped up for a few seconds before the diagram opened. To know what caused this performance issue, run the Evaluate Operation Performance tool. In its dialog, click </span><span>the 'Start' button, t</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">hen open the diagram again reproducing the slow operation, and o</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">nce it is done, click the 'Stop' button to see the results. </span><span>You can use this information to investigate the particular expression and improve its efficiency.</span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Evaluating_operation_performance.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Evaluating operation performance" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711017 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00518: Event

- page_id: `136711017`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711017/Event
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:12.576+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An event is the specification of some occurrence that may potentially trigger effects by an object, that is, an event shows what should happen to change a particular [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MD2024x']in a system. There are the following event types:

Events are important in diagrams which represent a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='MD2024x']of a system. These diagrams are listed in the following table.

| Diagram name | Event usage description |
| --- | --- |
|  | To specify an event type for the . |
|  | To specify a event type for the , or transition to self. |
|  | To specify an event type for the protocol transition, or protocol transition to self. |

When specifying an event type for a transition, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Event Type' space='MD2024x'] on the diagram pane. The same assignment is valid for a transition to self, protocol transition, and protocol transition to self.

Event types, their functions, samples, and command syntax are described in the following table.

| Name | Function and Sample | Command syntax |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Any Receive Event | A trigger for an AnyReceiveEvent is triggered by the receipt of any that is not explicitly handled by any related trigger. | all |
| Call Event | A call event specifies the receipt by an object of a invoking a call of an :In this example, the call event type is specified with the create() operation. | <operation ()> |
| Change Event | A change event specifies a change in the system configuration that makes a condition true:In this example, the change event type is specified, and its Change expression property is specified as phone is busy. | when (<expression>) |
| Signal Event | A signal event represents the receipt of an asynchronous signal instance. A signal event may, for example, cause a statemachine to trigger a transition:In this example, the signal event type is specified, and its Signal property is specified as phone answered. | <signal name> |
| Time Event | A time event specifies a point in time. At the specified time, the event occurs. There are two possible types of eventoccurrences: at the relative time and at the absolute time:In this example, the relative time event is specified, and its When property is specified as 90 sec.For more information, see . | after (<time>) - an event occurrence at the relative timeat (<time>) - an event occurrence at the absolute time |

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol State Machine diagram' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MD2024x']

The same assignment is valid for a transition to self, protocol transition, and protocol transition to self.</p><p>Event types, their functions, samples, and command syntax are described in the following table.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Function and Sample</th><th>Command syntax</th></tr><tr><td><h4>Any Receive Event</h4></td><td><p>A trigger for an AnyReceiveEvent is triggered by the receipt of any <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>that is not explicitly handled by any related trigger.</p></td><td><em>all</em></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Call Event</h4></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>A call event specifies the receipt by an object of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>invoking a call of an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[operation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="event5.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Event" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In this example, the call event type is specified with the <em>create()</em> operation.</p></div></td><td><em>&lt;operation ()&gt;</em></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Change property is specified as <em>phone is busy</em>.</p></div></td><td><em>when (&lt;expression&gt;)</em></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Signal Event</h4></td><td><div class="content-wrapper">A signal event represents the receipt of an asynchronous signal instance. A signal event may, for example, cause a state<p>machine to trigger a transition:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="event8.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Event" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In this example, the signal event type is specified, and its Signal property is specified as <em>phone answered</em>.</p></div></td><td><em>&lt;signal name&gt;</em></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Time Event</h4></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>A time event specifies a point in time. At the specified time, the event occurs. There are two possible types of event<br />occurrences: at the relative time and at the absolute time:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="event11.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Event" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In this example, the relative time event is specified, and its When property is specified as <em>90 sec</em>.</p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specifying a time for a time event" /></ac:link>.</p></div></td><td><ul><li><em>after (&lt;time&gt;)</em> - an event occurrence at the relative time</li><li><em>at (&lt;time&gt;)</em> - an event occurrence at the absolute time</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b6cf08a-97eb-41d3-bb72-a527d2b25d9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="01b10abf-8c58-4f1a-bc27-6b5cc64511ee" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Protocol State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715172 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00519: Event support

- page_id: `136715172`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715172/Event+support
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:10.830+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > UML model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tools provide an API for listening to events while changing a model. There is a possibility either to get an event immediately after the property has been changed, or get the event about all the changes in the session, after this session has been closed.

There are four different listener registration types:

- The w hole repository listener. You will get events about the changes in all the elements.
- The specific element listener. You will get events about the changes in any property of this element.
- The e lement’s specific property listener. You will get events about the changes of this property.
- The s pecific element type listener. You will get events about the changes in all the elements of the specific metatype.

The transaction listener is notified, when all the changes within the session are done and the session is closed.

You can find the code examples in *<program installation directory>\openapi\examples\events*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

There is a possibility either to get an event immediately after the property has been changed, or get the event about all the changes in the session, after this session has been closed.</p><p>There are four different listener registration types:</p><ul><li><strong>The w</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">hole repository listener. </strong>You will get events about the changes in all the elements.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">The specific element listener. </strong>You will get events about the changes in any property of this element.</li><li><strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">The e</span></strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">lement’s specific property listener. </strong>You will get events about the changes of this property.</li><li><strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">The s</span></strong><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">pecific element type listener. </strong>You will get events about the changes in all the elements of the specific metatype.</li></ul><p>The transaction listener is notified, when all the changes within the session are done and the session is closed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48621266-8f0c-4295-8e01-7b9597d76cbe"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can find the code examples in <em>&lt;program installation directory&gt;\openapi\examples\events</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4c86826-2597-4896-87f5-24fa8306eafa"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="44cd2443-8cac-4efc-80ef-0eddee8e5215"><ac:parameter <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715328 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00520: Example

- page_id: `136715328`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715328/Example
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:17:22.813+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Developer Guide > Oracle DDL generation and customization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this sample, we will extend the current Oracle View DROP statement. In the Default template, we have the Oracle view drop function. In this sample, the simple macro is presented, and it generates the following text:

```text

```

See the following sample:

```text

```

We will add a new tag to identify the “CASCADE CONSTRAINTS” clause in the DROP VIEW statement. The following script will be generated:

```text

```

There are three steps to do this:

###### **Step #1. Creating a new stereotype and tags**

We need to create a new stereotype with the Boolean property, or extend the default “View” stereotype with a new property. Let's name a new property “cascade_clause”, apply the stereotype to the View object, and set value to *true*.

We added a new tag to the view stereotype in the default profile.

###### ****

###### **Step #2. Changing the template file**

Add the following lines to the template file:

```text

```

This line retrieves a boolean value from the given tag in the given stereotype “$view_stereotype” and sets it to a newly created $cascadeOption value.

Then add a new checking clause into the drop statement. See the final method:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>In this sample, we will extend the current Oracle View DROP statement. In the Default template, we have the Oracle view drop function. In this sample, the simple macro is presented, and it generates the following text:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff98097b-ec0f-47e5-85e0-4b8dd4de7aab"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    DROP VIEW view_name;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>See the following sample:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd214291-a8a7-4b6a-ac42-7b667c2851ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    #macro (dropView $data )
    #writeLine( "DROP VIEW $utils.getQualifiedName( $data )${semicolon}" $nospace)
    #end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>We will add a new tag to identify the “CASCADE CONSTRAINTS” clause in the DROP VIEW statement. The following script will be generated:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a21e59ae-3cde-48a1-9ae4-018ec906ba60"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ 	DROP VIEW view_name CASCADE CONSTRAINTS;]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>There are three steps to do this:</p><h4><strong>Step #1. Creating a new stereotype and tags</strong></h4><p>We need to create a new stereotype with the Boolean property, or extend the default “View” stereotype with a new property. Let's name a new property “cascade_clause”, apply the stereotype to the View object, and set value to <em>true</em>.</p><p>We added a new tag to the view stereotype in the default profile.</p><h4><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;"><br /></strong></h4><h4><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Step #2. Changing the template file</strong></h4><p>Add the following lines to the template file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="294b4f13-dfa4-4d76-a9ed-8674cea596bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    #set($cascadeOption =false)
    ##this line is required to for setting a new variable to the false state.
    #set($cascadeOption = $oracleHelper.getBooleanValueFromDefaultProfile($data,$VIEW_STEREOTYPE, "cascade_clause") )]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>This line retrieves a boolean value from the given tag in the given stereotype “$view_stereotype” and sets it to a newly created $cascadeOption value.</p><p>Then add a new checking clause into the drop statement. See the final method:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="028221c4-31db-4949-ab08-f30891c9f943"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    #macro (dropView $data )
    ## sets a value to a new variable
    #set($cascadeOption = $oracleHelper.getBooleanValueFromDefaultProfile($data,$VIEW_STEREOTYPE, "cascade_clause") )
    #writeLine( "DROP VIEW $utils.getQualifiedName( $data )
    #if ($cascadeOption) CASCADE CONSTRAINTS
    #end${semicolon}" $nospace)
    #end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714506 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00521: Examples

- page_id: `136714506`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714506/Examples
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:38.494+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Query tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section provides three sets of sample code to demonstrate some scenarios with the query tool. The codes may not be executable or return a correct result. They are just examples to show you how to use the Query tool and how it works.

**(i) Example 1**

Print any classes whose containing in package com.nomagic.reportwizard.

Without QueryTool:

```text

```

With QueryTool:

```text

```

**(ii) Example 2**

Print any classes whose containing in model Design and name ends with Bean.

Without QueryTool:

```text

```

With QueryTool:

```text

```

**(iii) Example 3**

Matches any property element which owner element is class whose name attributes value is exactly equal to foo.

Without QueryTool:

```text

```

With QueryTool:

```text
property"))
  $p.name
#end]]>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This section provides three sets of sample code to demonstrate some scenarios with the query tool. The codes may not be executable or return a correct result. They are just examples to show you how to use the Query tool and how it works.</p><p><strong>(i) Example 1</strong></p><p>Print any classes whose containing in package com.nomagic.reportwizard.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Without QueryTool:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e738701c-e2d0-4431-8719-81963989d573"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($c in $Class)
  #if ($c.qualifiedName.startsWith("com.nomagic.reportwizard"))
    $c.name
  #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">With QueryTool:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a922019-5e2d-4cc6-a459-fca60e4a7bdc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import("query", "com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.QueryTool")
#foreach ($c in $query.get("class[qualifiedName^=com.nomagic.reportwizard]"))
  $c.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>(ii) Example 2</strong></p><p>Print any classes whose containing in model Design and name ends with Bean.</p><p>Without QueryTool:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="803d35cf-98e7-401a-b6d0-d9c52d12f5c6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($match = false)
#macro (recursiveCheck $e)
  #if ($e.elementType == "model" && e.name == "Design")
    #set ($match = true)
  #else
    #recursiveCheck($e.owner)
  #end
#end
 
#foreach ($c in $Class)
  #set ($match = false)
  #recursiveCheck($c)
  #if ($check)
    #if ($c.name.endsWith("Bean"))
      $c.name
    #end
  #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>With QueryTool:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9dbc818-3887-4f47-b26a-2d4f1fe87dbb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import("query", "com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.QueryTool")
 
#foreach ($c in $query.get("model[name=Design] class[name$=Bean]"))
  $c.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>(iii) Example 3</strong></p><p>Matches any property element which owner element is class whose name attributes value is exactly equal to foo.</p><p>Without QueryTool:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d3daedf-ec41-4367-9c26-fc8806f22844"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($p in $Property)
  #set ($owner = $p.owner)
  #if ($owner.elementType == "class" && $owner.name == "foo")
      $p.name
  #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>With QueryTool:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0014fd1e-a197-4487-8d57-d742ca732179"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import("query", "com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.QueryTool")
 
#foreach ($p in $query.get("class[name=foo] > property"))
  $p.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136714500 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00522: Examples of dependency matrix tool

- page_id: `136714500`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714500/Examples+of+dependency+matrix+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:38.186+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Dependency Matrix tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The code in this section demonstrates some scenarios with using the Dependency Matrix tool.

For not empty cells, prints the row element name, column element name and the name of dependency inside the intersecting cell

```text

```

Counts the number of relations for each column.

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The code in this section demonstrates some scenarios with using the Dependency Matrix tool.</p><p>For not empty cells, prints the row element name, column element name and the name of dependency inside the intersecting cell</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ed1738e6-8664-46d6-947b-14b0bce71753"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $project.getDiagrams("Dependency Matrix"))
 #set ($matrix = $depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))
 #foreach ($row in $matrix.getRowNodes())
 #foreach ($col in $matrix.getColumnNodes())
 #foreach ($rel in $matrix.getCellDependencies($row, $col))
 $row.element.name has $rel.name with $col.element.name
 #end
 #end
 #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Counts the number of relations for each column.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3db5ce61-0646-4e19-a5cf-541c358b4a50"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $project.getDiagrams("Dependency Matrix"))
#set ($matrix = $depmatrix.getMatrix($diagram))
#foreach ($col in $matrix.getColumnNodes())
#set ($count = 0)
#foreach ($row in $matrix.getRowNodes())
#set ($numberOfrelation = $matrix.getCellDependencies($row, $col).size())
#set ($count = $count + $numberOfrelation)
#end
$col.element.name has $count relations
#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711491 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00523: Excel and CSV Import dialog

- page_id: `136711491`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711491/Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:43.180+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Importing data from Excel or CSV files
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1960831571

1960831575

1960831574

**On this page**

5

1960831570

##### Introduction

The**Excel/CSV Import**dialog allows you to specify settings before importing content from an Excel/CSV file to the model. This mechanism allows you to create mapping between an Excel or CSV file columnand target element properties. Once you specify mapping options, you can save your map for reuse or import it as an existing one.

##### Opening the Excel/CSV Import dialog

To open the **Excel/CSV Import** dialog

- On the main menu, select File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map . 
The **Excel/CSV Import** dialog opens.

##### Description of the Excel/CSV Import dialog areas

The**Excel/CSV Import** dialogconsists of the following areas:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='MD2024x']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The main areas, highlighted in orange, in the Excel/CSV Import dialog: select file, mapping options, and mapping.

###### Select the Excel/CSV file area

The Select Excel/CSV file area allows you to select the Excel or CSV file from your file system or from your model if the Excel or CSV file is attached to a project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files' space='MD2024x']]]>

The Select Excel/CSV file area contains the buttons described in the following table.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (mandatory) | If you select From file system, the Open dialog opens. The selected file path is displayed in the Excel/CSV File field.If you select From model, the Select Excel/CSV file dialog opens. The selected attached file name is displayed in the Excel/CSV File field. |
|  | Refreshes the mapping area after changing the file. |

###### Mapping Options area

The Mapping Options area allows you to specify mapping options. These options define how file columns meet the element properties after data import. The following table lists all available options and provides their descriptions in detail.

| Mapping option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Import Type (mandatory) | Select either elements or instances you want to create in the model after data import. |
| Element Type (mandatory) | Click and select the type of the elements that are created in the model after data import. |
| Instance Classifier (mandatory) | Click and select the element which is set as classifier for all imported i. |
| Stereotype | Click and in the Select Stereotype dialog select one or more stereotypes to apply them for created elements after the import.You can select stereotypes only if the Element Type option value is selected. |
| Properties to Map (mandatory) | Click and in the Properties to Map dialog select one or more properties of the element type. The imported elements contain selected properties and their values are filled with data from the Excel or CSV file.You can specify properties only if the Element Type option value is selected. |
| Nested Properties to Map (mandatory) | Click and in the Nested Properties to Map dialog select one or more nested properties of the . The imported instances contain selected properties and their values are filled with data from the Excel or CSV file.You can specify nested properties only if the Instance Classifier option value is selected. |
| Target Scope | Click and in the Select Owner dialog select or create the element that contains the created elements after the import.To create a new element using the Select Owner dialoga. Click the Creation Mode button.b. Click the Create Owner button.c. Select an appropriate element from the list. |
| Search for Elements In | Select the search scope of imported elements:Target Scope Only (default) - searches for an imported element only under the element selected as the Target Scope option value. If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, this option increases the import speed.Target Scope Recursively - searches for imported elements recursively from the element which is selected as the Target Scope option value. If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, this option might slowdown the import speed. |
| Search for References In | Select search scope of elements that are referenced with imported elements:Target Scope Only (default) - searches for referenced elements only under the element selected as the Target Scope option value. If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, this option increases the import speed.Entire Model - searches for referenced elements recursively from the Model package. If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, this option might slowdown the import speed. |
| Sheet | Select the Excel file sheet you want to import. The first sheet is selected by default.It is available only for Excel files. |
| First cell | Select the Excel file cell you want to start importing from. The first cell filled with data is read by default. If the selected sheet is empty, it reads from the A1 cell.It is available only for Excel files. |
| CSV Delimiter | Select the delimiter of the CSV file from the drop-down list. If you want to specify a custom delimiter in the next box, select Other. By default, the comma (,) is selected.It is available only for CSV files. |
| Identification Property | Select the property that identifies if the imported element already exists in the model. It allows to avoid element duplication.Select the Default value to identify elements by their Name and requirements by their ID. The rest of the values are the same as those selected in the Select Properties to Map option but only .If the imported element Identification Property option value matches with an already-existing element property, the identified element properties are updated. |
| Identify Relationship (end) Source by: | This option is displayed only if any kind of direct relationship is selected as the Element Type option value.Click and select the property from the list that identifies the source of the relationship end.Select the Default value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select <UNSPECIFIED > value to create the same relationship between the same elements.The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.The mechanism identifies relationships according to Identification Property option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the Identify Relationship (end) Source by option value. If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those properties and tags that are common for both of them. |
| Identify Relationship (end) Target by: | This option is displayed only if any kind of direct relationship is selected as the Element Type option value.Click and select the property from the list that identifies the target of the relationship end. Select the Default value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select <UNSPECIFIED > value to create the same relationship between the same elements.The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.The mechanism identifies relationships according to Identification Property option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the Identify Relationship (end) Source by option value. If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those properties and tags that are common for both of them. |
| First Row Contains Headings check box | Clear the check box if your Excel/CSV file does not contain headings. The check box is selected by default. |
| Import Plain Text check box | Select the check box to import/export plain text only (without HTML). The check box is not selected by default. |

###### Mapping area

The Mapping area allows you to manage mapping between element properties and Excel/CSV file columnsby creating new mappings, re-mapping, or deleting them:- To create a new map or re-map individually, drag columns from the left tree/right tree onto columns in the right tree/left tree. 
 - To create a new map or re-mapall columns in the ordinal position, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']at the bottom of the mapping area. 
-To delete mapping individually, select an appropriate map and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']at the bottom of the mapping area. 
 - To delete all mappings, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']at the bottom of the mapping area.

If you do not define your own mapping (that is, the mapping area is empty), the columns by default are mapped on the ordinal position.

The hashtag (#) column from the modeling tool table is not included in the mapping.

1960839142

**Related pages**

- [Importing data from Excel or CSV files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files)

- Working with map groups
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog' space='MD2024x']

The first sheet is selected by default.</span><br /><span>It is available only for Excel files.</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>First cell </strong></td><td><span>Select the Excel file cell you want to start importing from. The first cell filled with data is read by default. If the selected sheet is empty, it reads from the A1 cell.</span><br /><span>It is available only for Excel files.</span></td></tr><tr><td><strong>CSV Delimiter</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>Select the delimiter of the CSV file from the drop-down list. If you want to specify a custom delimiter in the next box, select </span><strong>Other</strong><span>. By default, the comma (,) is selected.</span><br /><span>It is available only for CSV files.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="121"><ri:attachment ri:filename="csv_delimiter.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Identification Property</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Select the property that identifies if the imported element already exists in the model. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">It allows to avoid element duplication.</span></p><p>Select the <span><strong>Default</strong> value to identify elements by their Name and requirements by their ID. The</span> rest of the values<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6bc110f7-7263-4bc5-b5cb-2c8877ac45a1"> are the same</ac:inline-comment-marker> as those selected in the <strong>Select Properties to Map </strong>option but only <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Primitive" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Primitive data types]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0fc117fa-6270-4213-b0a3-6821ec11692f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span><span>If the imported element </span><strong>Identification Property</strong><span> option value matches with an already-existing element property, the identified element properties are updated.</span><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by:</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a7a73fb-6e0b-488f-902a-dbb605261a9d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This option is displayed o<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3dba51e8-a50b-46b4-88bd-7527da156654">nly if a</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">ny kind of direct relationship is selected as the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Element Type</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> option value.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the property from the list that identifies the source of the relationship end.</p><p>Select the <strong>Default</strong> value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select &lt;UNSPECIFIED &gt; value to create the same relationship between the same elements.</p><p>The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:</p><ul><li>If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.</li><li><span>The mechanism identifies relationships according to<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><strong>Identification Property</strong> option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.<br /></span></li><li><span>If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the <strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710427 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00524: Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog

- page_id: `136710427`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710427/Excel+and+CSV+Sync+Options+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:38.413+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Sync with Excel or CSV files
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

155935942

155935944

155935941

**On this page**

43

155935943

The **Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog allows you to specify settings before syncing data between an Excel/CSV file and a table. You can select files for syncing and define syncing and mapping options in this dialog.

##### Opening the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog

To open the**Excel/CSV Sync** **Options** dialog

- In the table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='excel_csv_sync.png'] and do one of the following: - Select the Sync Options command. - Select the Excel/CSV File > Select File . Click the Details button on the left bottom corner of the dialog. The Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog opens.

##### Description of the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog areas

The **Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog consists of the following areas:

44

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The main areas, highlighted in orange, in the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog: select file, sync, mapping options, and mapping.

###### Select Excel/CSV file area

The Select Excel/CSV file area allows you to select the Excel or CSV file from your file system or from your model if the Excel or CSV file is attached to a project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files' space='MD2024x']]]>

You can link only one Excel/CSV file with the same modeling tool table.

The Select Excel/CSV file area contains the buttons described in the following table.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | If you select From file system, the Open dialog opens. The selected file path is displayed in the Excel/CSV File field.If you select From model, the Select Excel/CSV file dialog opens. The selected attached file name is displayed in the Excel/CSV File field. |
|  | Refreshes the mapping area after changing the file. |

###### Sync Options area

The Sync Options area contains one option that allows you to specify the sync mechanism behavior when importing data to a table if rows in your file are deleted. All available values are described below.

| Sync option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| If rows in the file are deleted | Delete elements from the model - deletes the same elements that were deleted in the file from your model.If some elements are read-only in Excel file, they cannot be deleted from the model.Remove elements form the table - removes the same elements that were deleted in the file only from the modeling tool table, not from the model.Mark as obsolete (default) - The table Legend automatically displays and highlights the removed elements (obsolete) in red. |

###### Mapping Options area

The Mapping Options area allows you to specify mapping options. The following table lists all available options.

| Mapping option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Sheet | Select the Excel file sheet you want to sync. The first sheet is selected by default.It is available only for Excel files. |
| First cell | Select the Excel file cell you want to start syncing from. The first cell filled with data is read by default. If the selected sheet is empty, it reads from the A1 cell.It is available only for Excel files. |
| CSV delimiter | Select the delimiter of the CSV file from the drop-down list. If you want to specify the custom delimiter in the next box, select Other. By default, the comma (,) is selected.It is available only for CSV files. |
| Identification Property | Select the property that identifies if the element already exists in the model. It allows to avoid element duplication.The Default value identify elements by their Name and requirements by their ID first. The rest of the values are the same as columns shown in the table of your model but only of .If the imported element Identification Property option value matches with an already-existing element property, the identified element properties are updated. |
| Identify Relationship (end) Source by: | This option is displayed only if any kind of direct relationship is selected as the Element Type option value in the .Click and select the property from the list that identifies the source of the relationship end.Select the Default value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select <UNSPECIFIED > value to create the same relationship between the same elements.The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.The mechanism identifies relationships according to Identification Property option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the Identify Relationship (end) Source by option value. If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those properties and tags that are common for both of them. |
| Identify Relationship (end) Target by: | This option is displayed only if any kind of direct relationship is selected as the Element Type option value in the .Click and select the property from the list that identifies the target of the relationship end.Select the Default value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select <UNSPECIFIED > value to create the same relationship between the same elements.The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.The mechanism identifies relationships according to Identification Property option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the Identify Relationship (end) Source by option value.If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those properties and tags that are common for both of them. |
| First row contains headings check box | Clear the check box if your Excel/CSV file does not contain headings. The check box is selected by default. |
| Sync Plain Text check box | Select the check box to import/export plain text only (without HTML). The check box is not selected by default. |

###### Mapping area

The Mapping area allows you to manage mapping between modeling tool table columns and Excel/CSV table columns by creating new mappings, re-mapping, or deleting them:- To create a new map or re-map one by one, drag columns from the left tree/right tree onto columns in the right tree/left tree. 
 - To create a new map or re-map all columns in the ordinal position, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the mapping area. 
 - To delete mapping one by one, select an appropriate map and click[IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the mapping area. 
 - To delete all mappings, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the mapping area.

If you do not define your own mapping - that is, the mapping area is empty - the columns by default are mapped on the ordinal position.

The hashtag (#) column from the modeling tool table is not included in the mapping.

155947252

The selected file path is displayed in the <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> field.<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">I<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">f you select <strong>From model</strong></span></span></span>, the <strong>Select Excel/CSV file</strong> dialog opens</span></span></span>. The selected attached file name is displayed in the <strong>Excel/CSV File</strong> field.</span></span></span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Refresh Mapping" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Refresh Mapping" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table Criteria area" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Refreshes the mapping area after changing the file.</span></span></span></td></tr></tbody></table><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></h4><h4><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Sync Options area</span><br class="auto-cursor-target" /></span></span></span></h4><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Sync Options area contains one option that allows you to <span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">specify <span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">the sync mechanism behavior</span></span></span> when importing data to a table if rows in your file are deleted. All available values are described below. <br /></span></span></span></span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Sync option</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>If rows in the file are deleted</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Delete elements from the model </strong>- deletes the same elements that were deleted in the file from your model.<br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></span></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dba0abc1-a161-44f6-9860-442f95a3acd4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If some elements are read-only in Excel file, they cannot be deleted from the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Remove elements form the table </strong>- removes the same elements that were deleted in the file <strong>only</strong> from the modeling tool table,<strong> not</strong> from the model.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Mark as obsolete</strong><span> (default) - <span>The table</span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Legends"> Legend</a><span> <span><span>automatically</span></span> displays and highlights the removed elements (obsolete) in red.</span></span></li></ul></div></td></tr></tbody></table><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></h4><h4><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Mapping Options area</span></span></span><br /></span></span></span></span></h4><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Mapping Options area <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">allows you to</span></span></span></span></span></span> specify mapping options. The following table lists all available options.</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 15.454%;" /><col style="width: 84.546%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Mapping option</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Sheet </strong></td><td>Select the Excel file sheet you want to sync. The first sheet is selected by default.<br />It is available only for Excel files.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>First cell </strong></td><td>Select the Excel file cell you want to start syncing from. The first cell filled with data is read by default. If the selected sheet is empty, it reads from the A1 cell.<br />It is available only for Excel files.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>CSV delimiter</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Select the delimiter of the CSV file from the drop-down list. If you want to specify the custom delimiter in the next box, select <strong>Other</strong>. By default, the comma (,) is selected.<br />It is available only for CSV files.<br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="121"><ri:attachment ri:filename="csv_delimiter.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Identification Property</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the property that identifies if the element already exists in the model. It allows to avoid element duplication.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>The </span><span><strong>Default</strong> value identify elements by their Name and requirements by their <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2186fac8-f852-4b88-bbfd-f8bd51c81c9c">ID first</ac:inline-comment-marker>. The</span><span> rest of the values </span>are the same<span> as columns shown in the table</span><span> of your model but only of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Primitive" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Primitive data types]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f620f492-819c-4f3e-867a-e5fcf8d1b4f4"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If the imported element </span><strong>Identification Property</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> option value matches with an already-existing element property, the identified element properties are updated.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by:</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a7a73fb-6e0b-488f-902a-dbb605261a9d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span><span>This option is displayed o</span>nly if a<span>ny kind of direct relationship is selected as the </span><strong>Element Type</strong><span> option value</span> </span><span>in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table Criteria area" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Criteria area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the property from the list that identifies the source of the relationship end.</p><p>Select the <strong>Default</strong> value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select &lt;UNSPECIFIED &gt; value to create the same relationship between the same elements.</p><p>The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:</p><ul><li>If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.</li><li><span>The mechanism identifies relationships according to<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><strong>Identification Property</strong> option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.<br /></span></li><li><span>If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the <strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by </strong>option value. </span></li><li>If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">properties </span>and tags that are common for both of them.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Identify Relationship (end) Target by:</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e82097bf-6da5-413a-bdfe-00a194dd62d6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>This option is displayed only if any kind of direct relationship is selected as the <strong>Element Type</strong> option value </span><span>in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Table Criteria area" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Criteria area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the property from the list that identifies the target of the relationship end.</p><p>Select the <strong>Default</strong> value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select &lt;UNSPECIFIED &gt; value to create the same relationship between the same elements.</p><p>The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:</p><ul><li>If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.</li><li>The mechanism identifies relationships according to<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><strong>Identification Property</strong> option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.</li><li>If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the <strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by </strong>option value.</li><li>If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">properties </span>and tags that are common for both of them.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><strong>First row contains headings </strong>check box</td><td>Clear the check box <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">if your Excel/CSV file does not contain headings.</span> The check box is selected by default.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Sync Plain Text</strong> check box</td><td colspan="1">Select the check box to import/export plain text only (without HTML). The check box is not selected by default.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>Mapping area</h4><p>The Mapping area allows you to manage mapping between <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">modeling tool table columns and Excel/CSV table columns</span></span></span> by creating new mappings, re-mapping, or deleting them:<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br class="auto-cursor-target" />          -  To create a new map or re-map <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">one by one, drag</span> columns from the left tree/<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">right tree</span></span></span> onto columns in the right tree/<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> left tree</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">.<br />          -  <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create a new map or re-map</span></span></span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">all columns in the ordinal position</span></span></span>, click <ac:image ac:title="Map 1:1" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="map_1_1_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">at the bottom of the mapping area</span></span></span></span></span>.<br />          -  <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">delete mapping</span> one by one</span></span></span></span></span>, select an appropriate map and c</span></span></span>lick<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <ac:image ac:title="Delete " ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Delete " ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> at the bottom of the mapping area<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">.<br />          -  <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To delete all mappings</span></span></span></span></span></span></span>, click <ac:image ac:title="Delete All" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Delete All" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_all_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">at the bottom of the mapping area</span></span></span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">. <br /></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></p><p>If you do not define your own mapping - that is, the mapping area is empty - the columns by default are mapped on the ordinal position.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ded18c57-dbe0-4132-898a-917401c53c18"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The hashtag (#) column from the modeling tool table is not included in the mapping.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fb3948b1-3153-4fe5-aae3-1113b27e23d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">155947252</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><p ## PAGE 00525: Exception

- page_id: `136714383`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136714383/Exception
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:16:28.893+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Debug report template
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The warning message, see the figure below, will open whenever an exception, such as IndexOutOfBoundException, occurs during the report generation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exception Warning Message.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711024 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00526: Exception Handler

- page_id: `136711024`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711024/Exception+Handler
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:13.633+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

943151593

943151596

943151594

An Exception Handler element specifies a body to execute if the specified exception occurs during the execution of the protected node.

The Exception Handler may be drawn from an Output Pin to an Input Pin or from an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='MD2024x'] to an Input Pin, as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Representation of an Exception Handler.

943151592

**Related pages**

- Formatting symbols
- Specification window
- Model Elements
- Editing property values
- Class diagram
- Action

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136712051 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00527: Executable concepts

- page_id: `136712051`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136712051/Executable+concepts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:14:06.998+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

We call executable such concepts that can be specified by some expressions and calculated. In modeling tools developed by No Magic, executable are:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MD2024x'] - that are predefined or custom constraints having a purpose to validate a model or particular model elements.
- Derived properties - properties that are automatically calculated from the other properties.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart Package' space='MD2024x'] - that are special collections of model elements.
- Executable opaque behaviors - reusable expression fragments callable form other executable concepts.
- Metric definitions - calculate aggregate parameters of models.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Macro Engine Documentation' space='MD2024x'] - a script that is created using a particular script language. With the help of macros, you can control items that are allowed in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Developer Guide' space='MD2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space='MD2024x'] - a behavior with implementation-specific semantics.

In a modeling tool, executable concepts are defined in one of the languages that our modeling tool can evaluate:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Getting started with specifying criteria' space='MD2024x'] - a graphical way to construct expressions out of predefined blocks.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Object Constraint Language' space='MD2024x']ObjectConstraint Language .
- Binary - an expression that is written in Java, compiled and added to our modeling tool with a plugin.
- One of the following scripting languages:

- 
  - Groovy
  - JavaScript
  - JavaScript Rhino
  - Jython

#### INFO: Additional scripting languages

Additional scripting languages

Additional scripting languages can be plugged into our modeling tool, if they have JSR-223-compliant implementation.

You can test and evaluate your expressions with the help of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Expression Evaluation' space='MD2024x'].

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p>We call executable such concepts that can be specified by some expressions and calculated. In modeling tools developed by No Magic, executable are:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Validation rules]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> - that are predefined or custom constraints having a purpose to validate a model or particular model elements.</li><li>Derived properties - properties that are automatically calculated from the other properties.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Smart packages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> - that are special collections of model elements.</li><li>Executable opaque behaviors - reusable expression fragments callable form other executable concepts.</li><li>Metric definitions - calculate aggregate parameters of models.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Macro Engine Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Macros]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> - a script that is created using a particular script language. With the help of macros, you can control items that are allowed in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Developer Guide" /></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Opaque Behavior" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Opaque behaviors]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> - a behavior with implementation-specific semantics. </li></ul><p>In a modeling tool, executable concepts are defined in one of the languages that our modeling tool can evaluate:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Getting started with specifying criteria" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StructuredExpression]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> - a graphical way to construct expressions out of predefined blocks. </li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Object Constraint Language" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Object </span><span class="confluence-link">Constraint Language</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Binary - an expression that is written in Java, compiled and added to our modeling tool with a plugin.</li><li>One of the following scripting languages:</li></ul><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Groovy</li><li>JavaScript</li><li>JavaScript Rhino</li><li>Jython</li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="229657b4-a0f4-4a51-b96a-604b480a4ca3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Additional scripting languages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Additional scripting languages can be plugged into our modeling tool, if they have JSR-223-compliant implementation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>You can test and evaluate your expressions with the help of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Expression Evaluation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Expression Evaluation tool]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e911d4d6-d4ab-4178-b243-3086a134c0f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="c26fc222-1788-441e-9539-ff5fff4d89eb" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711027 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00528: Execution Environment

- page_id: `136711027`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711027/Execution+Environment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:14.320+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Execution Environment is a type of node that defines a set of services that support software. For example, an execution environment in the processor on a server might provide the operating system-level services needed to support a database application installed in that environment. Some typical stereotypes for an execution environment include *<OS>*, *<workflow engine>*, *<database system>*, and *<J2EE container>*. 
 
Specify the Execution Environment element in the Execution Environment [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].

The Execution Environment dialog box contains the following specific panes:

- Nested Nodes : lists nodes defined (nested) in the Execution Environment .
- Deployed Artifacts : an artifact or artifact instance that has been deployed to a deployment target.

You can format the Execution Environment symbol properties in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024x'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">An Execution Environment is a type of node that defines a set of services that support software. For example, an execution environment in the processor on a server might provide the operating system-level services needed to support a database application installed in that environment. Some typical stereotypes for an execution environment include <em>&lt;OS&gt;</em>, <em>&lt;workflow engine&gt;</em>, <em>&lt;database system&gt;</em>, and <em>&lt;J2EE container&gt;</em>. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">  </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Specify the Execution Environment element in the Execution Environment <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">.   </span></p><p><span class="hcp1">The</span> <span class="hcp2">Execution</span> <span class="hcp2">Environment</span> <span class="hcp1">dialog box contains the following specific panes:</span></p><ul><li class="kadov-p"><span class="hcp3"><span>Nested</span></span> <span class="hcp3"><span>Nodes</span></span><span class="hcp1">: lists</span><span class="hcp1"> nodes</span> <span class="hcp1">defined (nested) in the</span> Execution Environment<span class="hcp1">.</span></li><li class="kadov-p"><span class="hcp3"><span>Deployed</span></span> <span class="hcp3"><span>Artifacts</span></span><span class="hcp1">: </span>an artifact or artifact instance that has been deployed to a deployment target.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">You can format the Execution Environment symbol properties in the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">. </span></p>
````
