
|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateType | java.lang.String | A template type such rtf, html, and htm. |
| attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |  |
| outputname | java.lang.String | An output filename without a filename extension. |  |
| importObject | java.lang.Object | An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(templateFilename)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be given the template filename. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(attachedFile)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be given the template filename. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(templateFilename, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be named after the template filename. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return |  | java.lang.String |  |

##### **$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be named after the template filename. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return |  | java.lang.String |  |

##### **$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. "importer" will be used as the name of a context value. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return |  | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. "importer" will be used as the name of a context value. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return |  | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, ContextName, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| contextName | java.lang.String | Add an object context's name. |  |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, ContextName, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| contextName | java.lang.String | Add an object context's name. |  |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, context)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| context | java.util.Map<String, Object> | An additional context added to a new template. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, context)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| context | java.util.Map<String, Object> | An additional context added to a new template. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.copy(inputFilename)**

Copy an input file to an output file using the same name in the binary format. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | inputFilename | java.lang.String | An input filename |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error. |

****

##### **$file.copy(inputFilename, outputFilename)**

Copy an input file to an output file in the binary format. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | inputFilename | java.lang.String | An input filename. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output filename. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error. |

****

##### **$file.exists(pathname)**

Test if a file denoted by a specific pathname exists. By default, the current directory refers to a template location. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | pathname | java.lang.String | A pathname string. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | True if and only if a file or directory denoted by a specific pathname exists; otherwise, false. |

****

##### **$file.computeName(directory, name)**

Create a pathname string from a given directory and name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | directory | java.lang.String | A parent directory of a file. |
| name | java.lang.String | A filename (excludes the filename extension). |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A pathname from the given directory and name. |

For example:

```text

```

##### ****

##### **$file.computeName(directory, name, fileType)**

Create a pathname string from a given directory, name, and file type.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | directory | java.lang.String | A parent directory of a file. |
|  | name | java.lang.String | A filename (excluding the extension). |
|  | fileType | java.lang.String | A file type, such as rtf, txt, and html. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A pathname from the given directory, name, and file type. |

For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1bb8a7a4-4050-4de9-94ad-78b62e4c54ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.create('overview.html','overview.html','')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col style="width: 111.0px;" /><col style="width: 132.0px;" /><col style="width: 125.0px;" /><col style="width: 666.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong><br /><br /></td><td>templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>outputFileName </td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output filename. </td></tr><tr><td> importObject</td><td> java.lang.Object</td><td>An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td> java.lang.String</td><td>An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.create(attachedFile, outputFileName, importObject)</strong></h3><p>Generate a report output from a given template name. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0972e03f-63c4-4b89-9eae-d4aedddbf427"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.create($attachedFile,'overview.html','')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong><br /><br /></td><td><p>attachedFile</p></td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>outputFileName </td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output filename. </td></tr><tr><td>importObject</td><td>java.lang.Object</td><td>An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.create(templateType, templateFilename, outputname, importObject)</strong></h3><p>Generate a report output from a given template name. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9ac4eda-1a9a-459f-9043-677cdd80e7ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.create('html','overview','overview','')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong><br /><br /><br /></td><td> templateType</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A template type such rtf, html, and htm. </td></tr><tr><td> templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td> outputname</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output filename without a filename extension.</td></tr><tr><td> importObject</td><td> java.lang.Object</td><td>An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.create(templateType, attachedFile, outputname, importObject)</strong></h3><p>Generate a report output from a given template name. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d16afed-26cc-466a-93d3-ddc2b00e1b11"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.create('html',$attachedFile,'overview','')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong><br /><br /><br /></td><td>templateType</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A template type such rtf, html, and htm. </td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;"><p>attachedFile</p></td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>outputname</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output filename without a filename extension.</td></tr><tr><td>importObject</td><td>java.lang.Object</td><td>An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(templateFilename)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be given the template filename. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b399fb7-8d35-4ec3-8603-3dddfbba447d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait('overview.html')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td> templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path <br />to generate a file.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(attachedFile)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be given the template filename. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="57f32d7f-5374-4aad-95fb-0bae58b41316"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait($attachedFile)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td><p>attachedFile</p></td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path to generate a file.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(templateFilename, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be named after the template filename. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1bb8a654-eab2-47f6-ad09-f7696141b25e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait('overview.html',$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td> templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td> contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td><br /></td><td>java.lang.String</td><td><br /></td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be named after the template filename. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4cc32ce4-9e74-4469-afb8-d6949a127b29"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait($attachedFile,$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td style="text-align: left;">attachedFile</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td><br /></td><td>java.lang.String</td><td><br /></td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. &quot;importer&quot; will be used as the name of a context value. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c477362-e675-4790-ac30-9226ac65ca7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait('overview.html','overview.html',$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s)   </strong></td><td>templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>outputFilename </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output report filename.</td></tr><tr><td>contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return </strong></td><td><br /></td><td><span>java.lang.String</span> </td><td>An absolute path to generate a file.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. &quot;importer&quot; will be used as the name of a context value. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="156632f6-c44c-41b0-ac55-86b60402559c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait($attachedFile,'overview.html',$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s)   </strong></td><td><p>attachedFile</p></td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>outputFilename </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output report filename.</td></tr><tr><td>contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return </strong></td><td><br /></td><td><span>java.lang.String</span> </td><td> An absolute path to generate a file.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, ContextName, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7eedfdf7-a0a6-44fd-a99e-252291e3e3e5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait('overview.html','overview.html','var',$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><strong>Parameter(s)    </strong></td><td> templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td> outputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output report filename.</td></tr><tr><td> contextName</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>Add an object context's name.</td></tr><tr><td> contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path to generate a file. </td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, ContextName, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89bf2ffa-199a-4dba-816c-644b91b0e25d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait($attachedFile,'overview.html','var',$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><strong>Parameter(s)    </strong></td><td style="text-align: left;">attachedFile</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>outputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output report filename.</td></tr><tr><td>contextName</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>Add an object context's name.</td></tr><tr><td>contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path to generate a file. </td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, context)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ee3f106-bc69-4f6b-a535-0ee7556a25e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait('overview.html','overview.html',$map)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong> Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td>templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td> outputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output report <br />filename.</td></tr><tr><td> context</td><td>java.util.Map&lt;String, Object&gt; </td><td>An additional context <br />added to a new template. </td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path to <br />generate a file. </td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, context)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29501624-dafa-48c3-8c3e-775bc804ebef"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait($attachedFile,'overview.html',$map)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong> Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td style="text-align: left;">attachedFile</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>outputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output report filename.</td></tr><tr><td>context</td><td>java.util.Map&lt;String, Object&gt; </td><td>An additional context added to a new template. </td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path to generate a file. </td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.copy(inputFilename)</strong></h3><p>Copy an input file to an output file using the same name in the binary format. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="625ab104-2416-42d6-9de4-a9ba3ed8e694"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.copy('icon.gif')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td> inputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An input filename </td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output pathname. <br />It will return an empty <br />string if there is an error. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$file.copy(inputFilename, outputFilename)</strong></h3><p>Copy an input file to an output file in the binary format. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24c27b18-a4f5-4e27-b697-be8abcbb628f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ $file.copy('icon.gif','icon.gif')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td> inputFilename </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An input <br />filename. </td></tr><tr><td> outputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output <br />filename. </td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output pathname. <br />It will return an empty <br />string if there is an error.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$file.exists(pathname)</strong></h3><p>Test if a file denoted by a specific pathname exists. By default, the current directory refers to a template location. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="314ef9bc-4803-426d-abd4-b76ea31633b5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.exists("$template.resourcesLocation/myimage.png")
````html
$file.exists("C:/myfolder/myimage.png")
$file.exists("mytemplate.txt")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>pathname </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A pathname string. </td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>True if and only if a file <br />or directory denoted by <br />a specific pathname exists; <br />otherwise, false. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$file.computeName(directory, name)</strong></h3><p>Create a pathname string from a given directory and name.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"> Parameter(s) </td><td>directory </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A parent directory <br />of a file. </td></tr><tr><td> name</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A filename (excludes <br />the filename extension).</td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A pathname from the <br />given directory and name.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f0d0e6c-2f43-4bb7-8592-7b1e84f8e817"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.computeName('actors', $ac.ID, 'html')
output: 'actor/_123456789.html']]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><strong><br /></strong></h3><h3><strong>$file.computeName(directory, name, fileType)</strong></h3><p>Create a pathname string from a given directory, name, and file type.</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>directory </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A parent directory <br />of a file. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>name</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A filename (excluding <br />the extension).</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>fileType  </td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A file type, such as rtf, <br />txt, and html. </td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A pathname from the <br />given directory, name, <br />and file type. </td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136715385 space=MD2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00566: Files of Properties

- page_id: `136715385`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136715385/Files+of+Properties
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:58:38.120+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering
- labels: ['supported-languages']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

419473883

419473885

419473884

Code can be generated out of prepared UML models. Mapping should be implemented between the identifiers used in the UML model and the language to which the model is being generated. This mapping includes the following sections:

- Build-in types (their default values)
- Generalization types
- Possible class declarations. Attributes and operations declaration and visibility modifiers
- Code generation options.

A separate *prop* file is created for every language supported in modeling tools developed by us. Files are located in the *<modeling tool installation directory>\data* folder. The file name pattern is *lang.prop*, where lang stands for the name of the programming language.

The following table lists supported languages:

| Supported Language | File of Properties |
| --- | --- |
| Java | java.prop |
| C++ | C++.prop |
| CORBA IDL | idl.prop |
| JAVABytecode | javabytecode.prop |
| DDL | ddl.prop |
| IDL | idl.prop |
| XML Schema | xmlschema.prop |

Files of language properties are separated into sections where all related elements are grouped. You can edit existing entities, add new ones, and change the default values.

We strongly recommend that you edit default values only. In general, all sections have a list of possible and default values for the element.

419473882

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a63aa9c7-4ea0-4595-be8f-2ebf16674417"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">419473883</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b4a93322-8b79-46e7-9eb3-c1e4cb744525"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">419473885</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f0f8dd6-ce00-43cc-8c03-cfbfd980406c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">419473884</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Code can be generated out of prepared UML models. In general, all sections have a list of possible and default values for the element.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5dc52af-3781-4753-b67a-0ec8eea472bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">419473882</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong>:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="bbf86920-508f-4d7b-bf58-a8cba5cf6383"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Code Engineering" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710324 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00567: Filling the table with elements

- page_id: `136710324`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710324/Filling+the+table+with+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:35.329+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Generic table
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can fill the Generic Table for the specified Element Type in the following ways:

43

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing data from Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024x']

##### Creating new elements

At least one element type must be selected in the **Element Type** box before adding a new element.

To create a new element

1. Click the **Add New** button on the Edit toolbar. If there is more than one element type selected, a submenu with the list of available element types opens. Only element types available to create in a possible diagram owner which contains the Generic Table will be displayed on the submenu.
2. Select an element type. The element of the selected type will be added to the last row of the table and to the model.
3. Name the new element in the table.

##### Adding existing elements from the model

Elements you want to add should correspond to selected element types.

To add an existing element from the model

1. Click the Add Existing button on the Edit toolbar. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024x']**Select Element** dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='gt_select_elements.png']
2. Select the element you want to add to the Generic Table. This element and its name will be added to the table as the last row. Use the **Multiple Selection**mode to add more than one element at a time.

If you have not defined the element type before starting to add elements to the table, it is automatically set after the first element is added to the table. The type of this element becomes the value of the **Element Type** box.

To add an existing element, you can drag a selected element to the contents of the Generic Table directly from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024x']. 
If you drag a container, for example, a package or a smart package, the Generic Table will display its contents, but not the container itself.

##### Copying and pasting elements from another resource

You can copy the data from other sources such as HTML, Excel, Word, and paste it to the Generic Table. The main rules for successful copy-and-paste operations in Generic Tables are described on the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Copying and pasting data' space='MD2024x'] page. The copy-and-paste operations create new elements or update already existing elements in the Generic Table.

To create elements in the table by pasting data from another resource

1. Copy a table from another resource. The number of columns in that table should be the same as the number of columns in the Generic Table.The data types of columns in the Generic Table must be compatible with copied information.
2. Open the Generic Table.
3. In the Criteria area, click [ATTACHMENT filename='three-dot-btn1.png'] to specify the Element Type.
4. In the table toolbar, click the **Show Columns** button and choose the columns that you want to fill with the copied information. The cells must be editable in the Generic Table.
5. Do one of the following: 
 - Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). 
 - In the main toolbar, select **Edit** > **Paste.** 
Each row displays one element with its properties. If the element already exists in the model, the information updates.If elements have ID prefixes in another resource, they are automatically set only for pasted elements in the Generic Table after the copy-and-paste command. Manage ID prefixes in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Numbering dialog' space='MD2024x']**Element Numbering** dialog. The following example demonstrates the main steps for pasting data into a Generic Table. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136710372 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00568: Filling the table with instances

- page_id: `136710372`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136710372/Filling+the+table+with+instances
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:12:36.813+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Instance table
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following outlines how to fill the Instance Table for the specified classifier:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filling the table with instances' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filling the table with instances' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filling the table with instances' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filling the table with instances' space='MD2024x'] [ATTACHMENT filename='instance_table_example_two_classifiers.png']

create_new_instanceTo create a new instance

1. On the table toolbar, click the **Create** button. The new instance can be created only if the classifier is already specified for the Instance Table. Otherwise, the **Create** button is not available. If there is more than one classifier specified, from the menu of the button, select a classifier for which you need to create an instance. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The new instance is added to the Instance Table as the last row.
2. Type the instance name and specify slot values.

create_new_with_partsTo create a new instance with parts

1. On the table toolbar, click **Create**>**Create with Parts**. The new instance with nested parts is created if:the classifier is specified for the Instance Table. Otherwise, the **Create with Parts** button is not available.the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filling the table with instances' space='MD2024x'] in the Instance Table.

add_existing_instancesTo add an existing instance

1. On the table toolbar, click Create > Add Existing . The Select Instance Specification dialog opens.
2. In the tree or list on the left side of the dialog, select an element and add it to the Selected elements list. You can select more than one element. Elements available for selection:
  - Instances of one or more classifiers specified for the table. An instance is available if it belongs to at least one of the classifiers.
  - Packages that contain these instances. In this case, the contents of a selected package, but not the package itself, will be added to the table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. 
The selected instance is added to the table as the last row. You can drag a selected element to the contents of the Instance Table directly from the Model Browser.To select adjacentelements, select a single element and then hold down the Shift key while you click other elements.To select nonadjacentelements, select a single element and then hold down the Ctrl key while you click other elements.

copy_instancesTo copy and paste instances from another resource

1. Copy the content of the table from another resource. The number of columns in that table should be the same as the number of columns in the Instance Table.The data types of columns in the Instance Table must be compatible with copied information.
2. Open the Instance Table where you want to paste the copied data.
3. Make sure the Classifier is specified in the Criteria area.
4. In the table toolbar, click the **Columns** button and choose columns of instance slot values that you want to fill with copied information. The cells must be editable in the Instance Table.
5. Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). 
Each row displays one instance with its slot values. If an instance already exists in the model, the information updates.If instances have ID prefixes in another resource, they are automatically set after the copy-and-paste command only for pasted instances in the Instance Table. Manage ID prefixes in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element Numbering dialog' space='MD2024x']**Element Numbering** dialog. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following outlines how to fill the Instance Table for the specified classifier:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="create_new_instance"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create new instances.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filling the table with instances" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="create_new_with_parts"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create new instances with parts.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filling the table with instances" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="add_existing_instances"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add already existing instances.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filling the table with instances" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="copy_instances"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Copy and paste instances from another resources (HTML, Excel, Word, etc.).]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filling the table with instances" /></ac:link><br /><ac:image ac:title="Instance Table filled with instances of two different classifiers" ac:alt="Instance Table filled with instances of two different classifiers"><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_table_example_two_classifiers.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filling the table with instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="444e4d94-a797-41cf-8913-1ad0808846ef"><ac:parameter ac:name="">create_new_instance</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a new instance</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the table toolbar, click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2571bcca-245f-4d62-801e-feb092543c8a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The new instance can be created only if the classifier is already specified for the Instance Table. Otherwise, the <strong>Create</strong> button is not available.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6a5530f9-cae4-4722-bc5d-d7de2ba890a1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If there is more than one classifier specified, from the menu of the button, select a classifier for which you need to create an instance.<br /><ac:image ac:title="More than the one classifier specified in the Instace Table" ac:alt="More than the one classifier specified in the Instace Table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="more_than_one_classifier_instance_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filling the table with instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The new instance is added to the Instance Table as the last row.</p></li><li>Type the instance name and specify slot values.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f02432b1-d245-4583-9bcb-3489f2b7d820"><ac:parameter ac:name="">create_new_with_parts</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a new instance with parts</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the table toolbar, click <strong>Create </strong>&gt;<strong> Create with Parts</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="98f9cc96-ec0a-4745-a146-64f37d0d0f70"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The new instance with nested parts is created if:</p><ul><li>the classifier is specified for the Instance Table. Otherwise, the <strong>Create with Parts</strong> button is not available.</li><li>the <ac:link ac:anchor="nested_properties"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[nested properties are displayed as columns]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filling the table with instances" /></ac:link> in the Instance Table.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d77dd28c-caf8-47cd-82f0-0777dd16ca43"><ac:parameter ac:name="">add_existing_instances</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing instance</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click <strong>Create</strong> &gt;<strong> Add Existing</strong>. The <strong>Select Instance Specification</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the tree or list on the left side of the dialog, select an element and add it to the Selected elements list. You can select more than one element. Elements available for selection:<br /><ul><li>Instances of one or more classifiers specified for the table. An instance is available if it belongs to at least one of the classifiers.</li><li>Packages that contain these instances. In this case, the contents of a selected package, but not the package itself, will be added to the table.</li></ul></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The selected instance is added to the table as the last row.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c292bb53-7ee9-4c75-bf7f-bfee61a16bf7"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can drag a selected element to the contents of the Instance Table directly from the Model Browser.</li><li>To select <span>adjacent </span>elements, select a single element and then hold down the Shift key while you click other elements.</li><li>To select <span>nonadjacent </span>elements, select a single element and then hold down the Ctrl key while you click other elements.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1629c608-6598-43f1-949a-ac594a25d3fb"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If an instance already exists in the model, the information updates.</li><li>If instances have ID prefixes in another resource, they are automatically set after the copy-and-paste command only for pasted instances in the Instance Table. Manage ID prefixes in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Element Numbering dialog" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:title="Copy and paste instances in the Instance Table from another resource" ac:alt="Copy and paste instances in the Instance Table from another resource"><ri:attachment ri:filename="paste_table_in_the_instance_table.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filling the table with instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136711769 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00569: Filter By Package Imports filter

- page_id: `136711769`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136711769/Filter+By+Package+Imports+filter
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:13:54.447+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Selecting elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1038774317

1038774329

1038774315

**On this page**

****

1038774319

##### Restricting type selection list to imported elements

To restrict the type selection list to imported elements

1. Create a Package diagram.
2. Drag the package with typed elements, as well as packages with types, on the diagram pane.
3. Create a package import between them by clicking Package Import in the diagram palette. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_package_import_button.png']

To apply the filter

1. Click a typed element to open the **Select Type** dialog.
2. Apply the **Filter By Package Imports filter**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As a result, the type selection list is restricted to imported elements only.

There are more [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MD2024x'] you can use for the same purpose.

##### Different abstraction levels

You can also use a Model instead of a package, in which case, the elements cannot be accessed from outside, and you can only select the types from packages that are owned within the Model element.

To only show elements from the desired abstraction level

1. Right-click the package to which you want to restrict your type selection, and select Refactor > Convert To > Model .
2. Select a typed element nested in that model, and specify its type. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_port_type.png']
3. Apply Filter By Package Imports to only list the elements that belong to the same abstraction level.

##### Validation

Use active validation to evaluate which element types are out of the scope specified by package imports.

1. Go to Analyze > Validation > Validate.
2. In the Validation dialog , select UML correctness constraints from the Validation Suite drop-down list.
3. Click Validate .
4. In the Validation Results panel, right-click the element you want to change or unset type for, and select Change Type , Unset Type , or Create Package Import Relationship accordingly. [ATTACHMENT filename='elements_out_of_scope.png']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136709573 space=MD2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00570: Filtering

- page_id: `136709573`
- space_key: `MD2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024x/pages/136709573/Filtering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-20T18:11:57.190+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

632682683

632682687

632682686

INLINE

The modeling tool filtering mechanism allows filtering types of model elements in particular lists. For example, you can filter the types of model elements that are displayed in the Model Browser, or you can select them when searching.

To filter the types of the model elements that are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MD2024x']**Containment** tab

1. In the Model Browser, open the Containment tab (in case it was closed or another tab was open).
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button_in_model_browser.png'] and select Filter . [ATTACHMENT filename='containment_tab_filter.png'] The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting element types' space='MD2024x'] dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_element_type_dialog.png']
3. Do one of the following:
  - Click to clear the check box next to the model element type, which you do not want to be displayed in the Containment tab.
  - Click to select the check box next to the model element type, which you want to be displayed in the **Containment** tab. An element cannot be filtered if it has a stereotype applied to it, too. You can only filter elements that have a custom stereotype applied, under the condition that the stereotype has the **Hide Metatype** property set to *true*in its customization.
4. Click OK . Items in the Containment tab are now filtered.

Containment

Model

Containment

To filter the types of model elements when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Searching' space='MD2024x']

1. From the Edit main menu, select Find . The Find dialog opens.
2. Next to the Type box, click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting element types' space='MD2024x'] dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_element_tyle_dialog_actor.png']
3. Click to select the check box of the model element type that you want to search for.
4. Click OK . You are now back to the Find dialog then click Find . The Search Results tab opens in the Model Browser.

632682682

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting element types' space='MD2024x']

Items in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab are now filtered.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63141028-7df0-4345-b256-5b4121ee2267"><ac:rich-text-body>All elements in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab are displayed in hierarchical order. If you clear the <em>Model</em> check box, no elements will be displayed in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab. The same is valid for all element types – if you clear the check box next to the type of element that has inner elements in the Model Browser, you won't be able to see these inner elements.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To filter the types of model elements when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Searching" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[searching]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Edit</strong> main menu, select <strong>Find</strong>. The <strong>Find</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Next to the <strong>Type</strong> box, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filtering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Selecting element types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Select Element Type]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_element_tyle_dialog_actor.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Filtering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click to select the check box of the model element type that you want to search for.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. You are now back to the <strong>Find</strong> dialog then click <strong>Find</strong>. The <strong>Search </strong><strong>Results</strong> tab opens in the Model Browser.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="087068b7-4811-4101-9f5a-6bf4b2630565"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">632682682</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Selecting element types" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
